# 3.5 Frame structures configuration

## 3.5.0-3 Frame structures configuration Document Owner Deputy Arkadiusz Cwik (ID: 11114651)

## 3.5.1 Slot pattern definition

### 3.5.1.0-2 Slot pattern definition Determination of rrmSlotPatternLength (ID: 11114662)

**3.5.1.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11114668`)

Slot pattern definition 5G-L2-PS shall determine the slot format according to a slot format pattern (see 5G_UP_ALG_2313_replaced([11114697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnGefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for slot format definition).

The rrmSlotPatternLength, in number of slots based on numerology of data channels assuming it is the same for UL and DL, shall be determined from the highest period between ssBurstSetPeriod and rrmPrachAssociationPeriod such that it is a multiple of radioframe length (ie 20 slots with scs=30kHz and 80 slots with scs=120kHz).

rrmSlotPatternLength = 2^µ * max(max(ssBurstSetPeriod ;rrmPrachAssociationPeriod ), 10)

where :

  * µ is the numerology of data channels. The value can be derived from NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELLGRP/scs.

  * ssBurstSetPeriod : NIDD parameter MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELLGRP/ssBurstSetPeriod.




rrmPrachAssociationPeriod is derived as per 5G_UP_ALG_1962_replaced([11111925](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRIPvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

The slot format pattern shall apply from SFN 0 subframe 0 to SFN N-1 subframe 9 and repeated every SFN % N = 0 where N = max(max(ssBurstSetPeriod ;rrmPrachAssociationPeriod ), 10)/10. Wrap up of slot format pattern on SFN 0 is guaranteed as N divides 2^10 given 10 bits of SFN. Possible values of ssBurstSetPeriod and rrmPrachAssociationPeriod as a subset of {5ms, 10ms, 20ms, 40ms, 80ms, 160ms}.

If csirsTrackingPeriod > rrmSlotPatternLength as defined above, then rrmSlotPatternLength shall be extended to csirsTrackingPeriod such that the slot pattern built from ssBurstSetPeriod and rrmPrachAssociationPeriod is repeated multiple times. Note that csirsTrackingPeriod always divides 160ms which is the maximum value for ssBurstSetPeriod and rrmPrachAssociationPeriod.

rrmPrachAssociationPeriod is aligned on 3gpp v15.2.0 and range is only up to 160ms.

PRACH configurations that do not allow to associate all SSBs to PRACH occasion shall be rejected.

Note that since Pattern1Length + Pattern2Length always divides 20ms and PatternLength larger than 5ms is not supported, rrmSlotPatternLength shall always contain a whole number of concatenated Pattern1 and Pattern2.

On an FDD carrier shared with LTE, rrmSlotPatternLength shall be a multiple of 80 slots because the length of the DL sharing pattern is 80 slots (see 5G_UP_ALG_2304_replaced([11171916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuIaFvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). If rrmSlotPatternLength as determined above with ssBurstSetPeriod, rrmPrachAssociationPeriod and csirsTrackingPeriod is not a multiple of 80 slots then 5G-L2-PS shall double the value repeatedly until it is.

csirsTrackingPeriod: NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/csirsForTracking.csirsTrackingPeriod

[CNI-88099-M] [End CNI-88099-M]

---

### 3.5.1.0-3 Slot pattern definition TDD slot pattern definition (ID: 11114672)

**3.5.1.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11114675`)

Slot pattern definition For TDD, the algorithm shall determine the slot format of each slot in the pattern step by step as follows:

1\. Slot formats providing symbol directions shall be determined from tddFrameStructure as per 5G_UP_ALG_2313_replaced([11114697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnGefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

2\. Prepare default TDRA list as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2389_replaced([11115349](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kptNHfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If actCsirsMultiplexing is false and actCellCsiRsImTddNonBf is false: Prepare common TDRA list as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2391_replaced([11115362](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpt0IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

3\. Apply SS/PBCH configuration as per 5G_UP_ALG_2330_replaced([11114751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnth_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

4\. Apply PRACH configuration as per 5G_UP_ALG_2336_replaced([11114798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpoUlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

5\. Apply CSI-RS for tracking configuration as per 5G_UP_ALG_2343_replaced([11114897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpo7p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

6\. Allocate cell specific CSI-RS for CSI as per 5G_UP_ALG_2348_replaced([11114925](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpo7r_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

7\. Determine PUSCH and PDSCH slot configuration as per 5G_UP_ALG_2407_replaced([11115721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpw3ffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_2373_replaced([11115215](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpr--_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

8\. Determine PDCCH and PUCCH slot configuration as per 5G_UP_ALG_2449_replaced([11170225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt9a8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_2455_replaced([11116019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

9\. Determine scheduling timings as per section 5G_UP_ALG_2470_replaced([11116084](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h3PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

10\. If actCsirsMultiplexing is true or actCellCsirsTddOnSsbBf is true or actCellCsiRsImTddNonBf is true: prepare common TDRA list as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2391_replaced([11115362](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpt0IPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Prepare dedicate TDRA list as defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2396_replaced([11115520](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpvCRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

11\. Determine the periodic configuration of each slot in the pattern as per 5G_UP_ALG_2535_replaced([11117394](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp-57fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

12\. Determine the aperiodic CSI configuration of each slot in the pattern as per 5G_UP_ALG_2347_replaced([11114923](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpo7rvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

### 3.5.1.0-4 Slot pattern definition FDD slot pattern definition (ID: 11114679)

**3.5.1.0-4.0-1**  (ID: `11114680`)

Slot pattern definition For FDD, the algorithm shall determine the slot format of each slot in the pattern step by step as follows:

0\. If the FDD carrier is shared with LTE (see 5G_UP_ALG_2308_replaced([11171934](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuIaGfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), apply the current DL sharing pattern rrmFddCurrentDlShareBitmap, see 5G_UP_ALG_2304_replaced([11171916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuIaFvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

1\. Apply SS/PBCH configuration as per 5G_UP_ALG_2333_replaced([11114769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpntjPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

2\. Apply PRACH configuration as per 5G_UP_ALG_2336_replaced([11114798](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpoUlfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

3\. Apply CSI-RS for tracking configuration as per 5G_UP_ALG_2343_replaced([11114897](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpo7p_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

4\. Determine PUSCH and PDSCH slot configuration as per 5G_UP_ALG_2407_replaced([11115721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpw3ffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_2373_replaced([11115215](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpr--_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

5\. Determine PDCCH and PUCCH slot configuration as per 5G_UP_ALG_2449_replaced([11170225](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt9a8vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_2455_replaced([11116019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

6\. Determine the periodic/semi-periodic configuration of each slot in the pattern (placeholder for CSI-RS and SR configuration)

7\. Determine scheduling timings as per section 5G_UP_ALG_2470_replaced([11116084](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h3PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

8\. Determine the aperiodic CSI configuration of each slot in the pattern as per 5G_UP_ALG_2347_replaced([11114923](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpo7rvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

### 3.5.1.0-5 Slot pattern definition L2-PS shall provide the downlink slots bitmap list for K2 resource(dlSlotsBitMap) during cell setup. The pattern length of the DL slots bitmap indication is the maximum periodicity of PRACH, SSB and TRS. availableDLSLotsBitMapForK2Resource is a patternLength* numberOfSlotsPerFrame bits bitmap where each bit is reserved to an slot which can be used to schedule the PUSCH. Bit 0 of availableDLSLotsBitMapForK2Resource[0] mapped to the subframe 0 slot0 of the pattern. (ID: 11114685)

## 3.5.2 DL sharing pattern on FDD carrier shared with LTE

### 3.5.2.0-1 DL sharing pattern on FDD carrier shared with LTE Requirements moved to Section 3.21.1; Thomas Deiss, 09-02-2022 (ID: 11114691)

## 3.5.3 Determination of slot format in TDD

### 3.5.3.0-1 Determination of slot format in TDD TDD slot format determination (ID: 11114697)

**3.5.3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11114701`)

Determination of slot format in TDD A slot format indicates the number of DL and/or UL symbols in the slot:

\- DLx indicates a 14 OFDM symbol slot with x DL OFDM symbols only, followed by (14-x) GP OFDM symbols. 

\- ULy indicates a 14 OFDM symbol slot with y UL OFDM symbols only, preceeded by (14-y) GP OFDM symbols.

\- BIx y indicates a 14 OFDM symbol slot with x DL OFDM symbols starting from 0, followed by (14-x-y) GP OFDM symbols, and y UL symbols until the end of the slot.

L2-PS shall first determine slot formats of all slots in the slot pattern according to the tddFrameStructure configuration (NRCELL.tddFrameStructure):

\- a semiStatic or tdLte tddFrameStructure is defined by higher layer configuration such that DL and UL symbol positions are fixed. Flexible symbols are used to indicate guard periods.

L2-PS shall save the slot format calculated above. This will be refered to as the configured slot format pattern.

L2-PS shall calculate the resultant slot format pattern which takes into account symbol muting as per 5G_UP_75_replaced([11347067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz0fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). It should be noted that the configured slot format pattern and the resultant slot format pattern may be identical. The configured slot format pattern is needed to handle the exceptional cases noted in 5G_UP_73_replaced([11347059](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJMz_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note that some requirements refer to the rrmSlotPattern. For FR1 TDD cells the rrmSlotPattern is synonymous with the resultant slot format pattern.

---

### 3.5.3.1 UL and DL slot formats determination based on semi static TDD configuration

#### 3.5.3.1.0-1 UL and DL slot formats determination based on semi static TDD configuration TDD Semistatic slot format determination (ID: 11114706)

**3.5.3.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11114710`)

UL and DL slot formats determination based on semi static TDD configuration The Cell Setup Request message carries the cell's configured slot format pattern in the tddFrameStructure element. If the Cross Border Interference feature is activated as indicated by the actInfTddFrameMuting element of the Cell Setup Request then the interfering cell's slot format pattern is carried in the infTddFrameStructure element. The same data type is used for both elements. This requirement provides the method for converting each element of a semiStatic frame structure from the message encoding to the slot format pattern encoding used by L2-PS.

A basic slot format pattern is repeated over the slot pattern. The length of the slot format pattern is determined as follows:

  * If the Cross Border Interference feature is not actived as indicated by the actInfTddFrameMuting element of the Cell Setup Request then:

  * rrmTddUlDlPatternLength = Pattern1Length + Pattern2Length where Pattern1Length and Pattern2Length are obtained from the cell's configured slot format pattern in the tddFrameStructure element of the Cell Setup Request.

  * Otherwise:

  * ownPatternLength = Pattern1Length + Pattern2Length where Pattern1Length and Pattern2Length are obtained from the cell's configured slot format pattern in the tddFrameStructure element of the Cell Setup Request.

  * interfererPatternLength = Pattern1Length + Pattern2Length where Pattern1Length and Pattern2Length are obtained interfering cell's slot format patterh in the infTddFrameStructure element of the Cell Setup Request.

  * rrmTddUldDlPatternLength is set to the least common multiple of ownPatternLength and interfererPatternLength.




Note: the rrmTddUlDlPatternLength must divide 20ms = 20*2^referenceScs.

{

Within the slot format pattern, slots with index :

 In range [0..Pattern1.StartGpOffset - 1] shall be configured with DL14 slot format

 Pattern1.StartGpOffset shall be configured with BIx y slot format where :

\- x is equal to Pattern1.StartSymbolGpOffset

\- y is equal to 14 – x – GpLength

 Pattern1.StartGpOffset + 1 shall be configured with

\- ULy, if GpLength+ Pattern1.StartSymbolGpOffset > 14, with y = 14 - (GpLength+ Pattern1.StartSymbolGpOffset – 14)

\- UL14 otherwise

 In Range [Pattern1Length .. Pattern1Length + Pattern2.StartGpOffset – 1] shall be configured with DL14 slot format

 Pattern1Length + Pattern2.StartGpOffset shall be configured with BIx y slot format where :

\- x is equal to Pattern2.StartSymbolGpOffset

\- y is equal to 14 – x – GpLength

 Pattern1Length + Pattern2.StartGpOffset + 1 shall be configured with

\- ULy, if GpLength+ Pattern2.StartSymbolGpOffset > 14, with y = 14 - (GpLength+ Pattern2.StartSymbolGpOffset – 14)

\- UL14 otherwise

 any other index, shall be configured with UL14 slot format

}

---

### 3.5.3.2 UL and DL slot formats determination when an interfering TDD cell is configured

#### 3.5.3.2.0-1 UL and DL slot formats determination when an interfering TDD cell is configured Slot format determination rules (ID: 11114717)

**3.5.3.2.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11114721`)

UL and DL slot formats determination when an interfering TDD cell is configured A configured slot format pattern is calculated in 5G_UP_ALG_2313_replaced([11114697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnGefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) based on the tddFrameStructure(NRCELL.tddFrameStructure) in Cell Setup. The configured slot format pattern may be modified if an interfering cell's TDD frame structure is passed in the infTddFrameStructure element of the Cell Setup message. The resultant slot format pattern is calculated as follows:.

  * Modifications to the configured slot format pattern are made only when all of the below conditions are true.

  * The local cell is configured as TDD FR1 and

  * The Coordination of TDD deployments across country border feature is set to TRUE in the actInfTddFrameMuting element of the Cell Setup message.

  * If no modifications are to be made the configured slot format pattern is copied to the resultant slot pattern and the requirement is complete.

  * As only semi-static patterns are to be supported an interfering cell slot pattern is determined using the rules in 5G_UP_ALG_2320_replaced([11114706](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnGfPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). When applying the rules the infTddFrameStructure element of the Cell Setup message is used for the calculations.
  * Both the configured slot format pattern and the interfering cell slot pattern are passed in the Cell Setup message relative to their own system frame number 0. The time alignment of the configured and interfering cell slot patterns is performed using the rules in 5G_UP_ALG_2327_replaced([11114736](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnthPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
  * The configured slot format pattern and the interfering cell slot pattern are combined to form the resultant slot format pattern. The combination is based upon the symbol muting rules in 5G_UP_75_replaced([11347067](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz0fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).
  * If the bilateralAgreement element of the Cell Setup message is TRUE and actBlankDlSymbBorderCoord=TRUE then the examples of the resultant slot format patterns are given in the example in 5G_UP_ALG_8850_replaced([11114725](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpntgvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

an interfering TDD cell is configured 11114732: UL and DL slot formats determination when an interfering TDD cell is configured The following tables illustrate the results of the symbol muting rules by considering few examples  If the bilateralAgreement element of the Cell Setup message is FALSE then the resultant slot format pattern is determined by applying rules in the following table to each slot: [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a table with 5 columns and 15 rows. The columns are "Configured slot format", "Interfering cell slot format", "Resultant slot format", and "Comments". The table describes different slot configurations and their resulting outcomes based on interference.

Configured slot format | Interfering cell slot format | Resultant slot format | Comments  
---|---|---|---  
D | D | D | No interference, all symbols downlink  
DL14 | D | M | Full interference between downlink and uplink, all symbols muted  
DL14 | UL14 | DL0 | No interference, all symbols uplink  
UL14 | U | U | Full interference between uplink and downlink, all symbols muted  
UL14 | D | M | When special slot configuration matches, no interference. Special slot configuration is unchanged.  
UL14 | DL14 | UL0 | When special slot configurations do not match, special slot configuration is modified. Rule is Resultx = min (Localx, 14-Remotex), Resulty = min (Localy, 14-Remotex).  
S | S | S | When special slot configuration and downlink, special slot configuration is modified. Rule is Resultx = Localx, Resulty = 0.  
Bl10,2 | Bl10,2 | Bl10,2 | When special slot configuration and uplink, special slot configuration is modified. Rule is Resultx = 0, Resulty = Localy.  
S | S | S | When downlink and special slot configuration, downlink is modified. Rule is Resultx = 14-Remotey, Resulty = 0.  
Bl4,4 | Bl10,2 | Bl4,4 | When uplink and special slot configuration, uplink is modified. Resultx = 0, Resulty = 14-Remotex.  
S | D | S |   
Bl10,2 | DL14 | Bl10,0 |   
S | U | Bl0,2 |   
Bl10,2 | UL14 | UL4 |   
D | S | D |   
DL14 | Bl10,2 | DL12 |   
U | S | UL4 |   
UL14 | Bl10,2 | UL4 |   
|  |  |   
 If the bilateralAgreement element of the Cell Setup message is TRUE, then the resultant slot format pattern is determined as follows: |  |  |   
|  |  |   
[image-1][image_desc]The image contains a table that describes the resultant slot format based on the configured slot format and the interfering slot format, along with comments explaining the rules. |  |  |   
Configured slot format | Interfering slot format | Resultant slot format | Comments  
---|---|---|---  
D | D | D | When a downlink slot is configured at both the local and remote cells, there is no interference, since all the symbols are uplink.  
DL14 | DL14 | DL14 |   
D | U | M | When a downlink slot is configured at the local cell and an uplink slot is configured at the remote cell, based on the bi-lateral agreement the local cell will mute all the downlink symbols.  
DL14 | UL14 | DL0 |   
U | U | U | When an uplink slot is configured at both the local and remote cells, there is no interference, since all the symbols are uplink.  
UL14 | UL14 | UL14 |   
U | D | U | When an uplink slot is configured at the local and a downlink slot is configured at the remote cell, based on bi-lateral agreement, the remote cell will mute all downlink symbols. The local cell will allow all uplink symbols.  
UL14 | DL14 | UL14 |   
S | S | S | When the special slot configurations at both the local and remote cells match, there is no interference. Special slot configuration is unchanged.  
BI10,2 | BI10,2 | BI10,2 |   
S | S | S | When the special slot configurations at the local and remote cells do not match, the special slot configuration is modified. The rule is Result, min(Local, 14-Remote,), Result, Local,.  
BI4,4 | BI10,2 | BI4,4 |   
S | D | S | When a special slot is configured at the local cell and a downlink slot is configured at the remote cell, the bi-lateral argument requires the remote cell to mute all downlink interfering symbols.  
BI10,2 | DL14 | BI10,2 |   
S | U | S | When a special slot is configured at the local cell and an uplink slot is configured at the remote cell, the special slot configuration is modified. The rule is Result, 0, Result, Local,.  
BI10,2 | UL14 | BI10,2 |   
D | S | D | [Before CNI-74648] When a downlink slot is configured at the local cell and a special slot is configured at the remote cell, the downlink slot is modified. The rule is Result. -14-Remote,  
DL14 | BI10,2 | DL12 |   
D | S | D | [CNI-74648] When a downlink slot is configured at the local cell, a special slot is configured at the remote cell, and actBlankDlSymbBorderCoord=FALSE, the downlink slot is modified. The rule is Result. -14-Remote,.  
DL14 | BI10,2 | DL12 |   
D | S | D | When a downlink slot is configured at the local cell, a special slot is configured at the remote cell, and actBlankDlSymbBorderCoord=TRUE, the downlink slot is modified. The rule is Result, Remote. [End CNI-74648]  
DL14 | BI10,2 | DL10 |   
U | S | U | When an uplink slot is configured at the local cell and a special slot is configured at the remote cell, based on the bi-lateral agreement, the remote cell will mute all the downlink symbols. All the uplink symbols will be scheduled.  
UL14 | BI10,2 | UL14 |   
|  |  |   
File: CNI_74648_Resultant_slot_pattern_example.docx |  |  |   
Source: |  |  |

an interfering TDD cell is configured 11114744: UL and DL slot formats determination when an interfering TDD cell is configured The configured and interfering slot format patterns are passed through the Cell Setup message relative to their own system frame number 0. However, in the over the air system the system frame number maybe offset from that presented in the Cell Setup message (see for example 5G_UP_68_replaced([11347030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJMyPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). The calculation of the resultant slot format pattern requires the two patterns to be aligned to a common starting point. The Cell Setup message carries a patternOffset element (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2073_replaced([11278643](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpRmY_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which is calculated by Control Plane and specifies the number of slots to offset the start of the slot format pattern to achieve alignment. For each step in the process the rightmost slot format in the pattern is removed and prepended to the remaining slot formats in the pattern. Four examples below demonstrate this action. [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a diagram illustrating the concept of "patternOffset" in the context of cell setup messages and alignment processes, likely related to telecommunications. The diagram is divided into two main examples, "Example 1" and "Example 2". **General Text:** The text at the top explains that "patternOffset is 'Number of slots to offset the [start of the] pattern'". It also labels two sections as "From Cell Setup message" and "Alignment process". **Example 1:** This example shows a "local tdLTE 3ms" with "Pattern 1=10" and "patternOffset=0". This is represented by a sequence of "D"s and "S" and "U"s: "DDDDDDDSUU". Below this, under the "Alignment process" heading, it shows the same pattern offset by 0 slots, labeled with a "0": "ODDDDDDDSUU". Then, it describes an "interfering cell tdLTE 0ms" with "Pattern 1=6", "Pattern 2=4", and "patternOffset=4". This is illustrated with four rows, each representing a different offset: \- Row 1 (offset 1): "DDDDDSUUDDD" \- Row 2 (offset 2): "DDDDDSUUDD" \- Row 3 (offset 3): "DDDDDDSUUD" \- Row 4 (offset 4): "DDDDDDDSUU" **Example 2:** This example shows a "local tdLTE 3ms" with "Pattern 1=10" and "patternOffset=0". This is represented by the sequence: "DDDDDDDSUU". Below this, under the "Alignment process" heading, it shows the same pattern offset by 0 slots, labeled with a "0": "ODDDDDDDSUU". Next, it describes an "interfering cell semiStatic 1:4" with "Pattern1=5" and "patternOffset=4". This is illustrated with four rows, each representing a different offset: \- Row 1 (offset 1): "UDDDS" \- Row 2 (offset 2): "SUDDD" \- Row 3 (offset 3): "DSUDD" \- Row 4 (offset 4): "DDSUD" The diagram uses colored blocks (green, yellow, orange) to represent different elements within the patterns, though the specific meaning of these colors is not explicitly defined in the text. The letters "D", "S", and "U" likely represent different types of data or signals.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5300.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] [image-1][image_desc]The image contains two examples, Example 3 and Example 4, illustrating different cell configurations and their associated patterns. **Example 3:** This example is divided into two parts, both related to "local cell" configurations. * **First part:** * **Configuration:** "local cell tdLTE 0ms, Pattern 1=6, Pattern 2=4, patternOffset=0" * **Pattern Display:** * A row labeled "0" shows a sequence of colored blocks: Green, Green, Green, Green, Yellow, Orange, Orange, Green, Green, Green, Green. The text above these blocks reads "DDDSUUDDDD". * **Second part:** * **Configuration:** "local tdLTE 3ms, Pattern 1=10, patternOffset=6" * **Pattern Display:** * A row labeled with a single Green block shows a sequence of colored blocks: Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Yellow, Orange, Orange. The text above these blocks reads "DDDDDDDSUU". * To the right of this row, there are numbered rows from 1 to 6, each displaying a sequence of colored blocks representing a pattern. * Row 1: Orange, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Yellow, Orange. Text: "UDDDDDDDSU" * Row 2: Orange, Orange, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Yellow. Text: "UUDDDDDDDS" * Row 3: Yellow, Orange, Orange, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green. Text: "SUUDDDDDDD" * Row 4: Green, Yellow, Orange, Orange, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green. Text: "DSUUDDDDDD" * Row 5: Green, Green, Yellow, Orange, Orange, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green. Text: "DDSUUDDDDD" * Row 6: Green, Green, Green, Yellow, Orange, Orange, Green, Green, Green, Green, Green. Text: "DDDSUUDDDD" **Example 4:** This example is also divided into two parts, describing a "local cell" and an "interfering cell". * **First part:** * **Configuration:** "local cell semiStatic 1:4 Pattern1=5, patternOffset=0" * **Pattern Display:** * A row labeled "0" shows a sequence of colored blocks: Green, Green, Green, Green, Yellow, Orange. The text above these blocks reads "DDDSU". * **Second part:** * **Configuration:** "interfering cell tdLTE 0ms, Pattern 1=6, Pattern 2=4, patternOffset=0" * **Pattern Display:** * A row labeled "0" shows a sequence of colored blocks: Green, Green, Green, Green, Yellow, Orange, Orange, Green, Green, Green, Green. The text above these blocks reads "DDDSUUDDDD".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5300.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] Note: Currently the configured slot format pattern is never rotated (the offsetPattern passed by Control Plane will always be 0). This element is provided in the Cell Setup message should it be needed in the future. It is an implementation decision whether to use the configured slot format patterns offsetPattern. The interfering slot format pattern shall always be used. File: PatternRotationExamples.xlxs Folder:

### 3.5.4 SS/PBCH slot configuration

#### 3.5.4.0-1 SS/PBCH slot configuration TDD SS/PBCH slot configuration (ID: 11114751)

**3.5.4.0-1.0-5**  (ID: `13481009`)

SS/PBCH slot configuration A SS/PBCH slot is a slot which has a SS/PBCH configuration, i.e. where SS/PBCH may be transmitted.

SS/PBCH slots positions within a SS burst set and the number of SS/PBCH blocks per SS/PBCH slot depends on numerology. SS burst set is repeated every _ssBurstSetPeriod within the slot pattern._

Each candidate SS/PBCH block shall be configured as follows:

 Transmitted/not transmitted according to the list of activated SS/PBCH (nActivatedSsPbch) as specified in the 5G_UP_7805_replaced([11347149](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubKa4fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and as per 5G_UP_ALG_4114_replaced([11173803](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuWch_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_4131_replaced([11173914](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuXqofvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) according to the frequency range and subcarrier spacing.

 SSB Indexes as per 5G_UP_IF_1429_replaced([11512163](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tTpwjvv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and corresponding to the indexes of the activated SS/PBCH list from 0 to nActivatedSsPbch-1.

 beamId as per 5G_UP_ALG_11982_replaced([11111700](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpP6FfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

A SS/PBCH slot configuration is active if it contains at least one activated (muted or not) SS/PBCH block.

In every active SS/PBCH slot, specific processing shall be triggered as per 5G_UP_ALG_4108_replaced([11173749](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1efvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and the beam indication shall be provided to indicate to L1 as per 5G_L2_23011_replaced([11155919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_248INvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

SS/PBCH blocks are expected to be mapped on DL symbols according to slot format. For FR1 TDD SS/PBCH is an exception to the muting rules as per 5G_UP_73_replaced([11347059](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJMz_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). To accomodate this exception the slot format determination is made using the configured slot format pattern as per 5G_UP_ALG_2313_replaced([11114697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnGefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In every active SS/PBCH slot;

If **NRCELL.muteLastSymbolInSSBSlot is set to 0**

Last DL symbol is not muted on any SSB slot.

Else If NRCELL.muteLastSymbolInSSBSlot is set to 1

Last DL symbol is muted on 0th SSB slot of transmitted SSB slots, when no CSI-RS is allocated in the last symbol of 0th SSB slot.

Else If **NRCELL.muteLastSymbolInSSBSlot** is set to 2****

Last DL symbol is muted on each transmitted SSB slot, when no CSI-RS allocated in the last symbol of this SSB slot.

SS-BURST SCS supported combinations are

**[Before CB013712-B] 240kHz for FR2 and**[End CB013712-B]****

 30kHz for FR1

Note nActivatedSsPbch is the number of activated SS/PBCH (see 5G_UP_82_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))

---

#### 3.5.4.0-2 SS/PBCH slot configuration FDD SS/PBCH slot configuration (ID: 11114769)

**3.5.4.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `12767844`)

SS/PBCH slot configuration SS/PBCH slots positions within a SS burst set and the number of SS/PBCH blocks per SS/PBCH slot depends on numerology. SS burst set is repeated every _ssBurstSetPeriod within the slot pattern._

For FDD, each candidate SS/PBCH block shall be configured as follows:

\- Transmitted/not transmitted according to 5G_UP_ALG_4114_replaced([11173803](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuWch_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with number of activated SS/PBCH (nActivatedSsPbch, see 5G_UP_82_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) set to 1.

\- Only one SS/PBCH block is expected to be configured and activated (nActivatedSsPbch, see 5G_UP_82_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Each SS/PBCH transmission occasion shall be mapped to the same SS/PBCH block index that shall be derived from the list of activated SS/PBCH as described in the requirement 5G_UP_7805_replaced([11347149](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubKa4fvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). 

  * [CB008577-A] If the cell has FDD beamforming enabled ([11111439](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpNd2_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), full sector beam for DL shall be indicated for SSB transmission. Else [End CB008577-A] no beamforming is being used, as per 5G_UP_ALG_55_replaced([11094821](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kmgIIvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))



A SS/PBCH slot configuration is active if it contains at least one transmitted SS/PBCH block.

In every active SS/PBCH slot, specific processing shall be triggered as per 5G_UP_ALG_4108_replaced([11173749](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuV1efvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

##### 3.5.4.1 SS/PBCH configuration error handling

###### 3.5.4.1.0-1 SS/PBCH configuration error handling Status and diagnostic information report (ID: 11114782)

**3.5.4.1.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11114786`)

SS/PBCH configuration error handling When the gNB detects an invalid SS/PBCH configuration (see 5G_UP_82_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), the cell setup procedure is rejected with the following data.

The 5G-L2-PS verifies:

 The number of transmitted SS/PBCH is equal to the SS/PBCH configured which are not inactive (see 5G_UP_82_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 ~~The configured SS/PBCH do not overlap Uplink symbols in the frame structure.~~ (removed due to the L2-PS cost to check it at the cell setup procedure)

By default all diagnosticInformation[0-13] fields are initialized to 0x00000000 and when the check is failed, then the gNB reports a negative acknowledgement with the following information:

 The status is set to “NOK” (EStatus5G_NotOk).

 The cause is set to 0x00000002 (ECause5G_InvalidParameter).

 The “diagnosticInformation“ array is fulfilled with data used for the check as follow:

 The “diagnosticInformation[0]” element is filled with the value 0x00000008.

 The “diagnosticInformation[1]” element is set to the value of groupPresence.

 The “diagnosticInformation[2]” element is set to the inOneGroup.

 The “diagnosticInformation[3]” element is set to the number of SS/PBCH configured.

 The “diagnosticInformation[4]” element is set to the number of SS/PBCH transmitted.

 The “diagnosticInformation[5]” element is set to the 4 most significant bytes of bitmap of the inactive SS/PBCH.

 The “diagnosticInformation[6]” element is set to the 4 least significant bytes of bitmap of the inactive SS/PBCH.

 The “diagnosticInformation[7-13]” elements are set to 0x00000000.

---

###### 3.5.4.1.0-2 SS/PBCH configuration error handling Additional alarm information (ID: 11114789)

**3.5.4.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `11114792`)

SS/PBCH configuration error handling The additional text sent in the alarm by the gNB is detailed hereafter, when faultId is set to EFaultId_GnbCpRtCellUpdateFailedAl with the extraFaultInfo[0] set to 0x00000008.

Cause: Invalid SS/PBCH configuration.

SS/PBCH group presence : < extraFaultInfo[1] in decimal >

SS/PBCH in one group presence : < extraFaultInfo[2] in decimal >

number of SS/PBCH configured : < extraFaultInfo[3] in decimal >

number of SS/PBCH transmitted : < extraFaultInfo[4] in decimal >

inactive SS/PBCH bitmap : < extraFaultInfo[5] in heaxdecimal or binary > < extraFaultInfo[6] in heaxdecimal or binary>

---

## 3.5.5 PRACH occasions and slots configuration

### 3.5.5.0-1 PRACH occasions and slots configuration PRACH slot positions (ID: 11114798)

**3.5.5.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `11114808`)

PRACH occasions and slots configuration A PRACH slot is a slot which has a PRACH configuration, i.e. where RA preamble transmission may occur.

PRACH slot positions within the slot format pattern and PRACH Preamble Ocassions configuration within PRACH Slots shall be derived from prachConfiguration and number of configured SS/PBCH (nConfiguredSsPbch, see 5G_UP_82_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) from message PsCell_CellSetupReq. Refer to 5G_UP_ALG_1959_replaced([11111903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRIOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the PRACH configuration.

If PRACH slot positions derived from prachConfiguration conflict with slot format (DL slot or Special slot), such slot should not be regarded as PRACH slot. For FR1 TDD PRACH is an exception to the muting rules as per 5G_UP_73_replaced([11347059](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJMz_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). To accomodate this exception the slot format determination is made using the configured slot format pattern as per 5G_UP_ALG_2313_replaced([11114697](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnGefvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The PRACH slots shall be indexed as per 5G_UP_ALG_1965_replaced([11111936](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvQvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) using rrmPrachSlotIndex.

For short PRACH format with same numerology as PUSCH, a PRACH slot occupies exactly one slot in the pattern and shall be indexed by rrmPrachSlotIndex.

[CB008474-A]

In TDD FR1, when short PRACH numerology is configured with different numerology as PUSCH (NRBTS/NRCELL/actPrachC2Scs15kHzTddFr1 is set to TRUE), each PRACH slot spans over two PUSCH slots (see 5G_UP_ALG_1959_replaced([11111903](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRIOfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). gNB-DU shall determine corresponding PUSCH slot which has valid Rach Occasions based on prachConfiguration. If startSymbol from prachConfigurationIndex is smaller than 7, both PUSCH slots contains valid RO, otherwise only odd slot contains valid RO.

[End CB008474-A]

For long PRACH format, there are as many PRACH slots in the same subframe in the pattern as indicated by 5G_UP_ALG_1969_replaced([11111949](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvRvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) according the PUSCH numerology. Each slot in the same subframe of the pattern share the same rrmPrachSlotIndex.

For long PRACH format 1 in FDD, one PRACH occasion will last for 3 ms starting from the subframe indicated in the prach configuration.

Only the PRACH slots associated to activated SS/PBCH are used. the one which are not associated to actived SS/PBCH, are reused for PUSCH transmissions as no UE will transmit a PRACH associated to a SS/PBCH not transmitted.

---

### 3.5.5.0-2 PRACH occasions and slots configuration PRACH occasions configuration (ID: 11114812)

**3.5.5.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11114820`)

PRACH occasions and slots configuration In each PRACH slot, there are up to X PRACH transmission occasions on which a preamble may be transmitted according to PRACH format and the number of configured PRACH regions.

For long PRACH format, all PRACH slots in the subframe shall be treated as 1 PRACH transmission occasion in time domain.

Each PRACH transmission occasion mapped to a SS/PBCH block [CB012192-A] list [End CB012192-A] as per 5G_UP_ALG_1970_replaced([11111961](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be configured with:

\- PRACH transmission occasion time index in the PRACH slot

\- PRACH region index

\- [CB012192-A] For each SSB in the SSB list as per 5G_UP_ALG_1970_replaced([11111961](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), [End CB012192-A] relative SS/PBCH block index for beam correspondence, used for example to determine Msg2 transmission beamId

\- [CB012192-A] For each SSB in the SSB list as per 5G_UP_ALG_1970_replaced([11111961](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), [End CB012192-A] beamId to be used for Msg1 reception and Msg3 reception

beamId for Msg1 and Msg3 reception is the same as the beamId used for the relative SS/PBCH block transmission.

For a cell on FDD carrier, each PRACH transmission occasion is mapped to a SS/PBCH block as per 5G_UP_ALG_1970_replaced([11111961](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). There is no beamforming on FDD carrier, so no beamID is needed for any UL channels (PUSCH, PUCCH, PRACH, etc.).

There is no beamforming on FDD carrier (BeamId is set to 0, as per 5G_UP_ALG_3388_replaced([11152417](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ksgCZPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Though, an SS/PBCH index different from 0 could be configured. The SS/PBCH index identified with the procedure indicated in 5G_UP_ALG_2333_replaced([11114769](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpntjPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall be used for each (SS/PBCH index, RACH occasion association).

A PRACH slot configuration is active if it has at least one PRACH transmission occasion configured.

In every active PRACH slot, preambles reception shall be configured to L1 as per 5G_UP_ALG_3376_replaced([11152282](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kse0TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and beam indication provided as per 5G_L2_23011_replaced([11155919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_248INvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In case of long PRACH format, preambles reception shall be configured to L1 as per 5G_UP_ALG_3376_replaced([11152282](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kse0TvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) at first PRACH slot of same preamble. L2PS shall send the beam indication(DlData_PatternConfigReq) provided as per 5G_L2_23011_replaced([11155919](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_248INvvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) at all PRACH slots of same preamble.

---

PRACH occasions and slots configuration 11114870: PRACH occasions and slots configuration 11114870: PRACH occasions and slots configuration 11114870: PRACH occasions and slots configuration 11114870: PRACH occasions and slots configuration L2-PS shall provide the PRACH transmission occasions list in cell setup response for TDD FR1 cells only while both activation flag actEcpriPhase3 in PsCell_CellSetupReq (NRBTS.actEcpriPhase3) message and RD parameter rdStaticPrachActivation_UP(refer to 5G_L2_1223_replaced([11184132](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27o26fvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) are set to TRUE. PRACH transmission occasions information will be sent to CP-RT. CP-RT forwards this information to DU-cOAM, which in turn forwards these to RU via eCPRI RP1 M-plane.

PRACH transmission occasions list includes:

\- PRACH pattern length(Prach Association Period)

\- PRACH transmission occasion information, includes PRACH scs, PRACH preamble repetition, CP duration of PRACH occasion, sequence duration, sequencyOffset, gtLowBandwidth, gtHighBandwidth, PRACH preamble bandwidth.

\- PRACH transmission occasion lists. It includes:

o PRACH transmission occasion timeOffset.

o PRACH transmission occasion reOffset.

[CB012192-A]

o beam list associated with the PRACH transmission occasion (5G_UP_ALG_1970_replaced([11111961](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpRvS_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). Each beam is represented by a patternID and eAxC information.

[End CB012192-A]

o In case of supercell mode enabled,

eAxC information per patternID in PRACH transmission occasion list in PsCell_CellSetupResp is substituted with information received from ulSubcellList in PsCell_CellSetupReq.

o otherwise,

information about eAxC is used from patternID applied for the PRACH transmission occasion except for PRACH conformance test. (During PRACH conformance test, CPlane shall set this information, refer to 5G_L3_IF_1200)

Note: This is due to the fact that eAxC information provided is only for patternID=0 when supercell mode is enabled and does not reflect the correct values for eAxC.

If NRCELL.addExtDelayCompensation is set to non-zero value.

\- Adjust timeOffset(MPlane) for each PRACH occasions as below;

timeOffset(MPlane) = timeOffset(MPlane) + NRCELL.addExtDelayCompensation;

\- Number of PRACH preamble repetition shall be adjusted as per the PRACH window shift, PRACH window is shifted by NRCELL.addExtDelayCompensation. Any incomplete repetition(moving out of the Uplink slot) shall not be considered for "PRACH preamble repetition" calculation.

\- CP duration of the PRACH occasion shall be adjusted with the following rule:

o if the symbol part of the adjusted timeOffset(MPlane) is bigger than or equal to the first PUxCH (OFDM) symbol then long CP duration shall be set ([image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a light-colored background. The equation is: $N_{CP,1}^{RA} = N_{CP}^{RA} + 16x [Tc]$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5326.001.png[/image_path][/image-0], otherwise short CP duration ([image-1][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is written in a subscript and superscript format. The equation is: $N_{CP}^{RA} = N_{CP}^{RA}$ The equation shows that the variable $N_{CP}^{RA}$ is equal to itself. This is a tautology and does not provide any new information. The equation is written in a clear and legible font. The background of the image is white.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5326.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]) shall be used.

Note: timeOffset(MPlane) is a value including frame time offset, slot time offset and rest of the time offset

PRACH occasion = [ Cyclic Prefix + Preamble sequence + … + Preamble sequence ]. In more detail,

[image-2][image_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating a data structure composed of several segments. The structure is presented as a horizontal arrangement of rectangular boxes, separated by vertical lines. The leftmost box is shaded gray and contains the text "Cyclic Prefix". To the right of this box are four identical white boxes, each containing the text "Preamble sequence". These five boxes together form a larger rectangular block. Below this block, there is a horizontal line, and then a series of vertical lines that appear to extend downwards, suggesting a continuation or a breakdown of the structure. However, no text or further elements are present in these lower sections. The overall impression is a representation of a data packet or frame, where the initial part consists of a cyclic prefix followed by multiple preamble sequences.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5326.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]

Note: During PRACH conformance test, all bits b0-b9 of IE receivingSlot to 1. Value of prachOccasions should therefore not be based on the value of IE receivingSlot but should simply be so that each and every UL slot of the PRACH conformance TDD frame pattern is a PRACH slot. There would be no need for PRACH pattern length of 5 / 50 ms and a PRACH pattern length of 1 / 10 ms could be used instead.

[image-3][image_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the concept of PRACH occasions in a communication system. The diagram uses a timeline and frequency axis to represent these occasions. On the left side, there are labels "frequencyOffset MPlane" and "timeOffset MPlan", suggesting that these are parameters that define the location of a PRACH occasion. The right side of the diagram shows two shaded rectangular areas, each labeled "PRACH occasion". These rectangles represent time intervals during which a PRACH transmission can occur. The upper PRACH occasion is associated with "PrachBandwidth MPlane", indicating that the bandwidth allocated for this occasion is defined by this parameter. Arrows point to the top and bottom of this occasion, suggesting a duration or range. The lower PRACH occasion is associated with "reOffset MPlane", implying a different type of offset or configuration. Similar arrows indicate its extent. At the bottom of the diagram, there is a label "start of PRACH patter", which likely indicates the beginning of a sequence or pattern of PRACH occasions. The diagram visually represents how different parameters influence the timing and frequency of PRACH opportunities.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5326.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]
