11116059: PUCCH configuration 11116059: PUCCH configuration 11116059: PUCCH configuration 11116059: PUCCH configuration 11116059: PUCCH configuration [Before CNI-111721] The 4 PUCCH tokens, or if long PUCCH is configured 12PUCCH tokens, are configured in slots without PRACH configuration or with PRACH configuration if PRACH multiplexing (NRBTS.actPrachMultiplexing) is enabled or long PUCCH (NRCELL.pucchModeSelect = longPucch) configured. [CNI-111721] If short PUCCH is configured (NRBTS.NRCELL.pucchModeSelect = shortPucch), 4 PUCCH tokens are configured for each UL slot which fulfills any one of the following conditions: * No PRACH is configured in the slot; * PRACH is configured in the slot and PRACH multiplexing is enabled (NRBTS.actPrachMultiplexing = TRUE); If long PUCCH is configured (NRBTS.NRCELL.pucchModeSelect = longPucch), no PUCCH token is required for PDSCH scheduling. [End CNI-111721] On a FDD carrier shared with LTE, if nrLteDssMode > 1 and FDM of PUCCH is enabled by setting the actFdmScheduling parameter, then MaxDssPucchTokenF0 (2) PUCCH F0 tokens are configured per slot in slots without PRACH configuration. No PUCCH resources are configured in slots with PRACH configuration before PRACH multiplexing is implemented. If nrLteDssMod > 1 and FDM of PUCCH is disabled by the actFdmScheduling parameter, the operation mode of 5GC001586-C according 5G_UP_ALG_2473_replaced([11116092](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) is applied in L2PS. The guaranteed number of PRBs in the inner area (between LTE PUCCH subbands) is 7 (shortPucch) or 6 (longPucch) (IE PucchModeSelect in PsCell_CellSetupReq). If nrLteDssMode > 1 and FDM of PUCCH is enabled by setting the actFdmScheduling parameter, in order to increase the number of connected UEs in a cell -the PUCCH F0 resources for HARQ A/N are shifted to the outer area, outside the LTE PUCCH. Thus every UL slot with the exception of PRACH slots is eligible for carrying CSI on PUCCH F2, simultaneously with HARQ A/N and/or SR for other UEs. The slots carrying PUCCH F2 are allocated in the course of the PsUserSetup procedure, like for dedicated FDD. -for NSA w/o PRB blanking, two PUCCH F0 resources for HARQ A/N per slot are allocated in the area outside the LTE PUCCH space. All remaining PRBs in the area outside the LTE PUCCH space are assigned to PUCCH F0 resources for SR. For overlapping HARQ A/N, CSI and SR assignments for the same UE, the rules of 5G_UP_ALG_22280_replaced([11187527](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvbanPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) apply. Please note that with this approach the use of standalone SR or SR multiplexed with HARQ on F0 will be introduced e2e, cf. 5G_UP_IF_1670_replaced([11513946](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tT9SgPv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_IF_1687_replaced([11514057](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tT-govv1Ee-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_1218_replaced([11104718](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_koAj__vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If nrLteDssMode > 1 and FDM of PUCCH is disabled by the actFdmScheduling parameter, the operation mode of 5GC001586-C is applied in 5G-L2-PS. Note: Even for the legacy LTE RUs it can be assumed that the full number of NR PRBs (so e.g. 52PRBs on 10MHz) can be supported. This has been analyzed in the FS1TAs for 5GC000719/724/729. Thus, there is no need for a special handling of legacy RU HW. Example for 10MHz: [image-0][image_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a Downlink Shared Uplink (DSS) UL FDM schema in 1586-K. It depicts the multiplexing of up to one PUCCH F2 and three PUCCH F0 within the same symbol, operating at 10MHz with 52 NR PRBs, specifically for C5 only, and both Standalone (SA) and Non-Standalone (NSA) modes. The diagram is structured as a grid representing time slots and Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs). It is divided into several sections: * **Top Section:** Labeled "Slot i, slot type C5" and "Slot i+1, slot type C5", indicating two consecutive time slots. The horizontal axis represents PRBs, numbered from 0 to 9 and A to D. The vertical axis indicates different resource allocations. * **Resource Allocation Areas:** * "1PRBs statically assigned": A single row of PRBs at the bottom, colored orange, representing static assignments. * "LTE PUCCH space": A row of PRBs colored light blue, indicating space for LTE PUCCH. * "PRBs statically assigned to LTE": A row of PRBs colored light blue, indicating static assignments for LTE. * "LTE / NR dynamic area": The largest section of the grid, colored green, representing the dynamic allocation area for LTE and NR. * "6PRBs statically assigned to NR": A section of PRBs colored green, indicating static assignments for NR. * "LTE PUCCH space": Another row of PRBs colored light blue. * "1PRBs statically assigned to NR": A single row of PRBs at the bottom, colored orange. * **UE Allocations:** Within the "LTE / NR dynamic area", different User Equipments (UEs) are represented by colored blocks: * UE1: A large green block in the upper left. * UE2: A large green block below UE1. * UE3: A large green block in the upper right. * UE4 (msg 3): A green block in the lower right. * UE5: A yellow block positioned between UE1, UE2, and UE3, and also a smaller yellow block below UE4. * **PUCCH Allocations:** * PUCCH F2: Represented by yellow blocks. * PUCCH F0 for HARQ Ack: Represented by orange blocks. * PUCCH F0 for SR (+HARQ): Represented by orange blocks. * PUSCH: Represented by green blocks. * Not used: Represented by white blocks. * **Legend:** A legend on the right side of the diagram explains the color coding for PUCCH F2, PUCCH F0 for HARQ Ack, Not used, PUCCH F0 for SR (+HARQ), and PUSCH. * **Explanatory Text:** A green text box on the right provides detailed explanations regarding the scheduling and multiplexing of PUCCH F0, SR, and HARQ A/N resources, including specific rules and considerations for different scenarios. * **Initial UL BWP:** A label indicating "Initial UL BWP" with a downward arrow, suggesting a reference point for the Uplink Bandwidth Part. The diagram visually demonstrates how different types of uplink control information (PUCCH F2, PUCCH F0 for SR, PUCCH F0 for HARQ Ack) and user data (PUSCH) are allocated and multiplexed across PRBs and time slots for various UEs in a DSS UL FDM configuration. It highlights the static and dynamic allocation areas, as well as the specific placement of PUCCH resources.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5627.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] For all diagrams in this requirement: File: DSS NR Slot in 1586-K - PRB allocation.xlsx Folder: Notes: 1\. For 10 MHz carriers, the scheme above has the restriction that * only up to two SR resources on F0 for each already assigned C5 slot in the pattern can be added in the outer area for 10MHz, another two more resources in the 7-th PRB of the inner NR reserved area. * On 15Mhz four resources more are available in the outer area, on 20MHz another four on top. 2\. Even in the second generation of NR UE chipsets, some vendors will not support the optional capability twoPUCCH-F0-2-ConsecSymbols allowing 2 PUCCH of format 0 (or 2) in consecutive symbols for the same UE. Therefore,  5G-L2-PS must schedule HARQ A/N on the same symbol as SR resource for slots where HARQ A/N is scheduled in the same slot as SR, cf. 5G_UP_ALG_2473_replaced([11116092](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).  5G-CP-RT must configure two HARQ resources in symbol 13 in the UE. The following example diagrams shows PRB allocation in slot i with type C5 according 5G_UP_ALG_2415_replaced([11115737](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpxehPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_2415_replaced([11115737](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpxehPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_2422_replaced([11115762](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpxei_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) featuring F2 followed by a slot i+1 with slot C5 carrying only a two PUCCH symbols for A/N on F0, assuming that k upper (1st diagram) or lower (2nd diagram) PRBs are blanked. [image-1][image_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating resource allocation in a cellular network. It depicts different User Equipments (UEs) and their assigned resources, likely Physical Resource Blocks (PRBs), over time. The diagram uses colored blocks to represent these resources. Here's a breakdown of the visual elements: * **Grid:** The entire image is overlaid with a fine white grid, suggesting a time-frequency plane or a similar resource grid. * **Color Legend (Right Side):** * Yellow block: Represents "UE5". * Gold block: Represents "UE4 (msg 3)". * White block: Unassigned or idle. * Dark Green block: Represents "UE1", "UE2", and "UE3". * Light Blue block: Labeled "prachPrbOffset". * **UE Allocations:** * **UE1:** Occupies a large dark green rectangular area in the upper left portion of the main allocation section. * **UE2:** Occupies a dark green rectangular area below UE1. * **UE3:** Occupies a large dark green rectangular area to the right of UE1 and above UE4. * **UE5:** A small yellow rectangular block positioned between UE2 and UE3, and below UE1. * **UE4 (msg 3):** A dark green rectangular block positioned below UE3 and to the right of UE5. * **prachPrbOffset:** A light blue rectangular area at the bottom, spanning a significant width. It has an arrow pointing to it from the left, indicating an offset. * **Other Elements:** * Several small gold blocks are present at the bottom, aligned with the "prachPrbOffset" area, and also to the right of UE4. These likely represent specific control signals or initial access procedures. * There are horizontal blue lines that appear to delineate the boundaries of resource allocations for different UEs. * Vertical lines also segment the resource blocks. * The top section of the image is mostly empty grid space, possibly representing future time slots or a different resource dimension. * The left side has some unlabeled vertical bars, which might indicate time or frequency axis labels, but they are not clearly defined. The diagram visually represents how different UEs are scheduled for transmission or reception within a given time and frequency resource pool, with specific blocks allocated for initial access (prachPrbOffset and associated gold blocks) and data transmission by individual UEs.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5627.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] [image-2][image_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a communication system, likely a cellular network, with different components represented by colored blocks and labels. The diagram is laid out on a grid, suggesting a timeline or resource allocation. On the left side, there are vertical labels that appear to represent different User Equipments (UEs) or entities. These are labeled as "UE1", "UE2", and "UE5". There is also a label "UE4 (msg 3)". The main body of the diagram consists of several horizontal bands of colored blocks. The topmost band, below a grey bar, is a light blue band filled with a grid pattern. This band has small orange and yellow blocks interspersed within it. Below the light blue band are large green blocks labeled "UE1", "UE2", "UE3", and "UE4 (msg 3)". "UE1" and "UE2" occupy the left side, with "UE1" above "UE2". "UE3" and "UE4 (msg 3)" occupy the right side, with "UE4 (msg 3)" above "UE3". There is a yellow block labeled "UE5" positioned between "UE1" and "UE4 (msg 3)", extending downwards. There are also small orange blocks adjacent to the yellow block and within the green blocks. Further down, there is a large green area with a grid pattern, spanning the width of the diagram. Below this, there is another light blue band with a grid pattern, similar to the one at the top. On the right side of the diagram, there is a legend with colored squares and corresponding labels: \- Yellow square: "pr" \- Gold square: "pr" \- White square: "pr" \- White square: "pr" \- Dark green square: "pr" There are also blue arrows indicating some form of signaling or data flow. One arrow originates from the light blue band at the top, points downwards, and then curves to the right, pointing towards the "pr" label. Another arrow originates from within the green blocks (near "UE3") and points downwards towards the "pr" label. The overall impression is a visualization of resource scheduling or communication events over time, with different UEs utilizing specific resources or transmitting messages. The labels "msg 3" and "pr" suggest specific communication protocols or message types.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5627.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] Notes: -In case of PRB blanking, the total bandwidth of Msg3 transmissions and retransmissions is restricted to 7PRBs, cf. 5G_UP_ALG_5026_replaced([11201742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwZD4vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In case of NB-IOT, we process like for PRB blanking. 5G-CP-RTverifies by checking MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/nbrOfBlankedPRBsLowEdgeUl if the higher part (over LTE PUCCH area) could be used for PUCCH transmission. The bandwidth of the initial BWP comprises all PRBs in the carrier bandwitdth to allow for frequency hopping of msg4 A/N not colliding with PUCCH F2. The guaranteed number of PRBs in the inner area (between LTE PUCCH subbands) is 7. It consists of 6 fixed NR PRBs and 1 PRB of minimum dynamic NR PRB allocation. Example for SA and NSA in the same DSS cell not blanked on 20MHz: [image-3][image_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating a Downlink Shared Channel (DSS) Uplink Frequency Division Multiplexing (FDM) schema. The schema is for a 1586-K system, supporting up to one PUCCH F2 and 2 to 6 PUCCH F0 multiplexed in the same symbol. It operates at 20MHz, with 106 NR PRBs, and is specific to C5, SA, and NSA configurations. The diagram is structured as a grid representing time-frequency resources. The horizontal axis represents PRBs (Physical Resource Blocks) labeled from 0 to 9, A, B, C, and D, divided into two slots: "Slot i, slot type C5" and "Slot i+1, slot type C5". The vertical axis represents different types of resource allocations and user equipment (UE) data. Key areas and labels on the vertical axis include: \- "3 PRBs statically assigned" at the bottom. \- "LTE PUCCH space" indicated by a blue shaded area. \- "prachPrbOffset" is a specific offset within the "LTE PUCCH space". \- ">=6 PRBs statically assigned to NR" is a region for NR assignments. \- "LTE / NR dynamic area" is the largest central region for dynamic allocation. \- "mPRBs statically assigned to LTE" is a region for LTE assignments. The diagram shows different colored blocks representing various types of data transmissions: \- Yellow blocks represent "PUCCH F2". \- Brown blocks represent "PUCCH F0 for HARQ Msg4 (with FH) or any other HARQ w/o FH". A note clarifies that with FH, only the second hop is evaluated in the U-plane, and both hops are configured in the UE. It also states that SR resources in symbol 12 have higher priority. \- Yellow blocks with a darker outline represent "PUCCH F0 for SR (+A/N)". \- Green blocks represent "PUSCH". \- White blocks are labeled as "Not used". Specific UE data blocks are shown within the "LTE / NR dynamic area": \- "UE1" is a large green block. \- "UE2" is a large green block below UE1. \- "UE3" is a large green block. \- "UE4 (msg 3)" is a green block below UE3. \- "UE5" is a smaller yellow block located between UE1 and UE2, and also a smaller yellow block to the right of UE4. A blue arrow indicates "Initial UL BWP" (Initial Uplink Bandwidth Part). A legend on the right side of the diagram explains the colors and their corresponding meanings: \- Yellow: PUCCH F2 \- Brown: PUCCH F0 for HARQ Msg4 (with FH) or any other HARQ w/o FH. Note: w/ FH only the second hop is evaluated in the U-plane. Both hops are configured in the UE. The SR resources in symbol 12 shall be assigned with higher priority. \- White: Not used \- Yellow with darker outline: PUCCH F0 for SR (+A/N) \- Green: PUSCH[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5627.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] Notes: -the SR allocation of one UE, being semi-permanent, must not be overwritten when CSI and SR resource for another UE is added or modified. -on 10MHz, one frequency hop of HARQ A/N F0 resource for msg4 configured in UE may overlap with PUCCH F0 for SR of another UE. This hop is not decoded by the U-plane. -wrt. multiplexing of different kinds of UCI on one PUCCH, the only UE capability mandatory for UE is mux-SR-HARQ-ACK-CSI-PUCCH-OncePerSlot set to sameSymbol. I.e., the UE supports multiplexing SR, HARQ-ACK and CSI on a PUCCH once per slot, when SR, HARQ-ACK and CSI are supposed to be sent with the same starting symbols on the PUCCH resources in a slot. For other HARQs, the same handling as for NSA is applied. In order to allow frequency hopping of HARQ A/N for msg4 interfering neither LTE nor blanked areas, the initial Ul BWP is spanned between the outermost PRB opposite to the blanked edge and the last (7th) PRB in the inner area guaranteed to NR.

---

### 3.5.11.1 PUCCH L1 capacity limits to be considered by L2-PS

#### 3.5.11.1.0-1 PUCCH L1 capacity limits to be considered by L2-PS Long PUCCH related L1 capacity limits (ID: 11116066)

**3.5.11.1.0-1.0-3**  (ID: `13455734`)

PUCCH L1 capacity limits to be considered by L2-PS The total number of available UL subcells in each cell is determined by the algorithm in 5G_UP_ALG_5230_replaced([11210126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw3lB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The maximum number of PUCCH F1/F3 per slot for a given cell with only a dedicated assigned subcell (no pooled subcell) is:

rrmMaxNumOfF1PerCellPerSlot = maxNumPucchF1PerSlot (refer 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2851_replaced([11309807](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrLD0fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

rrmMaxNumOfF3PerCellPerSlot = maxNumPucchF3PerSlot (refer 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2851_replaced([11309807](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrLD0fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If scheUePoolingAllowed is set to false

The maximum number of UCI on PUSCH per slot for a given cell with only a dedicated assigned subcell (no pooled subcell) is:

rrmMaxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot = maxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot (refer 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2075_replaced([11278656](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpRmZfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In a specific UL slot, the number of PUCCH F1 left for A/N reporting:

rrmNumOfF1ForHarq = rrmMaxNumOfF1PerCellPerSlot – number of PUCCH F1 required for reception of Scheduling Requests in same slot

**Editorial note:**

**the expectation here is that rrmNumOfF1ForHarq is updated for both common and dedicated PUCCH HARQ allocations.**

In a specific UL slot, the number of PUCCH F3 left for A/N reporting:

rrmNumOfF3ForHarq = rrmMaxNumOfF3PerCellPerSlot – number of PUCCH F3 required for reception of Periodic CSI Report (in same slot)

Note: This value may be determined within DL scheduler algorithm, e.g. when HARQ feedback is >2bit per UL feedback per slot. It must be considered as well in UL scheduler in case the UE is allocated for UCI on PUSCH, refer to 5G_UP_3216_replaced([11397338](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf2Gk_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The detailed counting table is described in 5G_L2_412_replaced([11152043](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24mJ-PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Rationale: In case HARQ on F3 and pCSI on F3 are multiplexed and transferred over UCI on PUSCH the 2 resources have to be deducted per slot, refer to 5G_UP_3216_replaced([11397338](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf2Gk_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In a specific UL slot, the number of UCI on PUSCH left for A/N reporting (resource usage is specified in L1 requirement for UCI on PUSCH capacity):

rrmNumCsiPart1OnPusch = rrmMaxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot – number of UCI required for reception of Periodic CSI Report (in same slot)

Note: Determined in UL scheduler, when a specific UE is allocated for PUSCH and a periodic CSI is allocated in the same slot. L1 capacity is specified in 5G_UP_3244_replaced([11397643](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf3UwPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_3217_replaced([11397345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf2GlfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The detailed counting table is described in 5G_L2_412_replaced([11152043](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24mJ-PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else **if scheUePoolingAllowed is set to true**

The maximum number of UCI on PUSCH per slot should be given by the baseband resource manager (BBRM) to the cell per BB pooling period:

rrmMaxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot = maxNumPuschPerSlotByBBRM

Note: maxNumPuschPerSlotByBBRM is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_5264_replaced([11210371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw4zLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The number of PUCCH F1 for A/N reporting should be given by the baseband resource manager (BBRM) to the cell per BB pooling period:

rrmNumOfF1ForHarq = maxNumPucchF1PerSlotByBBRM

Note: maxNumPucchF1PerSlotByBBRM is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_5264_replaced([11210371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw4zLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The number of PUCCH F3 for A/N reporting should be given by the baseband resource manager (BBRM) to the cell per BB pooling period.

rrmNumOfF3ForHarq = maxNumPucchF3PerSlotByBBRM

Note: maxNumPucchF3PerSlotByBBRM is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_5264_replaced([11210371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw4zLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The number of UCI on PUSCH left for A/N reporting (resource usage is specified in L1 requirement for UCI on PUSCH capacity): 

rrmNumCsiPart1OnPusch = rrmMaxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot

Note: the parameter (rrmNumCsiPart1OnPusch) is useless when BB pooling features are activated, the formula is updated just to ensure rrmNumCsiPart1OnPusch having a valid value.

[Before CB013712-A]

In TDD:

There are no PUCCH F1/F3 in TDD Special Slots.

If subcell pooling configuration is supported, one of the cells in the L1 board, for L1 boards supporting subcell pooling, gets additional shared subcell, hence, additional PUCCH capacity.

The maximum number of PUCCH F1/F3 per slot for a given cell with additional shared (pooled) subcell with subCellPooling is:

rrmMaxNumOfF1PerCellPerSlot= maxNumPucchF1PerSlot * currentNumOfAssignedUlSubcells / numOfUlSubcellsWithoutPooling ,

rrmMaxNumOfF3PerCellPerSlot= maxNumPucchF3PerSlot * currentNumOfAssignedUlSubcells / numOfUlSubcellsWithoutPooling

where corresponding values for currentNumOfAssignedUlSubcells and numOfUlSubcellsWithoutPooling can be found based on the min/max number of subcells assigned by the subcell pooling algorithm in 5G_UP_ALG_5233_replaced([11210144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw3lCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: The additional capacity from subcell pool can provide additional PUCCH resources for more HARQ A/N handling.

[End CB013712-A]

Note, in principle rrmNumOfF1ForHarq is even min(rrmMaxNumOfF1PerCellPerSlot – number of PUCCH F1 required for reception of Scheduling Requests in same slot; number of PUCCH tokens defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2455_replaced([11116019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), but there is no need to limit it by the number of PUCCH tokens which is the maximum of PUCCH F1 we can expect per UL slot due to DL FDM.

Note, before support of NIDD parameter actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot = true, C-Plane reserves enough PUCCH F1 and PUCCH F3 for L2-PS to allocate for HARQ feedback, i.e. L2-PS can’t exceed L1 processing capacity limits wrt. PUCCH if number of PDSCH scheduled per slot stays below 9.

---

#### 3.5.11.1.0-2 PUCCH L1 capacity limits to be considered by L2-PS Long PUCCH related L1 capacity limits - ABIL (ID: 13455934)

**3.5.11.1.0-2.0-1**  (ID: `13455935`)

PUCCH L1 capacity limits to be considered by L2-PS For ABIL TDD FR1:

There are no PUCCH F1/F3 in TDD Special Slots.

If subcell pooling configuration is supported, one of the cells in the L1 board, for L1 boards supporting subcell pooling, gets additional shared subcell, hence, additional PUCCH capacity.

The maximum number of PUCCH F1/F3 per slot for a given cell with additional shared (pooled) subcell with subCellPooling is:

rrmMaxNumOfF1PerCellPerSlot= maxNumPucchF1PerSlot * currentNumOfAssignedUlSubcells / numOfUlSubcellsWithoutPooling ,

rrmMaxNumOfF3PerCellPerSlot= maxNumPucchF3PerSlot * currentNumOfAssignedUlSubcells / numOfUlSubcellsWithoutPooling

where corresponding values for currentNumOfAssignedUlSubcells and numOfUlSubcellsWithoutPooling can be found based on the min/max number of subcells assigned by the subcell pooling algorithm in 5G_UP_ALG_5233_replaced([11210144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw3lCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: The additional capacity from subcell pool can provide additional PUCCH resources for more HARQ A/N handling.

[CB013712-A] Not valid for NewFR1 [End CB013712-A]

---

#### 3.5.11.1.0-3 PUCCH L1 capacity limits to be considered by L2-PS Short PUCCH related L1 capacity limits (ID: 11116077)

**3.5.11.1.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `13459334`)

PUCCH L1 capacity limits to be considered by L2-PS The total number of available UL subcells in each cell is determined by the algorithm in 5G_UP_ALG_5230_replaced([11210126](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw3lB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The maximum number of PUCCH F1/F3 per slot for a given cell with only a dedicated assigned subcell (no pooled subcell) is:

rrmMaxNumOfF1PerCellPerSlot = maxNumPucchF1PerSlot (refer 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2851_replaced([11309807](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrLD0fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

rrmMaxNumOfF3PerCellPerSlot = maxNumPucchF3PerSlot (refer 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2851_replaced([11309807](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TrLD0fvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If scheUePoolingAllowed is set to false

The maximum number of UCI on PUSCH per slot for a given cell with only a dedicated assigned subcell (no pooled subcell) is:

rrmMaxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot = maxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot (refer 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_2075_replaced([11278656](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpRmZfvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

In a specific UL slot, the number of PUCCH F1 left for A/N reporting:

rrmNumOfF1ForHarq = rrmMaxNumOfF1PerCellPerSlot – number of PUCCH F1 required for reception of Scheduling Requests in same slot

**Editorial note:**

**the expectation here is that rrmNumOfF1ForHarq is updated for both common and dedicated PUCCH HARQ allocations.**

In a specific UL slot, the number of PUCCH F3 left for A/N reporting:

rrmNumOfF3ForHarq = rrmMaxNumOfF3PerCellPerSlot – number of PUCCH F3 required for reception of Periodic CSI Report (in same slot)

Note: This value may be determined within DL scheduler algorithm, e.g. when HARQ feedback is >2bit per UL feedback per slot. It must be considered as well in UL scheduler in case the UE is allocated for UCI on PUSCH, refer to 5G_UP_3216_replaced([11397338](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf2Gk_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_4871_replaced([11196450](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwCepPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The detailed counting table is described in 5G_L2_412_replaced([11152043](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24mJ-PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Rationale: In case HARQ on F3 and pCSI on F3 are multiplexed and transferred over UCI on PUSCH the 2 resources have to be deducted per slot, refer to 5G_UP_3216_replaced([11397338](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf2Gk_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

In a specific UL slot, the number of UCI on PUSCH left for A/N reporting (resource usage is specified in L1 requirement for UCI on PUSCH capacity):

rrmNumCsiPart1OnPusch = rrmMaxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot – number of UCI required for reception of Periodic CSI Report (in same slot)

Note: Determined in UL scheduler, when a specific UE is allocated for PUSCH and a periodic CSI is allocated in the same slot. L1 capacity is specified in 5G_UP_3244_replaced([11397643](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf3UwPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_3217_replaced([11397345](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uf2GlfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The detailed counting table is described in 5G_L2_412_replaced([11152043](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_24mJ-PvTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Else **if scheUePoolingAllowed is set to true**

The maximum number of UCI on PUSCH per slot should be given by the baseband resource manager (BBRM) to the cell per BB pooling period:

rrmMaxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot = maxNumPuschPerSlotByBBRM

Note: maxNumPuschPerSlotByBBRM is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_5264_replaced([11210371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw4zLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The number of PUCCH F1 for A/N reporting should be given by the baseband resource manager (BBRM) to the cell per BB pooling period:

rrmNumOfF1ForHarq = maxNumPucchF1PerSlotByBBRM

Note: maxNumPucchF1PerSlotByBBRM is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_5264_replaced([11210371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw4zLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The number of PUCCH F3 for A/N reporting should be given by the baseband resource manager (BBRM) to the cell per BB pooling period.

rrmNumOfF3ForHarq = maxNumPucchF3PerSlotByBBRM

Note: maxNumPucchF3PerSlotByBBRM is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_5264_replaced([11210371](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw4zLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

The number of UCI on PUSCH left for A/N reporting (resource usage is specified in L1 requirement for UCI on PUSCH capacity): 

rrmNumCsiPart1OnPusch = rrmMaxNumCsiPart1OnPuschPerSlot

Note: the parameter (rrmNumCsiPart1OnPusch) is useless when BB pooling features are activated, the formula is updated just to ensure rrmNumCsiPart1OnPusch having a valid value.

[Before CB013712-A] 

In TDD:

There are no PUCCH F1/F3 in TDD Special Slots.

If subcell pooling configuration is supported, one of the cells in the L1 board, for L1 boards supporting subcell pooling, gets additional shared subcell, hence, additional PUCCH capacity.

The maximum number of PUCCH F1/F3 per slot for a given cell with additional shared (pooled) subcell with subCellPooling is:

rrmMaxNumOfF1PerCellPerSlot= maxNumPucchF1PerSlot * currentNumOfAssignedUlSubcells / numOfUlSubcellsWithoutPooling ,

rrmMaxNumOfF3PerCellPerSlot= maxNumPucchF3PerSlot * currentNumOfAssignedUlSubcells / numOfUlSubcellsWithoutPooling

where corresponding values for currentNumOfAssignedUlSubcells and numOfUlSubcellsWithoutPooling can be found based on the min/max number of subcells assigned by the subcell pooling algorithm in 5G_UP_ALG_5233_replaced([11210144](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kw3lCvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: The additional capacity from subcell pool can provide additional PUCCH resources for more HARQ A/N handling.

[End CB013712-A] 

Note, in principle rrmNumOfF1ForHarq is even min(rrmMaxNumOfF1PerCellPerSlot – number of PUCCH F1 required for reception of Scheduling Requests in same slot; number of PUCCH tokens defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2455_replaced([11116019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h0PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), but there is no need to limit it by the number of PUCCH tokens which is the maximum of PUCCH F1 we can expect per UL slot due to DL FDM.

Note, before support of NIDD parameter actIncreasedFdmUsersPerSlot = true, C-Plane reserves enough PUCCH F1 and PUCCH F3 for L2-PS to allocate for HARQ feedback, i.e. L2-PS can’t exceed L1 processing capacity limits wrt. PUCCH if number of PDSCH scheduled per slot stays below 9.

---

## 3.5.12 Scheduling timings

### 3.5.12.0-3 Scheduling timings K1 value computation (ID: 11116092)

**3.5.12.0-3.0-1**  (ID: `11116093`)

Scheduling timings Terminology

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11116097`)

Scheduling timings  k1: the timing difference in slots between the PDSCH and corresponding HARQ-AN, that is, assuming PDSCH is transmitted in slot n, corresponding HARQ-AN shall be received in slot n+k1; it is indicated to UE by PDSCH-to-HARQ-feedback timing indicator field in DCI (TS38.212 §7.3.1.2 ) together with RRC IE PUCCH-Config.dl-DataToUL-ACK (TS38.213 §9.1.2, TS38.331 §6.3.2) for DCI Format 1_1 and a specific set of values for DCI Format 1_0. On top, for a DCI format 1_0 with CRC scrambled by a TC-RNTI (CRI), a minimum time between the end of the PDSCH and its associated PUCCH transmisison is expected (µ=0, 21 symbols ; µ = 1, 27 symbols ; µ=3, 80 symbols).

 k1 set: the set of k1 values allowed in a cell, it is indicated to UE by RRC IE PUCCH-Config. dl-DataToUL-ACK, number of elements in this set shall not exceed 8 (TS38.331 §6.3.2 PUCCH-Config);

 k1 slot pattern: the mapping relation between PDSCH slots and PUCCH slots within a slot format pattern (see 5G_UP_ALG_2296_replaced([11114662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpmfb_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the definition of slot format pattern);.

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11116101`)

Scheduling timings Slots for k1 computation

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-2.0-3**  (ID: `12678460`)

Scheduling timings K1 value shall be computed for each slot in the pattern configured with PDSCH.

On an FDD carrier shared with LTE [CB007372-AA] and NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false [End CB007372-AA], 5G-L2-PS shall compute K1 values for all DL slots which are available for 5G assuming the maximum DL sharing pattern rrmFddMaxDlShareBitmap (see 5G_UP_ALG_2304_replaced([11171916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuIaFvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), excluding DL slots with SS/PBCH or TRS only configuration.

_[Note] This ensures that K1 values are available for any configured DL sharing pattern._

[CB007372-AA] Note: For DSS with NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=true all slots are available for scheduling because the NR dedicated area is always available to NR (see [12620260: Resource sharing in asymmetric DSS](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_NsAEcDfZEfCYiuaHdt7wgQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) [End CB007372-AA]

On an FDD carrier shared with LTE, 5G-L2-PS shall not be assigning the HARQ F0 resources in symbol 13 to UEs having F2 configured in the same slot, cf. 5G_UP_ALG_22280_replaced([11187527](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kvbanPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). 

**In FDD, K1 value shall be computed for:**

o SSB slots used for PDSCH transmission scheduled by DCI format 1_1, where numPdschPrbInSsbSlots is greater than 0.

o TRS slots where numPdschPrbInTrsSlots is greater than 0.

o Regardless of NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschAtSsbSlots and NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschRmCsirsForTracking, when NRBTS.NRCELL.pucchModeSelect = longPucch and actFdmScheduling = True.

**On an FDD carrier shared with LTE, K1 value shall be computed for:**

o SSB slots used for PDSCH transmission scheduled by DCI format 1_1, where numPdschPrbInSsbSlots is greater than 0.

o TRS slots where numPdschPrbInTrsSlots is greater than 0.

o Regardless of NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschAtSsbSlots and NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschRmCsirsForTracking, when NRBTS.NRCELL.pucchModeSelect = longPucch and actFdmScheduling = True.

**In TDD** FR1, K1 value shall be computed for:****

o SSB slots used for PDSCH transmission scheduled by DCI format 1_1, only while numPdschPrbInSsbSlots is greater than 0.

o TRS slots (in normal DL slot or special slot(BI10,2)), while numPdschPrbInTrsSlots is greater than 0.

o Regardless of NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschAtSsbSlots and NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschRmCsirsForTracking, when NRBTS.NRCELL.pucchModeSelect = longPucch and actFdmScheduling = True and when NRBTS.NRCELL.numOfSubSectors = 0 and NRBTS.NRCELL.actSuperCell =False.

If NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschAtSsbSlots=true and NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschRmCsirsForTracking=True,**K1 value shall be computed for:**

o SSB slots used for PDSCH transmission scheduled by DCI format 1_1, where numPdschPrbInSsbSlots is greater than 0.

o TRS slots where numPdschPrbInTrsSlots is greater than 0.

o Regardless of NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschAtSsbSlots and NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschRmCsirsForTracking, when NRBTS.NRCELL.pucchModeSelect = longPucch and actFdmScheduling = True or if NRBTS.NRCELL.cellTechnology is equal to TDD and (NRBTS.NRCELL.numOfSubSectors = 0 and NRBTS.NRCELL.actSuperCell = False).

Prach multiplexing shall be enabled.

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `11116112`)

Scheduling timings k1 set

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-3.0-7**  (ID: `13471560`)

Scheduling timings [Before CB013712-B] For FR2 SA scheduled PDSCH by DCI format 1-0 with MAC PDU of MSG4 (containing MAC CE CRI) is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_23347_replaced([11116136](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp1I6vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).[End CB013712-B] 

For any other PDSCH scheduled by DCI format 1-0 , K1 set is hardcoded as {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8}, see TS38.213 section 9.2.3 and 5G_UP_ALG_4448_replaced([11178110](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ku0WmfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

For PDSCH scheduled by DCI format 1-1, K1 is a set of different values in range [k1min, k1min + k1range], where:

  * K1min: the minimum slot difference between PDSCH and corresponding HARQ-AN, k1min shall satisfy the PDSCH processing time requirement and other requirements in some special conditions, see the details below;



L2PS shall report k1 set to C-Plane in the response message during cell setup procedure.

**FDD:**

if(NRBTS/NRCELL/prachConfigurationIndex ∈ {40, 41, 42, 43}) //extended cell range enabled

{

k1min = 4;

}

else

{

if(NRBTS/NRCELL. pucchModeSelect == longPucch) //long PUCCH is enabled

{

k1min = 3;

k1range = 1;

if(NRBTS/NRCELL. lteNrDssMode > ‘none’) //the carrier is shared with a LTE cell

{

k1min = MAX{3, k2max + 1};

k1range = 0;

}

if(NRBTS/NRDU. flexibleCAMode > 0 ‘BasicCAMode’) //DPA is enabled

{

if(flexibleCAMode == 3 ‘NumberCellAmount’)

{

k1min = MAX{k1min, 4};

if((front haul type is not eCPRI) and (NRBTS/NRCELL.increaseK1ULCA == 1))

{

k1min = MAX{k1min, 5};

}

}

else

{

if(NRBTS/NRCELL.actK1Opt == 1) // K1 optimization with Long PUCCH enabled

{

k1min = MAX{k1min, 3};

if(NRBTS/NRCELL.increaseK1ULCA == 1)

{

k1min = MAX{k1min, 4};

}

}

else if (NRBTS/NRCELL.actK1Opt == 2) // K1 optimization with Inter-gNB CA is configured

{

if(front haul type == eCPRI)

{

k1min = MAX{k1min, 5};

}

else

{

k1min = MAX{k1min, 4};

}

}

else

{

k1min = k2max + 3;

}

}

}//end DPA is enabled

}

else //short PUCCH is enabled

{

k1min = MAX{rdK1min, 2};

}

}

**FR1 TDD:**

if(NRBTS/NRCELL. pucchModeSelect == longPucch) //long PUCCH is enabled

{

if (TDD frame structure type is tdLte and tdLteConfig is config 0)

k1min = 6

else

k1min = MAX{(k2max + 1), rrmk1DuetoMinUePdschProcTime};

}

else //short PUCCH is enabled

{

k1min = MAX{rdK1min, 3};

}

[Before CB013712-B] 

**FR2 TDD:**

k1min = MAX{rdK1min, 2}.

[End CB013712-B] 

Notes:

1\. According to the minimum PDSCH processing time requirement for MAC PDUs other than RA Msg4, k1 must be larger than or equal to roundup(N1 / 14), where N1 is defined in TS38.214 table 5.3-1; For RA Msg4, k1 shall be greater than or equal to roundup((NT,1 + 0.5)*(2^μ)), see TS38.213 section8.4 for the definition of NT,1; The PDSCH processing time requirement for RA Msg4 is more rigid than that for non-Msg4, and DL scheduler doesn’t support different k1 setting for each type of MAC PDUs, so the requirement for RA-Msg4 is applied for all the MAC PDU scheduling;

2\. In case long PUCCH is enabled and (TD-LTE frame structure with tdLteConfig0 is not configured), k1 shall always be greater than k2max so that UL scheduler can know how many HARQ-AN bits will be multiplexed on the PUSCH to be scheduled;

2a. In case long PUCCH is enabled and (TDD frame structure type is tdLte and tdLteConfig is config 0), k1 > k2max is no longer mandatory. In such configurations, k1 >= k2max so that UL scheduler can know how many HARQ-AN bits will be multiplexed on the PUSCH to be scheduled;

3\. In case short PUCCH is enabled, k1 > k2max is no longer mandatory, because HARQ-AN bits will never be multiplexed on PUSCH; k1 must satisfy the minimum PDSCH processing time requirement in this case;

4\. rdK1Min is not specified by 3GPP specification, but an R&D parameter introduced for Airphone support and is defined in 5G_L2_1062_replaced([11184142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27o26_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For tests with Airphone, rdK1min shall be set to 2 for FR1 carriers[Before CB013712-B] and to 4 for FR2 carriers.[End CB013712-B] 

5\. GP symbols in special slots shall be excluded from PDSCH processing time, for example, in case of semiStatic frame structures, if PDSCH is scheduled in BIx,y slot, and y + 14 - numberOfShortPucchSymbolsInUlSlots > N1 then HARQ-AN can be transmitted in the next slot (n+1 slot), otherwise HARQ-AN shall be transmitted no earlier than n+2 slot. if BIx,y slots can’t be used for PDSCH (according to 5G_UP_ALG_2407_replaced([11115721](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpw3ffvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) , and PDSCH is scheduled in the slot preceding BIx,y slot, if x + y - numberOfShortPucchSymbolsInUlSlots > N1, then HARQ-AN can be transmitted in the next slot (n+1 slot); otherwise HARQ-AN shall be transmitted no earlier than n+2 slot. where N1 is defined in TS38.214 table 5.3-1

6\. If NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity is false, it shall be derived from 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). If NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity is true, it shall be derived from 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) with single K2 mode (This trick is applicable because multiple K2 is only on FR1 'tdLte' frame structure 5G_UP_66_replaced([11347021](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJMxvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G_UP_68_replaced([11347030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJMyPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). In future if multiple K2 will be supported with other frame structure, this tricky k2max setting and [12496661: k1 set for multiple K2 mode](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_zDHnAxRaEfCph-OiSyk16Q?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) needs re-evaluating).**Rationale for considering k2max is that 3GPP defines implicitly in 38.213 Ch. 9 that k1 must not be smaller than k2 if corresponding A/N is multiplexed in corresponding PUSCH. For simplification k1min shall be greater k2max to avoid difficult synchronization procedures between DL and UL scheduler.**

7\. Applicable for FR1 [Before CB013712-B] and FR2 NSA only[End CB013712-B] : **rrmk1DuetoMinUePdschProcTime is the k1 value required to fulfil the 3GPP given UE PDSCH processing procedure time. rrmk1DuetoMinUePdschProcTime = 4 to satisfy highest PDSCH processing procedure time which is Msg4 transmission related. Note, any other transmission than Msg4 would require only rrmk1DuetoMinUePdschProcTime <= 3 but L2-PS doesn't support transmission type/content dependent k1 slot patterns yet.**

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-4**  (ID: `11116136`)

Scheduling timings k1 set for TDD FR2 Msg4

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `13471616`)

Scheduling timings For FR2 scheduled PDSCH by DCI format 1-0 with MAC PDU of MSG4 (containing MAC CE CRI) 

k1 slot pattern shall not be applicable to Msg4 and k1 set is hardcoded as {6,7,8} and k1MinMsg4=6 due to the longer MSG4 processing time for FR2 SA case.

[CB013712-B] Not valid for NewFR1 [End CB013712-B]

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-5**  (ID: `11116142`)

Scheduling timings k1 value computation in P(S)Cell

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `11116155`)

Scheduling timings In this requirement “PUCCH token” indicates the maximum number of PDSCH slots associated with the same PUCCH slot, it shall be called “PUCCH token for k1 computation” to avoid confusion with the “PUCCH token” defined in [11116019](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_BxSLwPvJEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) which indicates the maximum number of concurrent PUCCH that can be decoded by L1 per UL sub-cell.

For the purpose of k1 computation, number of PUCCH tokens per PUCCH slot shall be set as follows:

  * For FR2, only short PUCCH is supported: number of PUCCH token per PUCCH slot shall be set to 2 if PUCCH can be transmitted in the slot, otherwise set to 0.
  * For FR1,

  * If short PUCCH is enabled, same as FR2 cell.

  * If long PUCCH is enabled, set to 5 if no UL slot is muted; set to 9 if partial UL slots are muted.



Selected PUCCH token shall be assigned to the DL TD scheduler.

if((NRBTS/NRDU.flexibleCAMode == ‘BasicCAMode’) && (NRCELL.cellTechnology == ‘FDD’) && **(NRBTS/NRDU.actInterNumerologyCa == TRUE) )**

{

5G-L2-PS shall determine k1 value for PDSCH slots according to 5G_UP_ALG_2478_replaced([11116165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp1v8_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for P(S)Cell;

(**Note: in 5GC001390 only FDD cell can be P(S)Cell, so the K1 algorithm will only be applicable to FDD cell only);**

}

else if( (NRBTS/NRCELL.cellTechnology == ‘TDD’) && (NRBTS/NRCELL. pucchModeSelect == longPucch)

{

5G-L2-PS shall determine K1 for slot N per 5G_UP_ALG_2481_replaced([11116175](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp1v9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for P(S)Cell and potential SCell;

}

else

{

5G-L2-PS shall determine PUCCH timing for HARQ feedback with K1 from PDSCH slot N such that:

  * K1min value is used whenever a PUCCH token in slot N+K1min is available for HARQ feedback.
  * If no PUCCH token is available for HARQ feedback in slot N+K1min, then closest PUCCH token available for HARQ feedback shall be selected from slot N+K1 with K1 in the _configured set of values_
  * If no PUCCH token is found for HARQ feedback, then it shall be mapped to the last slot with exhausted PUCCH tokens. The slot is marked as in PUCCH token overbooking.
  * L2-PS shall not calculate new k1 candidate value when there are already 8 k1 values in the K1 set, the left slots in the slot pattern shall try to find PUCCH token in slots from existing k1 values. If such PUCCH token cannot be found, then PDSCH for that slot will not be scheduled.



}

For example, in a FDD cell with DPA disabled, if PUCCH can be scheduled in PRACH slots, then k1 = k1min. Otherwise, assuming PDSCH is transmitted in slot n, if the UL slot in slot n + k1min is a PRACH slot, then k1 = k1min + 1 shall be used until the next SSB slot in DL, after that k1 = k1min can be used again.

Notes:

1\. The k1 value computing method described above applies to both CA UE and non-CA UE in its P(S)Cell respectively;

2\. In the following cases, uplink channel multiplexing in PRACH slot must be activated:

  * NRBTS/NRCELL/actPdschAtSsbSlots = TRUE AND PUCCH mode is short PUCCH AND DL PRB sharing in subframe 0/5/9 is enabled (rrmLteDLPrbSharing = TRUE, see 5G_UP_ALG_4722_replaced([11172097](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuJoPfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));



Rationale: If PRACH multiplexing is not enabled, L2-PS would have to compute k1 value for all DL slots in the maximum DL sharing pattern rrmFddMaxDlShareBitmap (see 5G_UP_ALG_2304_replaced([11171916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuIaFvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) which are available to 5G, if k1max is reached, DL slot #9 has to be excluded (k1 is not computed for slot #9), and peak TPUT is degraded. The reason why slot#9 is excluded is that CRM assign DL slots to NR in the following sequence: [3, 8, 1, 6, 4, 2, 7, 0, 5, 9], slot#9 is the last slot that will be assigned to NR.

  * Distributed PUCCH allocation is enabled (NRBTS/NRCELL.flexibleCAMode > 0 ‘BasicCAMode’) AND PUCCH mode is long PUCCH (NRBTS/NRCELL. pucchModeSelect = ‘longPucch’);



**[CB009510-A-UPD001]**

3\. K1 value shall be computed for SSB slots and TRS slots, regardless of NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschAtSsbSlots and NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschRmCsirsForTracking, when NRBTS.NRCELL.pucchModeSelect = longPucch and actFdmScheduling = True**[End CB009510-A-UPD001]**

  1. **[PR797110-25R1]If NRBTS.NRCELL.cellTechnology is equal to TDD, K1 value shall be computed for SSB slots and TRS slots, regardless of NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschAtSsbSlots and NRBTS.NRCELL.actPdschRmCsirsForTracking, when NRBTS.NRCELL.pucchModeSelect = longPucch and actFdmScheduling = True and NRBTS.NRCELL.numOfSubSectors = 0 and NRBTS.NRCELL.actSuperCell = False.**[End PR797110-25R1]****

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-6**  (ID: `11116158`)

Scheduling timings k1 value in SCell for CA guest UE

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-6.0-1**  (ID: `11116162`)

Scheduling timings CA guest UE is a UE which uses current cell as a SCell; as a contrast, those UEs which setup RRC connection in current cell (uses current cell as PCell) are called local UEs.

For FR2 CA, k1 slot pattern in SCell for CA guest UE is identical to the k1 slot pattern in its PCell.

For FR1 CA:

If(FDD cell acts as P(S)Cell)

{

if( (NRBTS/NRDU.flexibleCAMode == ‘BasicCAMode’) && (NRBTS/NRDU.actInterNumerologyCa == TRUE)) //pipe-line solution

{

k1 slot pattern in SCell shall be determined as per 5G_UP_ALG_2478_replaced([11116165](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp1v8_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

}

else //DPA enabled

{

k1 slot pattern in SCell is identical to the k1 slot pattern in P(S)Cell;

}

}

else //TDD cell acts as P(S)Cell

{

k1 slot pattern in SCell shall be determined as per 5G_UP_ALG_2481_replaced([11116175](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp1v9vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

}

While computing k1 slot pattern for SCell, P(S)Cell L2PS shall compute k1 value for the SSB slot or TRS slot in P(S)Cell even if NRBTS/NRCELL.actPdschAtSsbSlots is set to FALSE in P(S)Cell.

SCell L2PS shall select the k1 values from the k1 slot pattern computed by P(S)Cell for slots which can be used for PDSCH transmission. For the carrier shared with LTE, SCell L2PS shall select k1 values for DL slots which are available for 5G from assuming the maximum DL sharing pattern to 100% DL sharing pattern.

Notes

1\. For FR2 CA, only the case with identical slot pattern configured for both PCell and SCell(s) is supported currently;

2\. For FR1 CA, when Distributed PUCCH allocation is enabled, UL channel multiplexing in PRACH slot (5GC001348) must be enabled so that PUCCH can be transmitted in PRACH slot;

3\. In case FR1 TDD cell acts as P(S)cell, CA and UL slot muting is mutual exclusive currently, that is, if UL slot is muted, CA shall not be enabled and vice versa. this restriction is not enforced by PDL rule but highlighted in CuDo.

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-7**  (ID: `12496661`)

Scheduling timings **k1 set for multiple K2 mode**

---

**3.5.12.0-3.0-7.0-1**  (ID: `12496662`)

Scheduling timings [CB013305-C-UPD004] When multiple K2 is being used (see [11348263: Multiple K2 Mode](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubTk3vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9)), gNB shall choose K1 set according UL slots as below.

  * For the first UL slot after special slot, K1 set is [12, 11, 8, 7]
  * For the second UL slot after special slot, K1 set is [7, 6, 5, 4]



[End CB013305-C-UPD004]

### 3.5.12.0-4 Scheduling timings K1 value computation for PUCCH pipeline solution (ID: 11116165)

**3.5.12.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11116172`)

Scheduling timings [Before CNI-88614]This requirement is applicable only If distributed PUCCH allocation algorithm is not appliable according to 5G_UP_ALG_2885_replaced([11123447](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqjhoPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

In case inter-numerology CA is activated according to 5G_UP_1278_replaced([11357256](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucbmN_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), P(S)Cell 5G-L2-PS shall follow the pipeline solution according to 5G_UP_1383_replaced([11358572](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uciT7vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) to determine PUCCH timing for HARQ feedback with K1 for DL slots of carriers over one 5G-L2-PS with P(S)Cell carrier deployed and DL slots of carriers over another 5G-L2-PS with Scell carriers deployed.

For DL slots over 5G-L2-PS with P(S)Cell carrier deployed, P(S)Cell 5G-L2-PS shall determine the PUCCH timing for HARQ feedback with K1 for DL slot in slot NPcell_PDSCH which include SSB slots, TRS slots and PDSCH slots for L2PS scheduler with P(S)Cell carrier deployed such that:

\- K1minPcell is used whenever a PUCCH token in slot NPcell_PDSCH + K1minPcell is available for HARQ feedback.

\- If no PUCCH token is available for HARQ feedback in slot NPcell_PDSCH + K1minPcell, then closest PUCCH token available for HARQ feedback shall be selected from slot NPcell_PDSCH +K1 with K1 which is range in [K1minPcell, K1minPcell + K1Range].

\- If no PUCCH token is found for HARQ feedback, then it shall be mapped to the last slot in range of [K1minPcell, K1minPcell + K1Range] with exhausted PUCCH tokens. The slot is marked as in PUCCH token overbooking.

After decision of PUCCH timing for HARQ feedback for each P(S)Cell DL slot, P(S)Cell 5G-L2-PS shall determine the PUCCH timing with K1 for DL slots of NScell_PDSCH over another 5G-L2-PS with Scell carriers deployed for CA UE HARQ feedback through P(S)Cell PDSCH according to pipeline solution defined in 5G_UP_1383_replaced([11358572](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uciT7vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) which have to same PUCCH timing for HARQ feedback.

  * While µScell_PDSCH = 1, then 5G-L2-PS determine the same PUCCH timing of NPcell_PDSCH for NScell_PDSCH while:



NPcell_PDSCH = Rounddown(NScell_PDSCH * 2^( µPcell_PUCCH - µScell_PDSCH)) + x * 2^µPcell_PUCCH; only x=2 is supported.

NPcell_PUCCH = NPcell_PDSCH + K1Pcell

One example of FDD and FR1 TDD CA with 4:1 DL/UL ratio refers to pipeline concept at 5G_UP_1383_replaced([11358572](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uciT7vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) regarding K1 value for P(S)Cell and Scell.

Note:

  * K1 table is calculated when P(S)Cell setup according to inter-numerology CA feature flag according to 5G_UP_1278_replaced([11357256](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ucbmN_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and may not include the configuration of Scell frame structure due to Scell may be setup later.

  * The algorithm will only consider the band combination for FDD and FR1 TDD, so in case µPcell_PDSCH = 0; then µScell_PDSCH = 1; or µPcell_PDSCH = 1; then µScell_PDSCH = 0;

  * Current algorithm will work for µPcell_PDSCH = µPcell_PUCCH.




K1 set of values shall be in range K1min and K1min + K1range where:

\- K1min is the maximum between rdK1min and a fixed value based on carrier numerology such that the number of symbols between last scheduled PDSCH symbol and first PUCCH symbol in reporting slot respects the UE processing timing (see 38.214 section 5.3 for more details on UE processing timing), so K1min is always based on more stringent numerology, that is to say  = 0.

  * On an FDD carrier, only long PUCCH is supported, then the K1 set is defined by K1min = 3 and K1range = 1 for PDSCH transmissions scheduled by DCI format 1_1. Rationale is that the long PUCCH or the PUSCH carrying the HARQ Ack/Nack starts at symbol 0 already and hence the 3GPP given UE PDSCH processing procedure time of 8 to 11 symbols requires K1min > 1 (considering 3GPP 38.214 ch. 5.3 and 38.213 ch. 9.2.5 incl. timing advance and cell range up to 15km). As there might be UEs with additional DM-RS for PDSCH the UE PDSCH processing procedure time can be greater than 14 symbols and hence requires even K1min>2\. Note, with long PUCCH configured, K1 shall always be greater than K2 to guarantee that UL scheduler gets required UCI info (e.g. expected number of A/N bits) which shall be multiplexed in PUSCH in time, i.e. before starting PUSCH scheduling.

  * K1range is the maximum number of slots between two PUCCH tokens available for HARQ. K1min + K1range shall not exceed the maximum value supported by L1 as per 5G_UP_ALG_2473_replaced([11116092](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp0h3vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). The cardinality of the K1 set reported to C-Plane via PsCell_CellSetupResp must be <= 8 and hence L2-PS shall remove the K1 values which were not used in any DL slot. If the cardinality of the K1 set is still >8 then highest by highest K1 value shall be removed from the set until a valid cardinality is reached whereby the corresponding DL slots with removed K1 value can’t be scheduled for PDSCH anymore.

  * rdK1min value is not allowed by 3gpp UE capability and is introduced for Airphone support and defined in 5G_L2_1062_replaced([11184142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_27o26_vTEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For the purpose of testing with Airphone, rdK1min shall be set to 2 in below6GHz and 4 in above6GHz.




The parameters are defined as follows:

K1minPcell, K1 minimum value for P(S)Cell K1 calculation.

µk1min, Numerology which will define the UE processing time of PDSCH according to section 5.3 of TS38.214, where µ corresponds to the one of (µPDCCH, µPDSCH, µUL) resulting with the largest PDSCH processing time.

µPcell_PDSCH, Numerology of PCell PDSCH.

µPcell_PUCCH, Numerology of PCell PUCCH, same as µUL above.

µScell_PDSCH, Numerology of SCell PDSCH.

NPcell_PDSCH, DL slot of PCell PDSCH.

NScell_PDSCH, DL slot of SCell PDSCH.

Note: Pipeline solution is not supported anymore.

[End CNI-88614]

### 3.5.12.0-5 Scheduling timings K1 value computation for long PUCCH (ID: 11116175)

**3.5.12.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11116178`)

Scheduling timings Slot for K1 computation

---

**3.5.12.0-5.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `12562022`)

Scheduling timings 5G L2PS shall determine k1 value for the following slots:

  * [Before CB014093-E] DL slots and special slots that can be used for PDSCH transmission. [CB014093-E]
  * DL slots that can be used for PDSCH transmission.
  * Special slots with minimum number of DL symbols required for PDSCH scheduling, calculated as per [11115218](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpr-_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https%3A%2F%2Fdn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com%2Fgc%2Fconfiguration%2F9) considering actEnhancedPDSCHSchSplSlot to be TRUE. [End CB014093-E]
  * UL slots with odd slot number;
  * UL slots with specific slot number for CA, refer to 5G_UP_ALG_14985_replaced([11116217](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XAPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

---

**3.5.12.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `11116186`)

Scheduling timings k1 value computation for long PUCCH

---

**3.5.12.0-5.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `12705968`)

Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings 12705968: Scheduling timings Basic k1 computation are performed in the following steps: * Set number of PUCCH tokens per UL slot (note 7) to **5 for "non-UL-slot-muted" case; to 9 for UL slot muted case;** * Select the starting slot for k1 computing with slot number = numOfPrecedingSlots – k1min. where numOfPrecedingSlots indicates number of slots before the first non-UL slot which is behind the first UL slot from the beginning of a radio frame, a muted UL slot is also considered as a non-UL slot; * k1 = k1min whenever a PUCCH token is available for HARQ feedback in slot N+k1min; * If PUCCH token in slot N+k1min is exhausted, then k1 shall be selected that N+k1 corresponds to the closest slot with PUCCH token available for HARQ feedback; * If no PUCCH token can be found for HARQ feedback, then k1 shall be set such that N+k1 corresponds to the last slot with exhausted PUCCH tokens; such PDSCH slot to PUCCH slot mapping is called “PUCCH token overbooking”; * In case of FR1 TDD cell compatible with tdLte Cfg0 frame structure,there shall be no PUCCH tokens for HARQ feedback in UL slots N mod 10= 5 and N mod 10=9 while N = [0,1,..,19] [CB009422-E] when PS BWP is disabled. If PS BWP is enabled, UL slots N mod 10= 8 and N mod 10=9 while N = [0,1,..,19] shall have no PUCCH tokens for HARQ feedback. [End CB009422-E] The k1 value computation method can be applied to all currently supported TDD frame structures, including: * FR1 TDD TD-LTE Cfg2 aligned frame structure without NR frame shift (tdLte, refer to 5G_UP_66_replaced([11347021](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJMxvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))); * FR1 TDD TD-LTE Cfg2 aligned frame structure with 3ms NR frame shift (tdLte with 3ms shift, refer to 5G_UP_68_replaced([11347030](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJMyPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))); * FR1 TDD periodic 5-slot frame structure (semi-static 4:1, refer to 5G_UP_58_replaced([11346939](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubIlt_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))); * FR1 TDD Bi-periodic 5-slot frame structure (semi-static 7:3, refer to 5G_UP_60_replaced([11346955](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubIluvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))); * FR1 TDD periodic 10-slot frame structure (semi-static 6:4, refer to 5G_UP_4446_replaced([11346967](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubIlvPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))); * Mixture of tdLte, semi-static 4:1, semi-static 7:3 and semi-static 6:4 for CA configurations with TDD PCell; * 5G FR1 TDD frame structure compatible with TD LTE configuration 0 (tdLte, refer to 5G_UP_13579_replaced([11347038](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJMyvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) If NRCELL.actPowerSavingBWP is True and if number of different k1 values in k1 set is less than 8, then k1 = 2 shall be added [CB009422-E] (for tdLte config0 frame structure k1 = 4 shall be added)[End CB009422-E] to the k1 set in order to be used as described in 5G_UP_ALG_1857_replaced([11111082](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpKagvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), and shall be added to the dlDataToUlAck array sent at cell setup response. Notes: 1\. The figure below shows an example for k1 computing: [image-0][image_desc]The image depicts a timing diagram illustrating the scheduling of PDSCH (Physical Downlink Shared Channel) in a 5G network, considering TDD (Time Division Duplex) and FDD (Frequency Division Duplex) configurations, along with specific parameters like K1min, token, and PRACH configuration. **Key Information and Components:** * **Title/Context:** "5GC001116 w/o 3ms shift, w/ K1min = 4 and token = 5, PDSCH can not be scheduled in SSB slot, and PRACH config index is 97" * This sets the scenario: a specific 5G configuration (5GC001116) without a 3ms shift, with K1min set to 4, token set to 5, and a PRACH configuration index of 97. It also states a constraint: PDSCH cannot be scheduled in SSB (Synchronization Signal Block) slots. * **Legend:** * SSB: Yellow * PRACH: Purple * DL (Downlink): Blue * UL (Uplink): Green * S (Special Slot): Gray * **K1 Set:** `{4,5,6,7,8,9,11,12}` \- This is the set of usable K1 values. * **TDDNg w/o shift Diagram:** * This section shows a timeline of slots for a TDD network without a shift. * Slots are numbered from 0 to 39. * Different colors represent different slot types: * Yellow: SSB * Purple: PRACH * Blue: DL * Green: UL * Gray: S (Special Slot) * Above the TDD timeline, there are numbers (e.g., 12, 11, 0, 9, 8, 7, 7, 6, 5, 4) and curved arrows. These numbers likely represent K1 values associated with specific slots, and the arrows indicate relationships or dependencies between slots. The K1 values shown above the slots are: `12 11 0 9 8 7 7 6 5 4 12 11 0 9 8 7 7 6 5 4 12 11 0 9 8 7 7 6 5 4 12 11 0 9 8 7 7 6 5 4`. * Dashed green arrows point from the top K1 numbers down to specific slots in the TDD timeline, indicating which K1 value is associated with which slot. * **FDDNg Diagram:** * This section shows a timeline of slots for an FDD network. * Slots are numbered from 0 to 19. * All slots are depicted as blue, indicating DL. * Below the FDD timeline, a sequence of numbers is shown: `11 9 7 6 4 11 9 7 6 4 11 9 7 6 4 11 9 7 6 4`. These are likely K1 values associated with the FDD slots. * A purple arrow points from the left, indicating the start of a range or a specific configuration. * The text "range of sCellDIDataToUIAckPattern" is associated with this FDD diagram. * **Textual Summaries:** * **SCell K1 table:** `{11,9,7,6,4,11,9,7,6, 4,11,9,7,6, 4,11,9,7,6,4}` \- This appears to be a repeating pattern of K1 values for SCells. * **K1: TDD K1 usable values:** `{4,5,6,7,8,12}` \- Usable K1 values specifically for TDD. * **FDD Scell K1 usable values:** `{4,6,7,9,11}` \- Usable K1 values for FDD SCells. * **TDD PCell/TDD SCell/FDD SCell/non-CA UE set of K1 values:** `{4,5,6,7,8,9,11,12}` \- This is the overall set of K1 values applicable to various UE types and cell configurations, which matches the "K1 set" mentioned earlier.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5665.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] File name: Figure of new K1 table.pptx Link: 2\. Purpose of this requirement are: * Support FR1 TDD + FDD CA with TDD cell acting as PCell; * Support different SSB/TRS configuration between serving cells; As a result of current requirement, number of PDSCH slots per bundle window is no more than 3 in a FDD SCell; number PDSCH slots in per bundle window is no more than 5 in a FR1 TDD SCell; 3\. Though k1 is computed for UL slots with odd slot number and special slot (which can’t be used for PDSCH transmission according to 5G_UP_ALG_2375_replaced([11115218](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpr-_PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in PCell, PDSCH can’t be scheduled in those slots in PCell; 4\. For all currently supported TDD frame structures except tdLte configuration 0 **, k1min = 4 and k1max = 12; In case some UL slots are muted, k1 can reach 13.** 5\. The design principle of this requirement maybe unsuitable for later CA feature or CA combinations, such as different TDD frame structure configuration, new TDD/FDD SCS; 6\. If 5GC002516 is enabled without bilateral agreement between operators (NRBTS/NRCELL.actInfTddFrameMuting = True AND NRBTS/NRCELL.infTddFrameStructure/infTddFrameMutingBiAgreement = False), no PUCCH token shall be assigned to UL slots with muted OFDM symbols, and no DL slots shall be associated with such UL slots. 7\. For tdLte configuration 0 frame structure, K1min=6 and K1max = [Before CB009422-E] 11[CB009422-E] 12 (see [12705821](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_SJYtYmFoEfCLrcnPCspVyQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9 "https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/bi_sjytymfoefclrcnpcspvyq?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9") for details)[End CB009422-E];

**3.5.12.0-5.0-3**  (ID: `11116208`)

Scheduling timings k1 value computation for SCells with TDD PCell

---

**3.5.12.0-5.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11116215`)

Scheduling timings For UEs configured with TDD PCell, the k1 table used in SCell shall be computed as follows:

  * For FDD SCell, select k1 values of all slots with odd slot number (2 _N + 1) from k1 set of PCell, N∈ [ ceiling( ([Before CB008053-C] Pattern1Length [CB008053-C] numOfPrecedingSlots [End CB008053-C] – k1min – 1)/2), rrmSlotPatternLength/2 – 1 + ceiling( ([Before CB008053-C] Pattern1Length [CB008053-C] numOfPrecedingSlots [End CB008053-C] – k1min – 1) /2)], that is, k1 of slot N in FDD SCell = k1 of slot (2_ N + 1) in TDD PCell;
  * For TDD SCell, k1 of slot N in TDD SCell = k1 of slot N in TDD PCell, N ∈ [Before CB008053-C] [ceiling((Pattern1Length – k1min – 1)/2), rrmSlotPatternLength/2 – 1 + ceiling((Pattern1Length – k1min – 1)/2)] [CB008053-C] [numOfPrecedingSlots – k1min, rrmSlotPatternLength -1 + numOfPrecedingSlots – k1min] [End CB008053-C];



Where:

  * rrmSlotPatternlength is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2296_replaced([11114662](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpmfb_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));



[CB008053-C]

  * numOfPrecedingSlots is the number of slots before the first non-UL slot which is behind the first UL slot from the beginning of a radio frame, a muted UL slot is also considered as a non-UL slot;



[End CB008053-C]

---

**3.5.12.0-5.0-4**  (ID: `11116217`)

Scheduling timings k1 value computation on specific slot for CA

---

**3.5.12.0-5.0-4.0-2**  (ID: `11116224`)

Scheduling timings k1 set actually used for scheduling is the extened k1 set which is determined as follows:

  * k1 set { k1(0), k1(1), …, k1(I – 1)}: set of k1 values without specail dealing of slot with slot number mod 10 = 8;
  * k1’ set {k11(0), k1’(1), …, k1’(I – 1)}: set of k1 values with special handling of slot with slot number mod 10 = 8, that is, k1 value of slot with slot number mod 10 = 8 is always computed;



where I = slotPatternLength; Rationale: in case of CA, PDSCH schedulable status both in PCell and in SCells shall be taken into consideration, and k1 value calculation carried out in cell setup procedure needs to be extended for both local PCell usage and potential SCell usage: as long as a slot is PDSCH schedulable for either PCell or SCell which could be a candidate secondary cell of PCell, k1 value(> 0) shall be calculated for it.

Extended k1 values are generated as follows:

BEGIN:

LOOP (all slots within slotPatternLength), for each slot n

K1(n) = K1’(n) = 0

IF (slot n is a DL slot or a special slot that can be used for PDSCH transmission)

calculate k1(n) with the “basic k1 computation method when long PUCCH is enabled”

set k1’(n) = k1(n)

ENDIF

IF (slot n is a UL slot or a special slot that can not be used for PDSCH transmission)

IF (n mod 2 == 1) // odd slot

calculate k1(n) as per 5G_UP_ALG_14983_replaced([11116186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp1v-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

set k1’(n) = k1(n)

ENDIF

IF ( [CB009422-C-UPD001]frame structure is not tdLte config 0 && [End CB009422-C-UPD001]n mod 10 == 8) // special dealing of Slot N mod 10 = 8

calculate k1’(n) as per 5G_UP_ALG_14983_replaced([11116186](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp1v-fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ));

ENDIF

ENDIF

END LOOP

IF (the total number of different K1 values of k1 set excluding value 0 is less than 8)

LOOP (all slots within slotPatternLength), for each slot n

replace k1(n) with the value of k1’(n)

ENDLOOP

ENDIF

END

Finally, K1 value of k1 set will be taken into usage as extended K1 value.

---

**3.5.12.0-5.0-5**  (ID: `11116229`)

Scheduling timings k1 dropping in case more than 8 k1 values are found

---

**3.5.12.0-5.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11116242`)

11116242: Scheduling timings 11116242: Scheduling timings In case some UL slots are muted, number of k1 found may exceed 8, while more than 8 k1 values are not allowed by 3GPP. To conform to 3GPP specification, if number of k1 found is more than 8, some k1 values with least applicable frame structures shall be dropped. According to the analysis on the interfered frame structure currently supported, in the worst case up to 9 k1 values can be found, that is, up to 1 k1 value shall be dropped in the worst case. The applicable frame structure for a specific k1 means the k1 value will be used for that frame structure if that frame structure is adopted in a CA configuration with the interfered frame structure. For example in the figure below, the interfered frame structure is tdLte without shift interfered by semi-static4:1, number of applicable frame structures for k1 = 5 is 2, which means k1 =5 will be used for 2 frame structures, which are: tdLte without shift, FDD; while k1 = 6 has 3 applicable frame structures, which are: tdLte without shift interfered by semi-static4:1, tdLte without shift, and semi-static 4:1. **[Before CB008053-C-UPD001]** [image-0][image_desc]The image displays a diagram illustrating the interference between a Time Division Long Term Evolution (tdLTE) system and a semi-static system. The top row represents the interfering semi-static system with a pattern of blue, yellow, and red blocks, indicating different time slots. Below this, several rows depict the interfered PCell (tdLTE without shift) and various candidate SCell frame structures (CSFS). Each row is segmented into blocks, with numbers above some blocks indicating specific values. Green arrows connect certain blocks, suggesting relationships or dependencies. The diagram also includes text labels and speech bubbles that provide explanations and classifications for the different frame structures. A table at the bottom summarizes the number of applicable frame structures for different K1 values. The text in the image is as follows: tdLte without shift interfered by semi-static 4:1, patternOffset = 0 5 10 15 Interfering: semi- static 4:1 13 13 12 8 7 5 13 12 11 7 13 12 11 13 12 11 13 11 13 12 K1 13 12 11 9876

# of applicable frame structures 6

K1 | 13 | 12 | 11 | 9 | 8 | 7 | 6 | 5 | 4  
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---  
# of applicable frame structures | 6 | 5 | 4 | 3 | 5 | 6 | 3 | 2 | 5

12562165: Scheduling timings 12562165: Scheduling timings In case PCell carrier are interfered and some UL slots are muted, maximum HARQ-AN bits per bundle window for CA UEs can be calculated as follows: query the table below with the frame structure of PCell frame structure, interfering frame structure, and frame structure of SCell, and sum up the HARQ-AN bits for each serving cell (including the PCell) found in the table; if there are more than 1 bundle windows in the table for the CA combination, HARQ-AN bits for each bundle window shall be calculated and then select the largest one as the maximum HARQ-AN bits for specific CA combination in a specific interfered case. N_HAN_Bundle[i] = N_HAN_PCell[i] + [image-0][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is a summation, denoted by the Greek letter sigma ($\Sigma$), with a subscript 'j'. Following the summation symbol, there is a term "N_HAN_SCell[i][j]". The entire expression is enclosed in square brackets.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5675.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] N_HAN_max = MAX{N_HAN_bundle[i]} Where: * N_HAN_Bundle[i]: number of HARQ-AN bits in bundle window [i]; * i: index of bundle window; * N_HAN_PCell[i]: number of HARQ-AN bits for PCell in bundle window [i]; * N_HAN_SCell[i][j]: number of HARQ-AN bits for the j-th SCell in bundle window [i]; * j: index of SCell; * N_HAN_max: maximum HARQ-AN bits per bundle window for specific CA combination in
