2[/docx_desc][docx_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/attachments/m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file5675_4.docx[/docx_path] [Before CB014093-E] these 2 tables [CB014093-E] this table [End CB014093-E] is derived from the interference case analysis slides: File name: k1_num_with_UL_muting_new.pptx Notes: 1\. The calculation result is used by C-Plane to decide whether some of the SCell candidates sent by CP-CU can be dropped to avoid maximum HARQ-AN bits per bundle window exceeding maximum allowed HARQ-AN bits on PUCCH (maxHarqAn); When a new frame structure is introduced or a new function that will update the HARQ-AN per bundle window is introduced, the tables above shall be updated and C-Plane shall be notified; 2\. Each row in the tables represents a bundle window of a specific PCell frame structure in a specific interfering case; number in the table cell indicates the number of HARQ-AN bits for a serving cell of specific frame structure with a specific PCell frame structure in a specific interfering case (a specific interfering frame structure); 3\. Cells with red back ground color indicates bundle window [1] has more HARQ-AN bits than bundle window2; while cells with blue background color indicates bundle window[2] has more HARQ-AN bits than bundle window[1]; 4\. For specific interfering case, if there are 2 bundle windows, but bundle window[x] always has more HARQ-AN bits than bundle window[y] for any serving cell frame structure, then only HARQ-AN bits of bundle window[x] is shown in the tables, HARQ-AN bits for bundle window[y] are not shown;

---

### 3.5.12.0-6 Scheduling timings K2 value shall be computed for each slot in the pattern configured with PUSCH in the respective slot used for scheduling. (ID: 11116264)

**3.5.12.0-6.0-10**  (ID: `13432635`)

Scheduling timings PUSCH timing K2 from PDCCH in slot N-K2 shall be determined such that:

\- On an FDD carrier shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false for the purpose of K2 computation, 5G-L2-PS shall assume one free PDCCH token in every DL slot which is available for 5G assuming the maximum DL sharing pattern rrmFddMaxDlShareBitmap (see 5G_UP_ALG_2304_replaced([11171916](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuIaFvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), excluding DL slots with SS/PBCH or TRS only configuration.

_[Note] This ensures that K2 values are available for any configured DL sharing pattern._

\- K2min value is applied whenever a PDCCH token in slot N-K2min is available for PUSCH scheduling.

In the case of a carrier shared with LTE and when nrLteDssMode **> 1 and when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false, the k2 value applied for a PDCCH token shall be selected according to the k2 list pattern computed in 5G_UP_ALG_5184_replaced([11172479](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuMrhfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) instead. The first PDCCH token shall use the first value of the k2 list for the corresponding slot and the consecutive tokens shall use the next values in the list in the same fashion.**

Note: Per 3GPP constraints, max one PDCCH token for UL shall be granted per UE for a DL slot.

Note: For 10MHz, the number of PDCCH token available for a slot may differ depending on the aggregation level of the UEs being scheduled. An AL4 UE shall consume twice the number of PDCCH token required for an AL2 UE.

\- If no PDCCH token is available for PUSCH scheduling in slot N-K2min, then closest PDCCH token available for PUSCH scheduling shall be selected from slot N-K2 with K2 in _the configured set of values._

\- If no PDCCH token available for PUSCH scheduling can be found, then PUSCH shall not be used. This case is not expected and shall be avoided by pattern design.

On an FDD carrier shared with LTE when NRCELL.NRDSSLTE.actAsymmetricDss=false, the number of PDCCH tokens is limited by the number of DL slots available for 5G in the DL sharing pattern. There are not enough PDCCH tokens for all UL slots configured with PUSCH. UL slots without PDCCH token shall be skipped in UL scheduling. 

Selected PDCCH token is assigned to the UL TD scheduler.

In the case of semiStatic frame structure in FR2:

  * By default rrmK2min = 4 shall be used to satisfy UE PUSCH preparation time of 36 symbols for µ=3 (see 38.214 section 6.4).
  * For 4-1 frame structure with 10 GP symbols, rrmK2min = 6 shall be used (rationale: to force UL grants scheduling in special slots, which are not used for PDSCH transmission).



In the case of semiStatic frame structure in FR1, rrmK2min = 2 to satisfy UE PUSCH preparation time of 12 symbols for µ=1 given that GP symbols shall be excluded from the computation. 

In the case of FR1 TDD with long PUCCH configured and distributed PUCCH allocation algorithm is activated,

if NRBTS/NRCELL/increasePUSCHPrepTimeULCA is TRUE

rrmK2min = 3

else //FALSE

rrmK2min = 2

to satisfy UE PUSCH preparation time of 12 symbols for µ=1 given that GP symbols shall be excluded from the computation for UL CA support.

**Note:**

  * With FR1 UL CA support, both PCell and SCell may need to support long PUCCH;

  * With FR1 UL CA support, UE will expect PUSCH preparation time shall be matched for all PUSCH scheduling in response to any DCI format scheduling an overlapping PUSCH. Refer to 9.2.5 of TS38.213.




In FDD, k2min = max( rdk2min; rrmK2min). In FDD with short PUCCH case, rrmK2min = 1 to satisfy UE PUSCH preparation time of 10 symbols for µ=0 (see 38.214 section 6.4). Note, content is not changed by 885-A for short PUCCH, only rephrased.

In FDD with long PUCCH case, rrmK2min = 2 to satisfy UE PUSCH preparation procedure time increased to more than 12 symbols if UCI is multiplexed in PUSCH (considering timing advance too), see also 3GPP 38.213 ch. 9.2.5.

In Fdd with long prach preamble format1, rrmk2Min = 2 to satisfy UE PUSCH preparation time.

In FDD with dlMimoMode is not CL32x4 or FR1 TDD with actCellCsiRsImTddNonBf is true, or **FR1 TDD with actCellCsirsTddOnSsbBf is true and number of activated SS/PBCH (nActivatedSsPbch, see 5G_UP_82_replaced([11347116](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubJz2PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is 2, when Aperiodic CSI feature is activated at cell level (via actAperCsiCell in PsCell_CellSetupReq):**

 rrmK2min = max (rrmK2min, k2minCsi)**,**

 where:

o rrmK2min as computed above

o k2minCsi as computed in 5G_UP_ALG_3550_replaced([11159568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktKJv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). For FR1 TDD case, k2minCsi is 3 here. According to PDL rules for (NRCELL- actCellCsiRsImTddNonBf, NRCELL-actCellCsirsTddOnSsbBf, NRCELL-dlMimoMode) and condition specified here, 8 port is not support here. Finally k2minCsi is 3 based on conclusion from 5G_UP_ALG_3550_replaced([11159568](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktKJv_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))/5G_UP_ALG_3547_replaced([11159543](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktKJvfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note: GP symbols shall be excluded when checking UE PUSCH preparation time considering that K2 is cell specific and that TA may be consuming guard period entirely.

If NIDD parameter NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode is 'partial' **and TDD FR1 cell and NRCELL-tddFrameStructure.frameStructureType is equal to 'tdLte' and TD LTE config frame pattern is not Config0, then rrmK2min=3**.****

If the frame strcuture type is tdLte, and TD LTE config frame pattern is Config0, then rrmK2min=4.

If (NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/actRimPdcchMuting is not equal to 0) and (PDCCH muting is required (see [13242857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_2wyukMKhEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ) [Before CB015505-A] and [13242859](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/TX_jv4M8MKiEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ) [End CB015505-A])),

  * if (NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.tddFrameStructureType = semiStatic and MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.ulDlDataSlotRatio = 1/4) AND (slot N is a DL slot) AND (slot N+k2Rim is the UL slot after a special slot), then 



> PUSCH on slot N+k2Rim shall be scheduled from slot N

\- if (NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.tddFrameStructureType = semiStatic and MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.ulDlDataSlotRatio = 3/7),

> if (slot N is a DL slot) AND (slot N+1+k2Rim is the second UL slot after a special slot), then
>
>> PUSCH on slot N+k2Rim shall be scheduled from slot N
>> 
>> PUSCH on slot N+1+k2Rim shall be scheduled from slot N+1
> 
> if (slot N is a DL slot) AND (slot N+1+k2Rim is the DL slot after an UL slot), then
>
>> PUSCH on slot N+k2Rim shall be scheduled from slot N

\- if (NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.tddFrameStructureType = tdLte) AND (slot N is a DL slot) AND (slot N+1+k2Rim is the second UL slot after a special slot), then

> PUSCH on slot N+k2Rim shall be scheduled from slot N
> 
> PUSCH on slot N+1+k2Rim shall be scheduled from slot N+1

Else,

\- k2Rim = none.

End If 

If Cancel DL mitigation (5G_UP_ALG_25970_replaced([11163548](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktaBV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) happens, k2Rim = none.

If k2Rim is none

\- k2Cell = rrmK2Min

Else,

\- k2Cell = max(rrmK2Min, k2Rim)

End If

If gNB is using 0.75ms timing pattern for the cells of a L2 subpool (5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then k2Cell = max of k2Cell of two cells in same L2Subpool (5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 Rationale: rrmK2min is the minimum K2 for PUSCH determined from the static configuration for a cell on cell setup. Without RIM feature, rrmK2min is the K2 used for PUSCH. With RIM feature, k2Cell = max(rrmK2Min, k2Rim) is the K2 used for PUSCH. After introduction of 0.75ms timing pattern, K2 for cells in a L2 subpool with 0.75ms timing pattern need be same (5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

k2Cell is K2 for PUSCH for the cell. 

Refer 5G_UP_ALG_30984_replaced([11116295](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-EPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for K2 value calculation part2**.**

---

### 3.5.12.0-7 Scheduling timings K2 value calculation part2 (ID: 11116295)

**3.5.12.0-7.0-3**  (ID: `13432636`)

Scheduling timings **Continue K2 calculation from 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

1> If (k2Rim is greater than rrmK2min) AND (NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/actRimPdcchMuting is not equal to 0) AND (PDCCH muting is required (see [13242857](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_2uvgIMKhEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ) [Before CB015505-A] and [13242859](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_jt_WkMKiEfCnuMJFWtMTpQ) [End CB015505-A])),

> > if (NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.tddFrameStructureType = semiStatic and MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.ulDlDataSlotRatio = 1/4) AND (slot N is a DL slot) AND (slot N+k2Rim is the UL slot after a special slot), then
>>
>>> PUSCH on slot N+k2Rim shall be scheduled from slot N
>> 
>> if (NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.tddFrameStructureType = semiStatic and MRBTS/NRBTS/NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.ulDlDataSlotRatio = 3/7),
>>
>>> if (slot N is a DL slot) AND (slot N+1+k2Rim is the second UL slot after a special slot), then
>>>
>>>> PUSCH on slot N+k2Rim shall be scheduled from slot N
>>>> 
>>>> PUSCH on slot N+1+k2Rim shall be scheduled from slot N+1
>>> 
>>> if (slot N is a DL slot) AND (slot N+1+k2Rim is the DL slot after an UL slot), then
>>>
>>>> PUSCH on slot N+k2Rim shall be scheduled from slot N
>> 
>> if (NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.tddFrameStructureType = tdLte) AND (slot N is a DL slot) AND (slot N+1+k2Rim is the second UL slot after a special slot), then
>>
>>> PUSCH on slot N+k2Rim shall be scheduled from slot N
>>> 
>>> PUSCH on slot N+1+k2Rim shall be scheduled from slot N+1

1> Else,

\- k2Rim = none.

1> End If 

If Cancel DL mitigation (5G_UP_ALG_25970_replaced([11163548](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktaBV_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) happens, k2Rim = none.

If k2Rim is none

\- k2Cell = rrmK2Min

Else,

\- k2Cell = max(rrmK2Min, k2Rim)

End If

If gNB is using 0.75ms timing pattern for the cells of a L2 subpool (5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), then k2Cell = max of k2Cell of two cells in same L2Subpool (5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

 Rationale: rrmK2min is the minimum K2 for PUSCH determined from the static configuration for a cell on cell setup. Without RIM feature, rrmK2min is the K2 used for PUSCH. With RIM feature, k2Cell = max(rrmK2Min, k2Rim) is the K2 used for PUSCH. After introduction of 0.75ms timing pattern, K2 for cells in a L2 subpool with 0.75ms timing pattern need be same (5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))).

If gNB is using single K2 mode (5G_UP_ALG_30394_replaced([11116305](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))),**k2Cell is K2 for PUSCH for the cell.**

If gNB is using multiple K2 mode (5G_UP_ALG_30394_replaced([11116305](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB shall determine the K2 for PUSCH according to 5G_UP_13617_replaced([11348263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubTk3vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

---

### 3.5.12.0-8 Scheduling timings K2 mode selection (ID: 11116302)

**3.5.12.0-8.0-1**  (ID: `11116305`)

Scheduling timings K2 mode selection

---

**3.5.12.0-8.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11116307`)

Scheduling timings [CB013305-C, CB013305-D]

When NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity is false, gNB shall use single K2 mode.

When NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity is true,

 gNB shall determine K2 mode based on its condition as 5G_UP_ALG_30906_replaced([11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB013305-E, CB013305-F]

 If NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode is 'full', gNB shall also determine K2 mode according to timing pattern interworking in a L2 subpool as 5G_UP_ALG_30903_replaced([11116317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-GPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CB013305-E, CB013305-F]

Refer 5G_UP_13621_replaced([11348255](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubTk3PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for K2 mode definition.

[End CB013305-C, CB013305-D]

---

**3.5.12.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11116311`)

Scheduling timings K2 mode determined by cell

---

**3.5.12.0-8.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `13246399`)

Scheduling timings When NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity is true:

 If NIDD paramter NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode is 'disabled' or 'full', 

o if rrmK2min=2,

 gNB shall initiate to use single K2 mode (K2=2).

 **If k2Rim is none,** gNB shall switch between single K2 mode and multiple K2 mode K2=4,3,2 according to 5G_UP_ALG_30707_replaced([11169564](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt4icfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_30809_replaced([11169581](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt4idfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).****

 If k2Rim is 3, gNB shall switch between single K2 mode and multiple K2 mode K2=4,3,2 according to 5G_UP_ALG_30707_replaced([11169564](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt4icfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_30809_replaced([11169581](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt4idfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). 

 [CB013330-B-UPD002] If k2Rim is not (none or 3), gNB shall use single K2 mode (K2 = k2Rim).[End CB013330-B-UPD002]

[Before CB013330-B-UPD002]

 When gNB is using single K2 mode, refer 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for K2Rim change.

[End CB013330-B-UPD002]

 When gNB is using multiple K2 mode,

 If k2Rim is changed from none to 3, **gNB shall switch from multiple K2 mode K2=4,3,2 to multiple K2 mode 4,3.**

 If k2Rim is changed from 3 to none, **gNB shall switch from multiple K2 mode K2=4,3 to multiple K2 mode 4,3,2.**

[CB013330-B-UPD002]

 If k2Rim is changed from (none or 3) to other values, gNB shall switch to single K2 mode with K2=k2Rim.

[End CB013330-B-UPD002]

o if rrmK2min=3,

 gNB shall initiate to use single K2 mode (K2=3).

 [CB013330-B]If k2Rim is not (none or 3), gNB shall use single K2 mode (K2=k2Rim). [End CB013330-B]

 [CB013330-B-UPD002]If k2Rim is (none or 3), [End CB013330-B-UPD002]gNB shall switch between single K2 mode and multiple K2 mode K2=4,3 according to 5G_UP_ALG_30707_replaced([11169564](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt4icfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_30809_replaced([11169581](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt4idfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[CB013330-B-UPD002]

 when gNB is using multiple K2 mode,

 If k2Rim is changed from (none or 3) to other values, gNB shall switch to single K2 mode with K2=k2Rim.

[CB013330-B-UPD002]

Refer 5G_UP_13621_replaced([11348255](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubTk3PvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for K2 mode definition.

Refer 5G_UP_13617_replaced([11348263](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ubTk3vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for multiple K2 mode details.

Refer 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for K2 for PUSCH under single K2 mode and k2Rim.

---

**3.5.12.0-8.0-3**  (ID: `11116317`)

Scheduling timings K2 mode and timing pattern interworking

---

**3.5.12.0-8.0-3.0-3**  (ID: `13455953`)

Scheduling timings When 2 cells in a L2 subpool[CB013330-B-UPD002] and k2Rim is (none or 3)[End CB013330-B-UPD002],

· If one cell needs to use multiple K2 mode as 5G_UP_ALG_30906_replaced([11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) or need to use 0.75ms timing pattern due to overload control as 5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), then

o It shall use multiple K2 mode with 0.75ms timing pattern,

o If buddy cell is using single K2 mode as 5G_UP_ALG_30906_replaced([11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), buddy cell shall switch to multiple K2 mode with 0.75ms timing pattern and shall use the K2 set corresponding to multiple K2 mode as 5G_UP_ALG_30906_replaced([11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Note: Buddy cell is another cell in the same L2 subpool.

o If the trigger is 5G_UP_ALG_30906_replaced([11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and both cells are using single K2 mode, the cell with trigger 5G_UP_ALG_30906_replaced([11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) shall first switch to use multiple K2 mode with 0.5ms 2cores 2cells timing pattern (5G_UP_14215_replaced([11388887](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufMmWfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), and then both cells in the L2 subpool switch to use multiple K2 mode with 0.75ms timing pattern in next PDCCH slot pattern. 

· If both cells in the L2 subpool do not need to use multiple K2 mode as 5G_UP_ALG_30906_replaced([11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and do not need to use 0.75ms timing pattern due to overload control as 5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) , then both cells in the L2 subpool shall use single K2 mode with 0.5ms timing pattern. gNB shall trigger both cells switching to single K2 mode with 0.5ms timing pattern when all followings are satisfied,

o A cell is in the condition of: not need to use multiple K2 mode as 5G_UP_ALG_30906_replaced([11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and not need to use 0.75ms timing pattern due to overload control as 5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)),

o Buddy cell is changed to: not need to use multiple K2 mode as 5G_UP_ALG_30906_replaced([11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and not need to used 0.75ms timing pattern due to overload control as 5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

o If gNB miss the switching occasion (5G_UP_ALG_30062_replaced([11116365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp3lJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) in current PDCCH slot pattern, gNB shall trigger switching in next PDCCH slot pattern. 

· If buddy cell is deleted, the remaining cell shall use 0.5ms timing pattern as 5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and re-evaluate K2 mode as 5G_UP_ALG_30906_replaced([11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

When 1 cell in a L2 subpool[CB013330-B-UPD002] and k2Rim is (none or 3)[End CB013330-B-UPD002],

  * If the cell needs to use multiple K2 mode as [11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) or needs to use 0.75ms timing pattern due to overload control as [11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), then
  * It shall use multiple K2 mode with 0.75ms timing pattern (check state transition diagrams ([11116490](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp4zQ_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9) and [11116499](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp4zRfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)) for more details),
  * If the cell in the L2 subpool does not need to use multiple K2 mode as [11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and does not need to use 0.75ms timing pattern due to overload control as [11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ), then cell shall use single K2 mode with 0.5ms timing pattern. gNB-DU shall trigger cell switching to single K2 mode with 0.5ms timing pattern when all following conditions are satisfied:
  * A cell is in the condition of: not need to use multiple K2 mode as [11116311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ) and not need to use 0.75ms timing pattern due to overload control as [11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ),
  * If gNB misses the switching occasion ([11116365](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp3lJvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) in current PDCCH slot pattern, gNB shall trigger switching in next PDCCH slot pattern.
  * If buddy cell is added, then the newly added cell in L2 subpool shall be created in 0.5 ms, single K2 mode state:
  * If the current cell is in 0.5ms, multiple K2 mode, then after 2 SFNs, both the cells shall move to 0.75ms, multiple K2 mode state.
  * If the current cell is in 0.75ms, multiple K2 mode, then after 2 SFNs, buddy cell shall move to 0.75ms, multiple K2 mode state.



​​​​​​​[CB013330-B-UPD002]When k2Rim is not (none or 3), then no matter if 1 or 2 cells are in the L2 subpool, cell(s) shall be forced to use 0.5ms timing pattern.[End CB013330-B-UPD002]

---

### 3.5.12.0-9 Scheduling timings L2 CPU Processing Timing Pattern (ID: 11116324)

**3.5.12.0-9.0-1**  (ID: `11116328`)

Scheduling timings Processing Timing Pattern Control

---

**3.5.12.0-9.0-1.0-4**  (ID: `13428592`)

Scheduling timings When NIDD parameter NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode is 'partial' or 'full' [CB013330-B-UPD002] AND k2Rim is (none or 3)[End CB013330-B-UPD002],

 gNB shall support 0.75ms timing pattern for K2=2 defined in 5G_UP_13435_replaced([11388784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufL_T_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode is 'full'. 

 gNB shall support 0.75ms timing pattern for K2=3 defined in 5G_UP_13436_replaced([11388805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufMmUfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 gNB shall support 0.75ms timing pattern according to HW condition as 5G_UP_13471_replaced([11388760](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufL_S_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 gNB shall support 0.75ms timing pattern for multiple K2 mode defined in [12636887: 0.75ms (2 cores 1 cell or 1 core 1 cell) timing pattern for Multiple K2 Mode TDD FR1](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI__P625UBTEfCal_QUJn4xrg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), [12636905: Cases of timing pattern switch for 0.75ms (2 cores 1 cell or 1 core 1 cell) with multiple K2 mode](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tpmZEkBWEfCal_QUJn4xrg?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9), 5G_UP_13619_replaced([11388869](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufMmV_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_13695_replaced([11388904](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufMmW_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) if NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode is 'full' and NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity is true. 

Definitions:

 k2Cell **is defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

Basic Rules:

 0.75ms timing pattern is applied at L2 subpool granularity.

 If gNB is using 0.75ms timing pattern for the cells of a L2 subpool (2 Core 2 Cells case), then k2Cell = max of k2Cell of two cells in same L2Subpool. 

 When 0.75ms timing pattern is used, all the cell(s) in the L2 subpool shall use 0.75ms timing pattern with K2 = k2Cell.

Eligible Condition:

A cell is applicable to use 0.75ms timing pattern, when:

 The cell is TDD FR1 and

 NRCELL-tddFrameStructure.frameStructureType is equal to 'tdLte'

Apply 0.75ms Timing Pattern:

When NIDD parameter NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode is 'partial' and rdFixBISlotTimings (5G_UP_ALG_29581_replaced([11118254](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqBWKPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is 1 and the cell is applicable to use 0.75ms timing pattern:

 gNB shall use 0.75ms timing pattern for K2= k2Cell for the cell.

Dynamic switch based on overload control and multiple K2 mode:

When NIDD parameter NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode is 'partial' or 'full', **:**

 If NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity is false**,**

o If the cells in a subpool are applicable to use 0.75ms timing pattern, gNB shall support dynamic switching timing pattern between 0.75ms and 0.5ms (2 cores 2 cells) as 5G_UP_ALG_29952_replaced([11116341](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-H_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the cells with K2 = k2Cell .

o If the number of cells in a L2 subpool is changed from 1 to 2 and the cells become applicable to use 0.75ms timing pattern, gNB shall support dynamic switching timing pattern as 5G_UP_ALG_29952_replaced([11116341](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-H_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for the cells with K2 = k2Cell.

 If NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity is true**, refer 5G_UP_ALG_30903_replaced([11116317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-GPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

Pattern Switch due to change the number of cells of a L2 subpool:

When gNB is using 0.75ms timing pattern and **NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity is false for a L2 subpool:**

 If k2Cell is changed between 2 and 3, gNB shall switch 0.75ms timing pattern for K2 from old k2Cell to new k2Cell for the cell(s) in the L2 subpool.

o The cell or its buddy cell RIM DL mitigation trigger may change k2Cell from 2 to 3. The cell or its buddy cell Cancel DL mitigation may change k2Cell from 3 to 2. Refer 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for K2 change results from RIM event.

 If the number of cells in a L2 subpool is changed from 2 to 1, gNB shall switch to 0.5ms (2 cores 1 cell) timing pattern with K2 = k2Cell **of the cell.**

 If the number of cells in a L2 subpool is changed from 1 to 2, 2 cells work in 0.5ms (2 cores 2 cells) timing pattern firstly, then follow dynamic switching timing pattern as in 5G_UP_ALG_29952_replaced([11116341](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-H_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

If NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity is true and the number of cells in a L2 subpool is changed, gNB shall do as 5G_UP_ALG_30903_replaced([11116317](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-GPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note1: Refer 5G_UP_3881_replaced([11372360](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ud8pLfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for L2 subpool concept. Cells in a L2 subpool are with the same TDD frame structure, 5G_UP_10383_replaced([11370063](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_udtYn_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_13137_replaced([11388486](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufKxIPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

Note2: Refer 5G_UP_13435_replaced([11388784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufL_T_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), **5G_UP_13436_replaced([11388805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufMmUfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for 0.5ms (2 cores 2 cells) timing pattern.**

Note3: Refer 5G_UP_13521_replaced([11388826](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufMmU_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), **5G_UP_13525_replaced([11388847](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufMmVfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for 0.5ms (2 cores 1 cell) timing pattern.**

---

**3.5.12.0-9.0-2**  (ID: `11116341`)

Scheduling timings Dynamic Switch Based on overload control

---

**3.5.12.0-9.0-2.0-4**  (ID: `12649863`)

Scheduling timings Switching to 750 microseconds

gNB shall consider switching timing pattern from 0.5ms [Before CB013895-B](2 cores 2 cells) [End CB013895-B]to 0.75ms when at least one cell in the L2 subpool **satisfies below conditions:**

If NRBTS.tmpActFeat1(Bit 0) is set to 1 

 IIR filtered average of size of max CS1 list for DL in the mixed slots is less than a pre-defined threshold. Refer 5G_UP_ALG_30274_replaced([11116355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp3lI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

o Rationale: When gNB becomes overload, the overload control functionality decreases the size of DL CS1 list first. Hence, the size of DL CS1 list is used as a metric for detecting high load state for UL scheduler.

 EFS may extend more conditions for switching trigger based on overload control.

Else

  * The real-time scheduling (RT) processing load severity metric (See 5G_UP_ALG_31301_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value in UL provided by the gNB-DU in one of the cells is greater than or equal to the value set for **activation threshold of rdSlotDurationSwitchingOlcPerCent (See 5G_UP_ALG_33094_replaced([11217340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxeB-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**



Switching to 500 microseconds

gNB shall consider resuming the timing pattern of 0.5ms [Before CB013895-B](2 cores 2 cells) when both cells [CB013895-B] when the cell(s) [End CB013895-B]in the L2 subpool **satisfy below conditions:**

If NRBTS.tmpActFeat1(Bit 0) is set to 1 

 gNB stays in 0.75ms timing pattern long enough to avoid the ping-pong switching. AND

 at least one of following is satisfied:

o The max DL CS1 list size becomes greater or euqal to rdAvgMaxSizeDlCs1ThresholdForSwitchTo0dot5ms (5G_UP_ALG_33009_replaced([11118274](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqBWLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) and the max UL CS1 list size becomes greater or equal to rdAvgMaxSizeUlCs1ThresholdForSwitchTo0dot5ms (5G_UP_ALG_33011_replaced([11118280](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqB9MPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). 

 The details are left to EFS. EFS may extend more conditions for switching trigger based on overload control.

Else

  * The real-time scheduling (RT) processing load severity metric (See 5G_UP_ALG_31301_replaced([11216081](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUQfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) value in UL provided by the gNB-DU in both the cells is less than the value set for **deactivation threshold of rdSlotDurationSwitchingOlcPerCent (See 5G_UP_ALG_33094_replaced([11217340](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxeB-vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))**

---

**3.5.12.0-9.0-3**  (ID: `11116355`)

Scheduling timings IIR filtered average of size of max CSI1 list for DL in the Bi-directional slots

---

**3.5.12.0-9.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11116362`)

Scheduling timings Metric of IIR filtered average of size of max CSI1 list for DL in the Bi-directional slots

 rrmAvgMaxSizeDlCs1BiSlot[t] = (1-a) * rrmAvgMaxSizeDlCs1BiSlot[t-1] + a * rrmMaxSizeDlCs1BiSlot[t]

with:

 t: index of the Bi-directional slots. Only Bi-directional slots are counted, Uni-directional slots are excluded. Refer 5G_UP_13433_replaced([11388742](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufL_SPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), [CB013305-BC] 5G_UP_13435_replaced([11388784](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufL_T_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), [End CB013305-BC] 5G_UP_13436_replaced([11388805](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufMmUfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 rrmMaxSizeDlCs1BiSlot: size of max CSI1 list for DL in the Bi-directional slot

 a: 1/rdAvgMaxSizeDlCs1BiSlotFilterConst, 5G_UP_ALG_29962_replaced([11118262](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqBWKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Metric initialization and re-initialization are left to EFS.

Candidate Condition for switching from 0.5ms to 0.75ms timing pattern:

rrmAvgMaxSizeDlCs1BiSlot[t] < rdAvgMaxSizeDlCs1BiSlotThreshold

with:

 rdAvgMaxSizeDlCs1BiSlotThreshold: defined in 5G_UP_ALG_29964_replaced([11118268](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kqBWLPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Rationale:

Default value of rdAvgMaxSizeDlCs1BiSlotFilterConst and rdAvgMaxSizeDlCs1BiSlotThreshold is designed as: when the rrmAvgMaxSizeDlCs1BiSlot becomes to 2, if the size of max CSI1 list for DL in the Bi-directional slots keeps to 1 in 1 seconds, then switching timing pattern can be considered.

Note:

Python script for calculating the R&D parameter is stored at: [https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB013000%20-%20CB013499/CFAM/CB013305%20PDCCH%20-%20increased%20uplink%20capacity/5G_UP_ALG_29960.py?csf=1&web=1&e=epbLD9](https://nokia.sharepoint.com/:u:/r/sites/ASCOP/AS%20Documents/FS1TAs-CFAMs/CB/CB013000%20-%20CB013499/CFAM/CB013305%20PDCCH%20-%20increased%20uplink%20capacity/5G_UP_ALG_29960.py?csf=1&web=1&e=epbLD9)

---

**3.5.12.0-9.0-4**  (ID: `11116365`)

Scheduling timings CPU Processing Timing Pattern Switch Execution

---

**3.5.12.0-9.0-4.0-3**  (ID: `12649877`)

Scheduling timings gNB shall take following considers when perform CPU processing timing pattern switch for cases of 5G_UP_13514_replaced([11388917](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufMmXfvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))**, 5G_UP_13516_replaced([11388957](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufNNYPvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_13523_replaced([11389057](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufNNY_vkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))[CB013895-B], [12636905: Cases of timing pattern switch for 0.75ms (2 cores 1 cell) with multiple K2 mode](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_tpmZEkBWEfCal_QUJn4xrg?vvc.configuration=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/cm/stream/_KjDNNu3wEe-rL47x_rCCmw) [End CB013895-B]**:****

 gNB shall perform switching in the 2nd radio frame of the current PDCCH slot pattern

 gNB shall perform switching after DLS/ULS handling for the last UL slot in the selected radio frame.

 gNB shall perform switching on a time that both old timing pattern and new timing pattern locate at a NRT slot on the switching time.

When gNB generates the next PDCCH slot pattern (see 5G_UP_ALG_4001_replaced([11169142](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt1fKvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_5861_replaced([11362969](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_uc_m4vvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), gNB shall consider the potential K2 for PUSCH change upon timing pattern switching (5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))), UL PDCCH symbol shall be allocated on DL slot which can satisfy new K2 for PUSCH (slot N is with UL PDCCH and slot N+K2 for PUSCH is UL slot).

---

**3.5.12.0-9.0-5**  (ID: `11116376`)

Scheduling timings Timing pattern interworing with PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload

---

**3.5.12.0-9.0-5.0-2**  (ID: `13255869`)

Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings 13255869: Scheduling timings **Timing pattern interworing with PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload**

Background

In a L2 subpool with 2 cells (2cells 2 cores) deployment, a DL scheduler (DLS) and a UL scheduler (ULS) on same core belong to 2 different cells.

[image-0][image_desc]The image contains a table that illustrates the configuration of an L2 subpool with 2 cores and 2 cells.

| core0 | core1  
---|---|---  
| DLS 0 | ULS 1  
  
Term:

Buddy cell: another cell in the same L2 subpool.

Configuration of PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload

Activation flag: NRBTS-**actPdschSkipInSpSlotDueUlOvl. Functionality is activated when this parameter is set to TRUE.**

Cell Level Eligible Configuration

· The cell is TDD FR1 [Before CNI-152144-A]and

· NRCELL-tddFrameStructure.frameStructureType is equal to 'tdLte' and

· NRCELL-actEnhancedPDSCHSchSplSlot is TRUE and

· NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity is FALSE

[End CNI-152144-A]​​​​​​​

Intention of PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload

For 2 cells in a L2 subpool, if one cell is in UL overload, gNB performs PDSCH skipping on buddy cell, so that the cell in UL overload can get more processing time for ULS.

Applicable condition when 2 cells in a L2 subpool

For UL overload cell: functionality is activated and meets cell level eligible configuration **and [Before CNI-152144-A]K2=2[CNI-152144-A] single K2 value is in use[End CNI-152144-A].**

For buddy cell: functionality is activated and meets cell level eligible configuration **.**

Events impact the applicability

  1. The number of cells in a L2 subpool is changed. i.e. A cell is added to/deleted from the L2 subpool.

  2. The activation flag change.

  3. Timing pattern change, because it may change K2.

  4. UL overload cell K2 change due to K2Rim - RIM PDCCH muting




**Rationale of the applicabilty is analyzed in 5G_UP_ALG_32843_replaced([11116386](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp3lK_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).**

References

 Timing pattern: 5G_UP_13432_replaced([11388728](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ufL_RvvkEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 K2 is K2 for PUSCH, K2Rim (RIM PDCCH muting): 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 RIM DL muting: 5G_UP_ALG_29888_replaced([11161119](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktSFjPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

 UL overload detection for PDSCH skipping in special slot: 5G_UP_ALG_32690_replaced([11216120](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kxXUSPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

 PDSCH skipping in special slot due to UL overload: 5G_UP_ALG_29911_replaced([11174311](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kuat-PvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

**3.5.12.0-9.0-6**  (ID: `11116386`)

Scheduling timings Rationale for timing pattern interworking with PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload

---

**3.5.12.0-9.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `13255879`)

Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings 13255879: Scheduling timings **Following is rationale of the applicability**

[Before CNI-152144-A]

0.5ms timing pattern single K2 mode and condition for PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload

1\. ULS of the UL overload cell needs to allocate UL PDCCH on special slot

 K2=2 (rrmK2min=2, K2Rim=none(no RIM PDCCH muting)).

i. Map to row 2/3/6/7 in figure below. ULS runs at slot 1 to allocate UL PDCCH on slot 3 (special slot) for PUSCH on slot 5.

2\. DLS of the buddy cell needs to allocate DL PDCCH on special slot and is not in RIM DL muting

 Map to row 3/7 or row 5/9 in figure below. DLS runs at slot 1 to allocate DL PDCCH on slot 3 (special slot) for PDSCH on slot 3.

3\. 1 and 2 need running on the same time, so that PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload can provide more processing time for ULS of the UL overload cell.

 Above 1 and 2 satisfy this condition.

**4.** Conclusion:****

 When UL overload cell in row2/6 or row3/7 and buddy cell in row 3/7 or row 5/9, timing pattern is applicable for buddy cell to do PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload.

The table has rows labeled with "PxSCH" and "PDCCH K2" values on the left, and "DLS/ULS 0.5ms" with corresponding row numbers on the left. The columns are labeled with numbers (7, 8, 9) and then "0 DL", "1 DL", "2 DL", "3 S", "4 UL", "5 UL", "6 DL", "7 DL", "8 DL", "9 DL". The cells within the table contain various codes and abbreviations, such as "DLO", "DL1", "DL2 UL4", "UL5", "DL3 UL5", "DL6", "DL7", "DL8", "DL9", "NRT", and numerical values like "0", "1", "3", "6", "7", "8", "9". Some cells are colored yellow, pink, and light blue, which might indicate different types of resources or statuses. Here is the table in markdown format:

| 7 | 8 | 9 | 0 DL | 1 DL | 2 DL | 3 S | 4 UL | 5 UL | 6 DL | 7 DL | 8 DL | 9 DL  
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---  
PxSCH |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |   
PDCCH K2 =2 |  |  |  | DLO | DL1 | DL2 UL4 | UL5 |  |  | DL6 | DL7 | DL8 | DL9  
PDCCH K2 =2 |  |  |  | DLO | DL1 | DL2 UL4 | DL3 UL5 |  |  | DL6 | DL7 | DL8 | DL9  
PDCCH K2=3 |  |  |  | DLO | DL1 UL4 | DL2 UL5 |  |  |  | DL6 | DL7 | DL8 | DL9  
PDCCH K2 =3 |  |  |  | DLO | DL1 UL4 | DL2 UL5 | DL3 |  |  | DL6 | DL7 | DL8 | DL9  
DLS/ULS 0.5ms | 0 | 1 |  | DL2 UL4 | UL5 | NRT | NRT | 6 | 7 | 8 | 9 |  |   
DLS/ULS 0.5ms | 0 | 1 |  | DL2 UL4 | DL3 ULS | NRT | NRT | 6 | 7 | 8 | 9 |  |   
DLS/ULS 0.5ms | 0 | DL1 UL4 | DL2 UL5 | NRT | NRT | NRT | 6 | 7 | 8 | 9 |  |  |   
DLS/ULS 0.5ms | 0 | DL1 UL4 | DL2 UL5 | 3 | NRT | NRT | 6 | 7 | 8 | 9 |  |  | [/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5710.001.png[/image_path][/image-0]  
  
Figure illustrates with NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.lteToNrFrameShift = 0ms from one cell point of view, PDCCH and DLS/ULS association: row 2/6, row 3/7, row 4/8, row 5/9.

Row2/6 indicates RIM DL muting on special slot.

Row4/8 indicates RIM DL muting and RIM PDCCH muting on special slot.

RIM DL muting is PDSCH skipping on special slot during RIM DL mitigation

RIM PDCCH muting is the PDCCH muting on special slot during RIM DL mitigation.

0.75ms timing pattern for single K2 mode and condition for PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload

1\. ULS of the UL overload cell needs to allocate UL PDCCH on special slot

 K2=2 (rrmK2min=2, K2Rim=none(no RIM PDCCH muting)).

i. Map to row 2/6 or row 3/6 in figure below. ULS runs at slot 0..1 to allocate UL PDCCH on slot 3(special slot) for PUSCH on slot 5.

2\. DLS of the buddy cell needs to allocate DL PDCCH on special slot and is not in RIM DL muting.

 Map to row 3/6 or row 5/8 in figure below. DLS runs at slot 0..1 to allocate DL PDCCH on slot 3 (special slot) for PDSCH on slot 3.

3\. 1 and 2 need running on the same time, so that PDSCH skipping on special slot can provide more processing time for ULS of the UL overload cell.

4\. 0.75ms timing pattern in single K2 mode needs both cell with the same K2.

**5.** Conclusion:****

 When UL overload cell in row2/6 or row 3/6 and buddy cell in row 3/6, timing pattern is applicable for buddy cell to do PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload.

The table is divided into two main sections. The top section has columns labeled with numbers 7 through 9, and then categories like "0 DL", "1 DL", "2 DL", "3 S", "4 UL", "5 UL", "6 DL", "7 DL", "8 DL", and "9 DL". The rows are labeled "PxSCH", "PDCCH K2 = 2" (repeated twice), and "PDCCH K2 = 3" (repeated twice). The cells contain codes like "DLO", "DL1", "DL2 UL4", "UL5", "DL6", "DL7", "DL8", "DL9", and "DL3 UL5". Some cells are highlighted in yellow. The bottom section has rows labeled "DLS/ULS 0.75ms" (repeated three times). The columns in this section are labeled with numbers 0 through 9, with some cells containing "NRT". The cells contain codes like "0", "1", "DL2 UL4", "DL3 UL5", "DL1 UL4", "DL2 UL5", and "3". Some cells are highlighted in pink and yellow. The table seems to illustrate how different types of data (DL for downlink, UL for uplink) and control information (PxSCH, PDCCH, DLS/ULS) are allocated across different time slots or resource blocks, possibly with different configurations indicated by "K2" values and durations like "0.75ms". The "NRT" likely stands for "Non-Real-Time".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5710.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

Figure illustrates with NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.lteToNrFrameShift = 0ms, PDCCH and DLS/ULS association: row 2/6, row 3/6, row 4/8, row 5/9.

Row 2 indicates RIM DL mitigation on special slot.

Row4/7 indicates RIM DL mitigation and RIM PDCCH muting on special slot

0.75ms timing pattern for multiple K2 mode and PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload

1\. This timing pattern is **NOT applicable for PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload because**

 0.75ms timing pattern requires both cells with K2.

 Figure illustrates with NRCELL/tddFrameStructure.lteToNrFrameShift = 0ms

i. ULS of UL overload cell runs at slot 9..0 to allocate UL PDCCH on slot 3 (special slot) for PUSCH on slot 5

ii. DLS of buddy cell runs at slot 1 to allocate DL PDCCH on slot 3 (special slot) for PDSCH on slot 3

iii.They do not run at the same time, hence it is not helpful for UL overload cell even if buddy cell does PDSCH skipping on special slot.

[image-2][image_desc]The image contains a table that illustrates different configurations of PxSCH, PDCCH, and DLS/ULS with associated time delays and channel assignments. The table has 4 rows and 13 columns. The first column lists the channel types: PxSCH, PDCCH, and DLS/ULS. The second column specifies time delays (tdLte, 0ms) and K2 values (K2=4,3,2; K2=4,3; K2=4,3(,2)). The remaining columns represent time slots or channel assignments, labeled with numbers 7 through 9, and then DL (Downlink) and UL (Uplink) assignments. Here's a breakdown of the table content:

Channel Type | Time/K2 Value | 7 | 8 | 9 | 0 DL | 1 DL | 2 DL | 3 S | 4 UL | 5 UL | 6 DL | 7 DL | 8 DL | 9 DL | Row Number  
---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---  
PxSCH | tdLte, 0ms |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  | 1  
PDCCH | K2=4,3,2 |  |  |  | DLO UL4 | DL1 (UL4) | DL2 (UL5) | (DL3) UL5 |  |  | DL6 | DL7 | DL8 | DL9 | 2  
PDCCH | K2=4,3 |  |  |  | DLO UL4 | DL1 UL4 | DL2 UL5 |  |  |  |  |  |  |  | 3  
DLS/ULS | K2=4,3(,2) | DLO UL4 | 1 | DL2 UL5 | 3 or NRT | NRT | 6 | 7 | 8 | 9 |  |  |  | 4 |   
The table shows how different channel types are mapped to specific time slots and DL/UL assignments based on their configurations. For example, in row 2, for PDCCH with K2=4,3,2, the time slot '0 DL' is mapped to 'DLO UL4', '1 DL' to 'DL1 (UL4)', and so on. Row 4, for DLS/ULS with K2=4,3(,2), shows a different mapping across time slots, including '3 or NRT' and 'NRT' for non-real-time traffic.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5710.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |   
  
**PDL rule de-activates multiple K2 when the functionality is activated, hence need not consider interworking with multiple K2.**

[CNI-152144-A]

Definition used in further points:

  1. ULSn,x,y = Instance of ULS running in processing slot ‘n’, using UL PDCCH resources from slot ‘x’ to send the UL DCI, to schedule an UL transmission on slot ‘y’ (=’x’+k2)
  2. DLSn,x = Instance of DLS running in processing slot ‘n’, using DL PDCCH resources from slot ‘x’ to send the DL DCI, to schedule a DL transmission on slot ‘x’



In a “2Core-2Cell” deployment, whenever

  1. ULS of a cell is overloaded and ULSn,x,y is using the PDCCH resources from a special slot ‘x’ to send the UL DCI
  2. DLSn,x of buddy cell uses the PDCCH resources from the same special slot ‘x’ to send DL DCI
  3. ULSn,x,y and DLSn,x are both running on processing slot ‘n’
  4. PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload shall provide more processing time to ULS of the UL overloaded cell
  5. Which means this pattern is applicable for the buddy cell to do PDSCH skipping on special slot due to UL overload.



NOTE: Above logic is dependent on current K2 value, which can be influenced by

  1. Frame structure in use
  2. RIM PDCCH muting feature (activated/deactivated)
  3. RIM DL muting (activated/deactivated)



[End CNI-152144-A]

**3.5.12.0-10.0-1.0-2**  (ID: `11116411`)

Scheduling timings Cell state machine name notation:

[Before CB013305-BC]

 1st number

o 5: 0.5ms timing pattern

o 7: 0.75ms timing pattern

 2nd number: 

o 1: 2 cores 1 cell

o 2: 2 cores 2 cells

[CB013305-BC]

 1st number: rrmK2min

 2nd number: min value in k2_pusch

 3rd number

o 5: 0.5ms timing pattern 

o 7: 0.75ms timing pattern

 4th number: 

o 1: 2 cores 1cell

o 2: 2 cores 2cells. Note: for fixed 0.75ms pattern, even if there is only one cell in L2 subpool, the timing pattern still follows 2cores-2cells case.

[End CB013305-BC]

[CB013305-C]

 5th: 

o m: multiple K2 mode

o if no: single K2 mode

[End CB013305-C]

11116430: Scheduling timings 11116430: Scheduling timings K2 and timing pattern state machine - dynamic 0.75ms, fixed K2=3 This requirement uses state machine diagram to describe CPU processing timing pattern transition defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) from a cell point of view. Cases in this requirement are applied when [Before CB013305-B-UPD001] NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDuration is TRUE [CB013305-B-UPD001] NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode is 'partial' [End CB013305-B-UPD001] and R&D parameter rdFixBISlotTimings = 0. Refer 5G_UP_ALG_30446_replaced([11116403](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp3lLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for state name notation. General Explanation: K2 is fixed to 3 and is not shown in state machine. A cell needs an attribute to track load status which indicates whether 0.75ms is needed or not. To reduce the number of states, this attribute is not reflected in diagram.  Event 0.75ms indicates the cell from low load to high load and needs 0.75ms.  Event 0.5ms indicates the cell from high load to low load and does not need 0.75ms. In event "create self", "self" is the cell to be created. Figure 1 below for state transition for dynamic timing pattern switching [image-0][image_desc]The image is a state transition diagram illustrating cell timing patterns with a fixed K2 value of 3. The diagram consists of four states: "Cell is NA", "51", "52", and "72". State "Cell is NA" is the initial state. From this state, there are two transitions, both labeled "create self", leading to states "51" and "52" respectively. State "51" is described as having "0.5ms" and "2cores 1 cell". There are two transitions originating from state "51": 1\. An "add buddy cell" transition leading to state "52". 2\. A "del buddy cell" transition leading back to state "51" (a self-loop). State "52" is described as having "0.5ms" and "2cores 2 cells". There are three transitions originating from state "52": 1\. A "del buddy cell" transition leading to state "51". 2\. A "self or buddy trigger 0.75ms" transition leading to state "72". 3\. A transition labeled "self trigger 0.5ms and buddy in low load or self in low load and buddy trigger 0.5ms" leading to state "52" (a self-loop). State "72" is described as having "0.75ms" and "2cores 2 cells". There are two transitions originating from state "72": 1\. A "del buddy cell" transition leading to state "51". 2\. A transition labeled "self trigger 0.5ms and buddy in low load or self in low load and buddy trigger 0.5ms" leading to state "52". The diagram visually represents how a cell's timing pattern and configuration (number of cores and cells) can change based on different triggers and conditions.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5718.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] Transition notes:  NA --> 51: this happens when to be created cell is the first cell in L2 subpool  NA --> 52: this happens when to be created cell is the second cell in L2 subpool  72 --> 52: a cell trigger 0.5ms [Before CB013305-B-UPD002] when another cell is in load load status [End CB013305-B-UPD002] File: dyn-075ms-fixed-k2-3-figure-1.puml Folder:

11116451: Scheduling timings 11116451: Scheduling timings K2 and timing pattern state machine - dynamic 0.75ms with K2=2 and 3 This requirement uses state machine diagram to describe K2 and CPU processing timing pattern transition defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) from a cell point of view. Cases in this requirement are applied for configuration:  Configuration 1: o NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode = 'full' [Before CB013305-BC-UPD001] o R&D parameter rdFixBISlotTimings = 0 [End CB013305-BC-UPD001] [CB013305-C] o NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity = False [End CB013305-C] Refer 5G_UP_ALG_30446_replaced([11116403](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp3lLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) for state name notation. General Explantion: A cell needs an attribute to track load status which indicates whether 0.75ms is needed or not. To reduce the number of states, this attribute is not reflected in diagram.  Event 0.75ms indicates the cell is in high load status and needs 0.75ms.  Event 0.5ms indicates the cell is in low load status and does not need 0.75ms. Attribute k2Cell (5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) is not shown in diagram. k2_pusch is the effective K2 for PUSCH. In event "create self", "self" is the cell to be created. Figure 1 below for state transition cases due to buddy cell addition/deletion [image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating cell K2 and timing pattern transitions. The flowchart consists of several rectangular nodes, each representing a state, and directed arrows connecting these states, indicating transitions. Each node contains a numerical identifier, followed by parameters like "rrmK2min", "k2_pusch", "k2Rim", timing information (e.g., "0.75ms", "0.5ms"), and core/cell configuration (e.g., "2cores 2 cells", "2cores 1 cell"). Some nodes are labeled "Cell is NA" with additional information about the subpool. The transitions between states are labeled with actions such as "del buddy cell", "create self", and "add buddy cell". The title of the flowchart is "Cell K2 and timing pattern transition: dynamic 0.75ms with K2=2 and 3". Here's a breakdown of the states and transitions: **States:** * **3372:** rrmK2min=3, k2_pusch=3, 0.75ms, 2cores 2 cells * **Cell is NA (0 cell in L2 subpool)** * **2272:** rrmK2min=2, k2_pusch=2, 0.75ms, 2cores 2 cells * **2372:** rrmK2min=2, k2Rim=none or 3, k2_pusch=3, 0.75ms, 2cores 2 cells * **3351:** rrmK2min=3, k2_pusch=3, 0.5ms, 2cores 1 cell * **Cell is NA (1 cell in L2 subpool)** * **2251:** rrmK2min=2, k2_pusch=2, 0.5ms, 2cores 1 cell * **2351:** rrmK2min=2, k2Rim=3, k2_pusch=3, 0.5ms, 2cores 1 cell * **3352:** rrmK2min=3, k2_pusch=3, 0.5ms, 2cores 2 cells * **2252:** rrmK2min=2, k2_pusch=2, 0.5ms, 2cores 2 cells * **2352:** rrmK2min=2, k2Rim=3, k2_pusch=3, 0.5ms, 2cores 2 cells **Transitions:** * 3372 -> 3351 (del buddy cell) * 3372 -> Cell is NA (0 cell in L2 subpool) (create self) * Cell is NA (0 cell in L2 subpool) -> 2272 (create self) * Cell is NA (0 cell in L2 subpool) -> 2372 (create self) * 2272 -> 2251 (del buddy cell) * 2272 -> Cell is NA (1 cell in L2 subpool) (create self) * 2372 -> 2351 (del buddy cell (k2Rim=3)) * 2372 -> 2251 (del buddy cell (k2Rim=none)) * 2372 -> Cell is NA (1 cell in L2 subpool) (create self) * 3351 -> 3352 (add buddy cell) * 3351 -> Cell is NA (1 cell in L2 subpool) (create self) * Cell is NA (1 cell in L2 subpool) -> 2251 (create self) * Cell is NA (1 cell in L2 subpool) -> 2351 (create self) * 2251 -> 2252 (add buddy cell) * 2251 -> 2351 (del buddy cell) * 2351 -> 2352 (add buddy cell) * 2351 -> 2251 (del buddy cell) * 3352 -> 3351 (del buddy cell) * 2252 -> 2251 (del buddy cell) * 2352 -> 2351 (del buddy cell)[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5722.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] State transition notes for figure 1:  2372 --> 2251: when the cell k2Rim=none  2372 --> 2351: when the cell k2Rim=3 Figure 2 below for state transition cases with rrmK2min=2 and 2cores 2cells [image-1][image_desc]The image is a state diagram illustrating cell K2 and timing pattern transitions, specifically focusing on dynamic 0.75ms with K2 values of 2 and 3. The diagram consists of five states, labeled 2352, 2252, 2272, and 2372. Each state box contains information about the cell's configuration, including `rrmK2min`, `k2Rim`, `k2_pusch`, timing parameters (0.5ms or 0.75ms), and core/cell configuration (2cores 2 cells). Arrows between states represent transitions, with labels indicating the conditions or events that trigger these transitions, such as specific `k2Rim` values or "buddy" configurations with associated timings. Here's a breakdown of the states and transitions: **States:** * **2352:** * `rrmK2min=2` * `k2Rim=3` * `k2_pusch=3` * `0.5ms` * `2cores 2 cells` * **2252:** * `rrmK2min=2` * `k2_pusch=2` * `0.5ms` * `2cores 2 cells` * **2272:** * `rrmK2min=2` * `k2_pusch=2` * `0.75ms` * `2cores 2 cells` * **2372:** * `rrmK2min=2` * `k2Rim=none or 3` * `k2_pusch=3` * `0.75ms` * `2cores 2 cells` **Transitions:** * **2352 to 2252:** Triggered by `k2Rim=none`. * **2252 to 2352:** Triggered by `k2Rim=3`. * **2352 to 2372:** Triggered by `0.75ms or buddy 0.75ms`. * **2252 to 2372:** Triggered by `0.5ms or buddy 0.5ms`. * **2272 to 2252:** Triggered by `0.5ms`. * **2252 to 2272:** Triggered by `0.75ms`. * **2272 to 2372:** Triggered by `k2Rim=none or buddy k2=2`. * **2372 to 2272:** Triggered by `k2Rim=3 or buddy k2=3`. * **2372 to 2352:** Triggered by `0.5ms or buddy 0.5ms`.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5722.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] State transition notes for figure 2:  2252 --> 2372 o buddy 0.75ms: buddy cell uses 0.75ms and k2_pusch=3  2372 --> 2252 o buddy 0.5ms: buddy cell uses 0.5ms when the cell need not 0.75ms  2352 -> 2372 o buddy 0.75ms: buddy cell uses 0.75ms  2372 --> 2352 o buddy 0.5ms: buddy cell uses 0.5ms when the cell need not 0.75ms  2272 -> 2372 o buddy k2=3: buddy cell k2Rim=3  2372 --> 2272 o buddy k2=2: buddy cell k2Rim=none when the cell k2Rim=none Figure 3 below for state transition cases not covered in figure 1 and figure 2 [image-2][image_desc]The image is a diagram illustrating the transitions between different cell states based on K2 and timing patterns. The diagram features four rectangular states, each labeled with a number and containing specific parameters. Arrows indicate the transitions between these states, with labels specifying the conditions for each transition. The states are: * **2251** : * rrmK2min=2 * k2_pusch=2 * 0.5ms * 2cores 1 cell * **3352** : * rrmK2min=3 * k2_pusch=3 * 0.5ms * 2cores 2 cells * **2351** : * rrmK2min=2 * k2Rim=3 * k2_pusch=3 * 0.5ms * 2cores 1 cell * **3372** : * rrmK2min=3 * k2_pusch=3 * 0.75ms * 2cores 2 cells The transitions are: * From **2251** to **2351** : Triggered by "k2Rim=3". * From **2351** to **2251** : Triggered by "k2Rim=none". * From **3352** to **3372** : Triggered by "0.75ms or buddy 0.75ms". * From **3372** to **3352** : Triggered by "0.5ms or buddy 0.5ms". The title of the diagram is "Cell K2 and timing pattern transition: dynamic 0.75ms with K2=2 and 3".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5722.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] State transition notes for figure 2:  3352 -> 3372 o buddy 0.75ms: buddy cell uses 0.75ms  3372 --> 3352 o buddy 0.5ms: buddy cell uses 0.5ms when the cell need not 0.75ms Note1: States with 0.75ms are new functionalities in CB013305-BC. Note2: States with 0.5ms are legacy functionalities. Transitions with involving only 0.5ms state are legacy. 11116468: Scheduling timings 11116468: Scheduling timings K2 and timing pattern state machine - Dynamic multiple K2 mode when 2cores 1 cell This requirement uses state machine diagram to describe K2 and CPU processing timing pattern transition defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_30394_replaced([11116305](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) from a cell point of view. Cases in this requirement are applied for configurations:  Configuration 1: o NRBTS-actBbPoolTddFr1 = False o NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity = True o [Before CB013305-C-UPD001] R&D parameter rdFixedMultipleK2ForIncUlPdcchCapacity = 0 [End CB013305-C-UPD001]  Configuration 2: o NRBTS-actBbPoolTddFr1 = True o NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity = True [Before CB013305-E-UPD001] [CB013305-E] o NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode = 'full' (PDL rule) o R&D parameter rdFixBISlotTimings = 0 [End CB013305-E] [End CB013305-E-UPD001] o [Before CB013305-C-UPD001] R&D parameter rdFixedMultipleK2ForIncUlPdcchCapacity = 0 [End CB013305-C-UPD001] o With 1 cell in L2 subpool State name notation refer 5G_UP_ALG_30446_replaced([11116403](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp3lLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) General Explanation: K2Load event indicates the event to trigger K2 mode switching as 5G_UP_ALG_30707_replaced([11169564](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kt4icfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)). Figure 1 [CB013305-D-UPD004] [image-0][image_desc]The image is a state diagram illustrating the transitions between different states related to "Cell K2 and timing pattern transition for dynamic multiple K2 mode when 2cores 1cell". The diagram consists of several rectangular boxes representing states, connected by arrows indicating transitions. Each state box contains a state identifier (e.g., "NA", "3351", "2251") and a set of parameters describing the state. Here's a breakdown of the states and transitions: **States:** * **NA:** This is the initial state. * No parameters are listed. * **3351:** * rrmK2min=3 * k2_mode=single * k2_pusch=3 * 0.5ms * 2cores 1cell * **2251:** * rrmK2min=2 * k2_mode=single * k2Rim=none * k2_pusch=2 * 0.5ms * 2cores 1cell * **2251m:** * rrmK2min=2 * k2_mode=multiple * k2Rim=none * k2_pusch=4,3,2 * 0.5ms * 2cores 1cell * **3351m:** * rrmK2min=3 * k2_mode=multiple * k2_pusch=4,3 * 0.5ms * 2cores 1cell * **2351:** * rrmK2min=2 * k2_mode=signle * k2Rim=3 * k2_pusch=3 * 0.5ms * 2cores 1cell * **2351m:** * rrmK2min=2 * k2_mode=multiple * k2Rim=3 * k2_pusch=4,3 * 0.5ms * 2cores 1cell **Transitions:** * **NA** to **3351** : Triggered by "create". * **NA** to **2251** : Triggered by "create". * **3351** to **3351m** : Triggered by "K2Load". * **2251** to **3351** : Triggered by "K2Load". * **2251** to **2251m** : Triggered by "K2Load". * **2251m** to **2251** : Triggered by "K2Load". * **2251m** to **2351m** : Triggered by "k2Rim=3". * **2251m** to **2351** : Triggered by "k2Rim=none". * **3351m** to **2351** : Triggered by "K2Load". * **2351** to **2351m** : Triggered by "K2Load". * **2351** to **2251** : Triggered by "k2Rim=3". * **2351** to **2251m** : Triggered by "k2Rim=none". * **2351m** to **2351** : Triggered by "k2Rim=3". * **2351m** to **2251m** : Triggered by "k2Rim=none".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5726.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] [End CB013305-D-UPD004] State transition notes for figure 1: · 2251 --> 2251m, [CB013305-D-UPD004] 2351 --> 2351m, 3351 --> 3351m [End CB013305-D-UPD004]: K2Load event requires a high number of UL PDCCH symbols · 2251m --> 2251, [CB013305-D-UPD004] 2351m --> 2351, 3351m --> 3351 [End CB013305-D-UPD004]: K2Load event requires a low number of UL PDCCH symbols · [Before CB013305-D-UPD004] 2251m --> 2251m: K2Load event requires a high number of UL PDCCH symbols. (Remove for figure simplificaiton) [End CB013305-D-UPD004] · 2351m --> 2251m: k2Rim=none and when needs a high number of UL PDCCH symbols [Before CB013305-D-UPD004] · 2351m --> 2251: k2Rim=none when need a low number of UL PDCCH symbols [CB013305-D-UPD004] · 2251m --> 2351m: k2Rim=3 and when needs a high number of UL PDCCH symbols · 2351 --> 2251: k2Rim=none when need a low number of UL PDCCH symbols · 2251 --> 2351: k2Rim=3 when need a low number of UL PDCCH symbols [End CB013305-D-UPD004] Notes: Note1: 2251m is new functionality in CB013305-C [CB013305-D] Note2: 2351m and 3351m are new functionality in CB013305-D [End CB013305-D] [CB013305-D-UPD004] Note3: 2251, 2351, 3351 are legacy functionality except K2 mode concept. [End CB013305-D-UPD004] For state 3351 and 3351m, k2Rim is not shown because it does not impact K2 for PUSCH when rrmK2min=3. Figure:

11116477: Scheduling timings 11116477: Scheduling timings [Before CB013305-E-UPD001][Before CB013305-F-UPD001] this req is deleted K2 and timing pattern state machine - Fixed multiple K2 mode [Before CB013305-25R3-UPD002] and fixed 0.75ms [End CB013305-25R3-UPD002] when 2cores 2cells This requirement uses state machine diagram to describe K2 and CPU processing timing pattern transition defined in 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) and 5G_UP_ALG_30394_replaced([11116305](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) from a cell point of view. Cases in this requirement are applied for configurations:  Configuration 1: o NRBTS-actBbPoolTddFr1 = True o NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity = True o NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode = 'full' (PDL rule) o R&D parameter rdFixBISlotTimings = 1 o R&D parameter rdFixedMultipleK2ForIncUlPdcchCapacity = 0 or 1 State name notation refer 5G_UP_ALG_30446_replaced([11116403](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp3lLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating the "Cell K2 transition when fixed multiple K2 mode". The flowchart starts with a state labeled "Cell is NA". From this state, there are two transitions, both labeled "create self". The first "create self" transition leads to a state labeled "2272m". This state has the following properties: * rrmK2min=2 * k2_mode=multiple * k2Rim=none * k2_pusch=4,3,3,2 * 0.75ms * 2cores 2 cells The second "create self" transition leads to a state labeled "3372m". This state has the following properties: * rrmK2min=3 * k2_mode=multiple * k2_pusch=4,3;3 * 0.75ms * 2cores 2 cells From the "2272m" state, there are two outgoing transitions: * A transition labeled "k2Rim=3" leads to a state labeled "2372m". * A transition labeled "k2Rim=none" leads back to the "2272m" state (forming a self-loop, though the arrow points upwards to the same box). The "2372m" state has the following properties: * rrmK2min=2 * k2_mode=multiple * k2Rim=3 * k2_pusch=4,3;3 * 0.75ms * 2cores 2 cells From the "2372m" state, there is one outgoing transition labeled "k2Rim=none" which leads back to the "2272m" state.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5728.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] Note1: 2272m is new functionality in CB013305-E [CB013305-F] Note2: 2372m and 3372m are new functionality in CB013305-F [End CB013305-F] [End CB013305-F-UPD001][End CB013305-E-UPD001] File: TBD Folder:

5G_UP_ALG_30394_replaced([11116305](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-FPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) from a cell point of view. Cases in this requirement are applied for configurations:  Configuration 1: o NRBTS-actBbPoolTddFr1 = True o NRCELL-actIncUlPdcchCapacity = True o NRBTS-actFlexibleBISlotDurationMode = 'full' (PDL rule) o R&D parameter rdFixBISlotTimings = 0 o R&D parameter rdFixedMultipleK2ForIncUlPdcchCapacity = 1 o 1 cell or 2 cells in the L2 subpool State name notation refer 5G_UP_ALG_30446_replaced([11116403](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp3lLvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) [image-0][image_desc]The image is a flowchart illustrating the cell K2 and timing pattern transitions in a fixed multiple K2 mode. The flowchart consists of several rectangular nodes, each representing a state, and directed arrows connecting these states, indicating transitions. Each node contains a state name (e.g., "2251m", "3351m") and a set of parameters associated with that state, such as "rrmK2min", "k2_mode", "k2_pusch", timing values (e.g., "0.5ms", "0.75ms"), and core/cell configurations (e.g., "2cores 1cell", "2cores 2cells"). The states and their parameters are: * **cell is NA** : This is the initial state. * Transitions from this state: * "create self" to "2251m" * "create self" to "3351m" * **2251m** : * rrmK2min=2 * k2_mode=multiple * k2_pusch=4,3,3,2 * 0.5ms * 2cores 1cell * Transitions from this state: * "add buddy" to "2272m" * "del buddy" to "2351m" * "k2Rim=3" to "2372m" * **3351m** : * rrmK2min=3 * k2_mode=multiple * k2_pusch=4,3;3 * 0.5ms * 2cores 1 cell * Transitions from this state: * "add buddy" to "3372m" * "del buddy" to "2351m" * **2272m** : * rrmK2min=2 * active_k2_mode=multiple * k2_pusch=4,3,3,2 * 0.75ms * 2cores 2cells * Transitions from this state: * "k2Rim=3" to "2372m" * "k2Rim=none" to "2251m" * **2372m** : * rrmK2min=2 * k2Rim=3 * active_k2_mode=multiple * k2_pusch=4,3;3 * 0.75ms * 2cores 2cells * Transitions from this state: * "del buddy" to "2251m" * "add buddy" to "2351m" * "k2Rim=none" to "2272m" * **2351m** : * rrmK2min=2 * k2Rim=3 * k2_mode=multiple * k2_pusch=4,3;3 * 0.5ms * 2cores 1cell * Transitions from this state: * "k2Rim=3" to "2372m" * "k2Rim=none" to "3351m" * **3372m** : * rrmK2min=3 * active_k2_mode=multiple * k2_pusch=4,3;3 * 0.75ms * 2cores 2cells * Transitions from this state: * "k2Rim=3" to "2351m" * "k2Rim=none" to "3351m" The title of the flowchart is "Cell K2 and timing pattern transition - fixed multiple K2 mode".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5730.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] Note1: 2251m is new functionality in CB013305-C Note2: 2351m and 3351m are new functionality in CB013305-D Note3: 2272m is new functionality in CB013305-E [CB013305-F] Note4: 2372m and 3372m are new functionality in CB013305-F [End CB013305-F] [End CB013305-F-UPD001][End CB013305-E-UPD001] File: TBD Folder:

**[Before CB015800-C]**

[image-0][image_desc]The image is a state transition diagram illustrating the behavior of CB013895 in a 2-core, 1-cell configuration. The diagram consists of several rectangular states, each representing a specific configuration or condition, and arrows connecting these states, indicating possible transitions. Each state is labeled with values for K2 and CB013895, along with a description of the associated time, core usage, and cell count. The transitions between states are triggered by specific conditions, such as "High K2 Load," "High OLC and CB013895 is disabled," or "RIM mitigation activated/deactivated." Here's a breakdown of the states and transitions: **States:** * **State 1 (Red):** * K2=4,3,2 * K2=4,3 * -0.5ms, 2cores 1cell * **State 2 (Gray):** * K2=2 (Legacy) * K2=3 (Legacy) * -0.5ms, 2cores 1cell * **State 3 (Blue):** * K2=4,3,2 * K2=4,3 * -0.75ms, 2cores 1cell * **State 4 (Orange):** * K2=4,3,2 * K2=4,3 * -0.75ms, 2cores 1cell * - New algorithm to mitigate RU limitations (max 24 scheduled UEs allowed) **Transitions:** * **From State 1 (Red) to State 3 (Blue):** * Trigger: "2 SFN and CB013895 is disabled" (Green arrow) * **From State 1 (Red) to State 2 (Gray):** * Trigger: "High K2 Load" (Purple arrow) * **From State 2 (Gray) to State 3 (Blue):** * Trigger: "High OLC and CB013895 is disabled" (Yellow arrow) * **From State 2 (Gray) to State 1 (Red):** * Trigger: "CB013895 disabled online" (Red arrow) * Trigger: "CB013895 enabled online" (Green arrow) * Trigger: "- RIM mitigation activated/deactivated" (Red arrow) * **From State 3 (Blue) to State 4 (Orange):** * Trigger: "CB013895 enabled online" (Purple arrow) * **From State 3 (Blue) to State 2 (Gray):** * Trigger: "High K2 Load or OLC - RIM mitigation activated/deactivated" (Red arrow) * **From State 3 (Blue) to State 1 (Red):** * Trigger: "2 SFN and CB013895 is enabled" (Brown arrow) * **From State 4 (Orange) to State 3 (Blue):** * Trigger: "High OLC and CB013895 is enabled" (Blue arrow) * Trigger: "- Low (K2 load and OLC) - and 2 SFNS" (Red arrow) * **From State 4 (Orange) to State 2 (Gray):** * Trigger: "CB013895 disabled online" (Red arrow) * Trigger: "- Low (K2 load and OLC) - and 2 SFNS" (Yellow arrow) * **From State 4 (Orange) to State 1 (Red):** * Trigger: "High K2 Load or OLC - RIM mitigation activated/deactivated" (Orange arrow) The diagram visually represents how CB013895 operates under different network conditions, transitioning between various states based on factors like K2 load, OLC (On-Link Control), SFNs (Self-Forming Networks), and RIM (Radio Interface Management) mitigation.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/CB013895 overview 2Core 1Cell.JPG[/image_path][/image-0]

**[CB015800-C]**

**Please note when CB013895-B is not activated (default radparam config in 26R2), for config 2 cores 1 cell we stay in 0.5ms. When CB013895-B is activated (default radparam config in 26R2) for config 2 cores 1 cell we switch to 0.75ms.**

[image-1][image_desc]The image is a state transition diagram illustrating the states for one cell in an L2 subpool, specifically for configurations of 2-Core-1Cells or 1-Core-1Cell. The diagram uses different colored boxes to represent states and arrows to show transitions between them, with labels on the arrows indicating the conditions for each transition. Here's a breakdown of the states and transitions: **States:** * **State 1 (Gray, top):** * K2=2 (Legacy) * K2=3 (Legacy) * Condition: - 0.5ms, 2cores-1 cell or 1core-1 cell * **State 2 (Red, left):** * K2=4,3,2 * K2=4,3 * Condition: - 0.5ms, 2cores-1 cell or 1core-l cell * **State 3 (Blue, middle):** * K2=4,3,2 * K2=4,3 * Condition: - 0.75ms, 2cores-1 cell or 1 core-lcell * **State 4 (Orange, bottom):** * K2=4,3,2 * K2=4,3 * Condition: - 0.75ms, 2cores-1 cell or 1 core-l cell * New algorithm to mitigate RU limitations(max scheduled UEs allowed greater than 16) **Transitions:** * **From State 1 (Gray) to State 1 (Gray):** * Green arrow: * CB013895 disabled online * CB013895 enabled online * RIM mitigation activated/deactivated * **From State 1 (Gray) to State 3 (Blue):** * Purple arrow: High K2 Load * **From State 1 (Gray) to State 2 (Red):** * Purple arrow: High OLC and CB013895 is disabled * **From State 1 (Gray) to State 4 (Orange):** * Yellow arrow: - Low (K2 load and OLC) * and 2 SFNs * **From State 2 (Red) to State 3 (Blue):** * Green arrow: 2 SFN and CB013895 is disabled * **From State 3 (Blue) to State 1 (Gray):** * Red arrow: - Low (K2 load and OLC) * and 2 SFNS * **From State 3 (Blue) to State 4 (Orange):** * Purple arrow: CB013895 enabled online * **From State 3 (Blue) to State 2 (Red):** * Brown arrow: - High K2 Load or OLC * - RIM mitigation activated/deactivated * **From State 4 (Orange) to State 3 (Blue):** * Purple arrow: CB013895 disabled online * **From State 4 (Orange) to State 1 (Gray):** * Red arrow: -
