The background is white.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5920.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] the slot offset of the uplink-downlink switching period with index [image-1][image_desc]The image contains a blurry, abstract representation of the letters "RIM" with a subscript "i" below and to the left of the "R". The colors are muted and blend into each other, creating a watercolor-like effect. The overall impression is one of artistic interpretation rather than clear, legible text.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5920.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] (see 5GC_UP_ALG_12684) with respect to the starting boundary of the RIM-RS transmission period as below

1) For single TDD pattern (Pattern1 only) in FR1 TDD frame configuration

[image-2][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $\overline{T}_{\text{offset}} = 2^{\mu} t_{\text{z}}^{\text{RIM}} T_{\text{per},1}^{\text{RIM}}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5920.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]

Where, [image-3][image_desc]The image contains the text "TRIM per, 1". The text is in a light blue and orange color gradient, with a slight blur effect. The "T" in "TRIM" is larger than the other letters and is positioned to the left and slightly above the rest of the text. The word "per" is below "TRIM", and the number "1" is to the right of "per".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5920.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] is the period [ms] for TDD pattern-1, which is defined by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/rsPattern1Period.

2) For dual TDD patterns (Pattern1 and Pattern2) in FR1 TDD frame configuration

[image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: $\overline{T}_{\text{offset}} = 2^{\lfloor L_{\tau}^{\text{RIM}}/2 \rfloor} (T_{\text{per},1}^{\text{RIM}} + T_{\text{per},2}^{\text{RIM}}) + 2^{\lfloor L_{\tau}^{\text{RIM}} \pmod 2 \rfloor} T_{\text{per},1}^{\text{RIM}}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5920.005.png[/image_path][/image-4] 

where,

[image-5][image_desc]The image contains the text "TRIM per, 1". The text is in a light blue and orange color gradient, with a slight blur effect. The "T" in "TRIM" is larger than the other letters and is positioned to the left and slightly above the rest of the text. The word "per" is below "TRIM", and the number "1" is to the right of "per".[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5920.004.png[/image_path][/image-5]: the period [ms] for pattern-1, which is defined by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/sPattern1Period.

determine the starting symbol position [image-0][image_desc]The image is a blurry, abstract representation of two objects that appear to be stylized letters or symbols. The left object is taller and more upright, with vertical lines and some diagonal elements. The right object is shorter and appears to be a more rounded or blocky shape. Both objects are rendered with a gradient of colors, including shades of white, yellow, brown, blue, and purple, creating a somewhat metallic or iridescent effect. The background is mostly white, with a dark, blurred border on the right and bottom edges. The overall impression is one of digital art or a graphic design element, but the lack of clear detail makes it difficult to identify specific objects or text.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] for all repetitions of RIM-RS transmission in the slot [image-1][image_desc]The image is a blurry, close-up shot of text on a white background. The text appears to be in a sans-serif font and is colored with a gradient of blue, orange, and yellow. The visible text reads "m. s.". There is also a superscripted character above the "m" that is too blurry to identify. The right side of the image is black, suggesting it might be a cropped portion of a larger image or a screenshot.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.002.png[/image_path][/image-1] within the frame [image-2][image_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of text. The visible text appears to be "RIM" in large, capitalized letters, with some colorful chromatic aberration. Below and to the left of "RIM," there is smaller text that is even more blurry, but it seems to be "n" followed by a subscripted "r". The background is white.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] where RIM RS need to be sent as below.

[image-3][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation written in white text against a light background. The equation is: $l_0 = T_{\text{offset}}^{\text{UD,RIM}} \mod N_{\text{symb}}^{\text{slot}}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]

in slots satisfying

[image-4][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical equation. The equation is: (1024 $N_{slot}^{frame,\mu}$ + $N_{slot}^{RIM}$ + $n_{f,\mu}^{RIM}$ - ($\bar{T}_{offset}$ + $|T_{offset}^{UDRIM}$ / $N_{symb}$|)) mod $N_{slot}^{P_1}$ = 0 The equation involves variables such as $N_{slot}^{frame,\mu}$, $N_{slot}^{RIM}$, $n_{f,\mu}^{RIM}$, $\bar{T}_{offset}$, $T_{offset}^{UDRIM}$, $N_{symb}$, and $N_{slot}^{P_1}$. The equation uses mathematical operators like addition, subtraction, absolute value, division, modulo, and equality.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]

\- [image-5][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression. The expression is: $\bar{n}_r^{\text{RIM}} \in {0, 1, \dots, N_{\text{slot}}^{\text{Pt}} / (1024 N_{\text{slot}}^{\text{frame},\mu}) - 1}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.006.png[/image_path][/image-5] counts the number of times the SFN periods within the RIM-RS transmission period;

\- [image-6][image_desc]The image contains a blurry, close-up view of text. The text appears to be "n14 RDM". The colors are muted and the edges of the letters are indistinct.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.007.png[/image_path][/image-6]: is the offset of frame after the starting boundary of the SFN in which the RIM-RS is mapped

\- [image-7][image_desc]The image contains the text "N Pt" with a superscript "e" above the "t". Below the "N" and "Pt" is the word "elor". The text is rendered in a stylized font with a rainbow-like color gradient.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.008.png[/image_path][/image-7] is the total number of slots in a RIM-RS transmission period (see 5G_UP_ALG_12626_replaced([11117421](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp_g9fvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)));

The equation is: $T_{offset}^{UD,RIM} = N_{ref}^{UD,RIM} - N_{symb,ref}^{RIM,i}$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.013.png[/image_path][/image-12], where [image-13][image_desc]The image contains text that appears to be a mathematical expression or a label. The reference point is the starting boundary of the first allowed UL reception within a uplink-downlink switching perriod. [End xxxx]

gNB-DU shall determine [image-15][image_desc]The image contains a blurry, pixelated text that appears to be a snippet from a document. The visible text reads: "T UD, RIN T officer". The rest of the image is black.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.012.png[/image_path][/image-15]as below

If NRRIM_PROFILE-rsNearFarConfig = 0-“near only”

[Before xxxx][image-16][image_desc]The image contains text that reads "T UD, RIM offset". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.016.png[/image_path][/image-16][xxxx] [image-17][image_desc]The image contains text that appears to be a label or a file name. The text is blurry and difficult to read clearly. However, some parts are discernible. The text reads: N RIM;i symb,ref[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.017.png[/image_path][/image-17][End xxxx] shall be corresponding to the last 2nd DL symbol in special slot within the UL-DL switching period where RIM-RS is mapped

Else If NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/rsNearFarConfig = 1-“ near-far config1”

[Before xxxx][image-18][image_desc]The image contains text that reads "T UD, RIM offset". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.016.png[/image_path][/image-18][xxxx] [image-19][image_desc]The image contains text that appears to be a label or a file name. The text is blurry and difficult to read clearly. However, some parts are discernible. The text reads: N RIM;i symb,ref[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.017.png[/image_path][/image-19][End xxxx] shall be corresponding to the last 2nd DL symbol in special slot for the first half repetitions , and corresponding to the last 2nd DL symbol in the last DL slot before the special slot for the second half repetitions .

Else If NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/rsNearFarConfig = 2-“ near-far config2”

[Before xxxx][image-20][image_desc]The image contains text that reads "T UD, RIM offset". The text is blurry and difficult to read.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.016.png[/image_path][/image-20][xxxx] [image-21][image_desc]The image contains text that appears to be a label or a file name. The text is blurry and difficult to read clearly. However, some parts are discernible. The text reads: N RIM;i symb,ref[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5923.017.png[/image_path][/image-21][End xxxx] shall be corresponding to the last 2nd DL symbol in special slot for the first half repetitions , and corresponding to the last 2nd DL symbol in the 2nd-to-last DL slot before the special slot for the second half repetitions .

##### 3.5.16.2.0-1 frequency domain configuration for RIM RS type 1/2 transmission calculation of index of frequence resource (ID: 11117996)

11118070: frequency domain configuration for RIM RS type 1/2 transmission When actNrRim is configured to (3-"framework-0"[CB009823-A] or 4-"framework-1"[End CB009823-A]), gNB-DU shall determine the index of frequence resource, [image-0][image_desc]The image is a blurry, close-up shot of text. The text appears to be in a colorful, stylized font. The visible words are "RIM" and "ing". The colors used in the text are shades of blue, yellow, orange, and purple. The background is white.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.001.png[/image_path][/image-0] used for RIM RS transmission by following:

[image-1][image_desc]$t_{f}^{\text{RIM}} = \left(\left\lfloor \frac{n_{\text{setID}}}{N_{t}^{\text{RIM}} N_{s}^{\text{RIM}}} \right\rfloor \pmod{N_{f}^{\text{RIM}}}\right)$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.002.png[/image_path][/image-1]

i_RIM_f = floor(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/rsType1NScId / N_RIM_s / N_RIM_t) mod N_RIM_f

  * [image-2][image_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry, and abstract representation of text. The text appears to be in a stylized font with vibrant, shifting colors, giving it a somewhat ethereal or digital effect. The colors blend and overlap, making it difficult to discern individual letters clearly. The visible text seems to be "ARIM" at the top, with some unclear characters or symbols below it, possibly "ly" or a similar combination. The overall impression is of a logo or a piece of digital art where text is a prominent element but rendered in a way that prioritizes visual effect over legibility.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.003.png[/image_path][/image-2] is : the index of frequency resource,



\- if actNrRim is configured to 3-“framework-0”, which indicates which element in /NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/startRbForRs will be used as the starting RB for RIM-RS transmission.

\- if actNrRim is configured to 4-“framework-1”, which indicates which element in [Before CB009823-F]/NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/startRbForRs[CB009823-F]rimRsPrbOffset (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12403_replaced([11279177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUCrvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End CB009823-F] will be used as the starting RB for RIM-RS transmission.

  * [image-3][image_desc]The image contains the text "n_seedID" in a stylized font with a rainbow gradient effect.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.004.png[/image_path][/image-3]: setID, defined by NRRIM_PROFILE-rsSetId.

  * [image-4][image_desc]The image contains the text "Nf RIM". The text is blurry and appears to be in a blue and orange color scheme.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.005.png[/image_path][/image-4]: the number of candidate frequency resources for RIM-RS,




\- if actNrRim is configured to 3-“framework-0”, which is equal to the number of elements in NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/startRbForRs.

\- if actNrRim is configured to 4-“framework-1”, which is equal to the number of elements in [Before CB009823-F]NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/startRbForRs[CB009823-F]rimRsPrbOffset (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12403_replaced([11279177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUCrvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))[End CB009823-F].

[image-5][image_desc]The image contains a close-up of text that is blurred and has a rainbow-like chromatic aberration effect. The visible letters appear to be "N", "g", and "RIN". The text is white with colorful edges, and it is set against a white background.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.006.png[/image_path][/image-5]: is the number of sequence candidates for the current RIM-RS resource, which is given by

For RS-1,

\- if actNrRim is configured to 3-"framework-0", N_RIM_s = #(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/rsType1NScId)

[CB009823-A]- if actNrRim i configured to 4-"framework-1"[CB009823-E] and NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/actRsEnoughIndication is equal to false[End CB009823-E], N_RIM_s = #(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/rsType1NScId)[End CB009823-A]

[CB009823-E]

\- if actNrRim is configured to 4-"framework-1" and NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/actRsEnoughIndication is equal to true, N_RIM_s = #(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/rsType1NScId) / 2[End CB009823-E]

[CB009823-A]For RS-2, N_RIM_s = #(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/rsType2NScId)[End CB009823-A]

\- [image-6][image_desc]The image contains the text "N_g RIM, 1". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The letters are colored with a gradient of blue, purple, and yellow. The subscript "g" is smaller than the other letters and is positioned below the "N". The comma after "RIM" is also small and positioned below the line of text. The number "1" is slightly larger than the subscript "g" and is positioned below the line of text.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.007.png[/image_path][/image-6] for RS-1 [CB009823-E]and if EnoughIndication is disabled

\- [image-7][image_desc]The image contains the text "N RIM,1/2". The text is in a light blue and white color scheme. The letters are slightly blurred.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.008.png[/image_path][/image-7] for RS-1 and if EnoughIndication is enabled[End CB009823-E]

[CB009823-A]- [image-8][image_desc]The image contains the text "N, RIM,a" with a subscript "g" below the "N". The text is blurry and appears to be in a sans-serif font. The colors of the text are a mix of blue, yellow, and white, with a black outline.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.009.png[/image_path][/image-8] for RS-2[End CB009823-A]

[image-9][image_desc]The image contains a close-up, blurry view of text. The visible text appears to be "N" followed by a subscript "t", and then "RIN". The colors are vibrant and have a rainbow-like effect, suggesting a digital rendering or a specific lighting condition. The background is white.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.010.png[/image_path][/image-9] is given by

\- [image-10][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in square brackets. The expression is a fraction. The numerator is "N_RIM1 / N_setID". The denominator is "N_RIM1 / N_s". The entire expression is enclosed in square brackets.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.011.png[/image_path][/image-10] for RS-1 [CB009823-E]and if EnoughIndication is disabled

\- [image-11][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in square brackets. The expression is a fraction. The numerator is "2N RIM1" divided by "sedD". The denominator is "N RIMN RIM1" divided by "N_f". The expression can be written as: $$ \left[ \frac{2N_{RIM1}}{sedD} \middle/ \frac{N_{RIMN}N_{RIM1}}{N_f} \right] $$[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.012.png[/image_path][/image-11] for RS-1 and if EnoughIndication is enabled[End CB009823-E]

[CB009823-A]- [image-12][image_desc]The image contains a mathematical expression enclosed in square brackets. The expression is a fraction. The numerator is "N RIM2" with a subscript "eotid". The denominator is "N RIMN RIM2" with a subscript "f". The text in the image is: [ N^RIM2 eotid / N_f^RIMN RIM2 ][/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5930.013.png[/image_path][/image-12] for RS-2[End CB009823-A]

For RS-1,

\-- if actNrRim is configured to 3-"framework-0", N_RIM_t = ceil(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/numRsSetId / N_RIM_f / #(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/rsType1NScId)

[CB009823-A]-- if actNrRim i configured to 4-"framework-1"[CB009823-E] and NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/actRsEnoughIndication is equal to false[End CB009823-E], N_RIM_t = ceil(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/numRsSetId / N_RIM_f / #(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/rsType1NScId)[End CB009823-A]

[CB009823-E]

\-- if actNrRim is configured to 4-"framework-1" and NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/actRsEnoughIndication is equal to true, N_RIM_t = ceil(2 * NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/numRsSetId / N_RIM_f / #(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/rsType1NScId)

[End CB009823-E]

[CB009823-A]

For RS-2,

\-- N_RIM_t = ceil(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/numRsSetId / N_RIM_f / #(NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/rsType2NScId)

[End CB009823-A]

and number of PRB used for RIM RS transmission. gNB-DU shall determine the starting RB index ( k1 ) corresponding to [image-0][image_desc]The image is a close-up, blurry, and abstract representation The visible text appears to be "N RIM" on the top line and "RB" on the bottom line. [CB009823-A]When NRBTS/NRCell-actNRRim is configured to 4-"framework-1", gNB-DU shall determine PRB range in term of starting PRB index and number of PRB used for RIM RS transmission. gNB-DU shall determine the starting PRB index ( k1 ) corresponding to [image-2][image_desc]The image is a blurry, pixelated graphic that appears to be a logo or a stylized representation of text. The colors are vibrant, with shades of blue, yellow, pink, and purple. The shapes are abstract and geometric, with some elements resembling letters or figures. The overall impression is one of movement and dynamism. Due to the extreme blurriness, it is impossible to definitively identify any text or specific objects within the image.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5933.003.png[/image_path][/image-2]from the list given by [Before CB009823-F] NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/startRbForRs[CB009823-F]rimRsPrbOffset (see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12403_replaced([11279177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUCrvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) )[End CB009823-F] as for RIM-RS transmission. [CB009823-F]The starting PRB and subcarrier offset (defined by rimRsPrbOffset and rimRsSubCarrierOffset(see 5G_CP_ARC_ALG_12403_replaced([11279177](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpUCrvvcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)))) are used to determine from which frequency RIM-RS shall be transmitted.[End CB009823-F]gNB-DU shall determine [image-3][image_desc]The image contains blurry text. The visible text appears to be "N RIM" and "RB". The letters have a rainbow-like chromatic aberration effect.[/image_desc][image_path]https://storage.googleapis.com/dng_files/images/HTML_m22dng_5G_UP_Algorithms_file_5933.004.png[/image_path][/image-3] given by by NIDD parameter NRBTS/NRCELL/NRRIM_PROFILE/numRbForRs as the number of PRB used for each RIM RS frequency resource. [End CB009823-A]

[CB009823-F]If rimRsSubCarrierOffset is not equal to 0, then one additional PRB shall be used for PRB allocation when: 1)DL PRB pooling is enabled (see 5G_UP_ALG_12735_replaced([11160843](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktQ3ZfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) 2)eCPRI FD configuration (see 5G_UP_ALG_5163_replaced([11204341](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kwkDDfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) 3)UL PRB pooling is enabled (see 5G_UP_ALG_13188_replaced([11162108](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktW-D_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), 5G_UP_ALG_15475_replaced([11162234](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlE_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) 4)beam overriding due to PRB overlap with other signals (see 5G_UP_ALG_13190_replaced([11162584](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_ktXlF_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))). [End CB009823-F]

## 3.5.17 R&amp;D parameters

### 3.5.17.0-1 R&amp;D parameters rdCsiRsSlotShiftPciMod (ID: 11118180)

**3.5.17.0-1.0-1**  (ID: `11118183`)

R&D parameters Defines slot shift for CSI-RS resources in when dlMimoMode is "CL 8x4 MIMO" is true.

Value range: 0...91

Default value: 0

Unit: NA

Online modifiable: No

Step size: 1

Used in: 5G_UP_ALG_7725_replaced([11115037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpqJzvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Trigger for deletion: This R&D parameter can only be removed after it has been replaced by an NIDD parameter because SKT is using it in their network.

---

### 3.5.17.0-2 R&amp;D parameters rdCsiRsCellShiftMod (ID: 11118186)

**3.5.17.0-2.0-2**  (ID: `11118193`)

R&D parameters Defines slots interval among cells for CSI-RS resources slots allocation when dlMimoMode is "CL 8x4 MIMO" is true. Larger slots interval among all cells with larger rdCsiRsCellShiftMod configuration.

Value range: [Before CNI-112296-A]0...159 [CNI-112296-A]0...319[end CNI-112296-A]

Default value: 0

Unit: NA

Online modifiable: No

Step size: 1

Used in: 5G_UP_ALG_7725_replaced([11115037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpqJzvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Trigger for deletion: This R&D parameter can only be removed after it has been replaced by an NIDD parameter because SKT is using it in their network.

---

### 3.5.17.0-3 R&amp;D parameters rdCsiRsSlotsInterval (ID: 11118197)

**3.5.17.0-3.0-2**  (ID: `11118204`)

R&D parameters Defines the interval between adjacent CSI-RS resource sets when dlMimoMode is "CL 8x4 MIMO" is true. Smaller interval between adjacent CSI-RS resource sets with larger rdCsiRsSlotsInterval configuraiotn. The value of rdCsiRsSlotsInterval should be conifgured according to different CSI-RS resource period and make sure at least one downlink slot with 14 symbols in the interval.

Value range: [Before CNI-112296-A]0...5[CNI-112296-A]0...319[end CNI-112296-A]

Default value: 0

Unit: NA

Online modifiable: No

Step size: 1

Used in: 5G_UP_ALG_7725_replaced([11115037](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpqJzvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

### 3.5.17.0-4 R&amp;D parameters rdNumberOfUeCsirsSymbol (ID: 11118207)

**3.5.17.0-4.0-5**  (ID: `12816229`)

R&D parameters _Note: for23R3, this requirement, in comparison to the version in previous release, is tagged to AC/M priority only. The reason is that the change for CB008940-J does not need ACSCT/M._

_Future-/Next release version consider keeping the original AC/M and ACSCT/M priority._

The parameter defines number of UE specific CSI-RS for the cell in U32.

Default value: 65535, means all possible UE specific CSI-RS is available;

If actDlSrsBm is true and dlMimoMode is CL 8x4MIMO, or slowPrbPoolingTddFr1DlAllowed is true (5G_CP_ARC_ALG_4283_replaced([11278751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_TpSNc_vcEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))) or (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed is true and actTddCsirsAllocOptimization is true) or (slowPrbPoolingTddFr1Ecpri72eDlAllowed is true and actTddCsirsOpt **[CB015668-A] (note: actTddCsirsOpt is not get from NIDD but from L3 indicator)**[End CB015668-A]**_* is true), after removing slots that have cell specific CSI-RS, L2 PS shall restrict_ _UE specific CSI-RS symbols in primary bitmap (csiRsResourcesInSsSlots) and secondary bitmap (csiRsResourcesInDataSlots) to_ rdNumberOfUeCsirsSymbol for test purpose.***

If _rdNumberOfUeCsirsSymbol_ is larger than total number of CSI-RS symbols in the two lists, no restriction shall be done.**

**Note _rdNumberOfUeCsirsSymbol*is 0 means all UE specific CSI-RS symbols are set as unavailable, all UEs will be configured with cell specific CSI-RS in this case. Whether TDRA list can recognize the cell as having UE specific CSI-RS or not is decided by SW team._** *

Note: when the periodicity of UE specific CSI-RS is larger than the list lengh, here only counts the non zero bits in the two lists.

---

### 3.5.17.0-5 R&amp;D parameters rdDisableUeSpecificCsiRsPerSlotFormatBitmap (ID: 11118217)

**3.5.17.0-5.0-1**  (ID: `11118219`)

R&D parameters The parameter defines the slots that shall not be reported to C-Plane as available for UE specific CSI-RS.

Bit j, counting from LSB, set to value “1” indicates that UE specific CSI-RS is not allowed in a slot with exact j + 1 DL OFDM symbols

Range: 0x0000 .. 0x3FFF

Default : 0x0220, i.e. no UE specific CSI-RS in BI6,y and slots BI10,y

Unit: NA

Online modifiable: No

Used in: 5G_UP_ALG_7753_replaced([11117216](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp9rzfvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)), [11116693 (Identifying OFDM symbols for CSI-RS transmissions)](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp6BcvvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ?oslc_config.context=https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/gc/configuration/9)

---

### 3.5.17.0-6 R&amp;D parameters rdCsiRsInDataSlotFddSlowPrbPooling (ID: 11118222)

**3.5.17.0-6.0-2**  (ID: `12574876`)

R&D parameters [Before CNI-137616-B] Moves CSI-RS for channel acquisition to data slot for FDD cell with slow PRB pooling enabled and actRateMatchedCsiIm disabled.

Value range: 0 (FALSE), 1 (TRUE)

Default value: 1 (TRUE)

Step size: -

Unit: -

Online modifiable: No

Used in: 5G_UP_ALG_2365_replaced([11115079](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpqw2vvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

Trigger for deletion: This R&D parameter can be removed after no issues have been observed over one release. [End CNI-137616-B]

Note: R&D parameter removed by CNI-137616.

---

### 3.5.17.0-7 R&amp;D parameters rdIncreasePUSCHPrepTimeULCA (ID: 11118229)

**3.5.17.0-7.0-2**  (ID: `11118235`)

R&D parameters [Before PR681812-23R2]

The parameter will increase the K2 so there is larger UE PUSCH preparation procedure time when cell can be UL SCell so there is enough UE preparation time for UCI over PUSCH.

Value range: 0 (FALSE), 1 (TRUE)

Default value: 0 (FALSE)

Step size: -

Unit: -

Online modifiable: No

Used in: 5G_UP_ALG_2484_replaced([11116264](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2XB_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

[End PR681812-23R2]

---

### 3.5.17.0-8 R&amp;D parameters rdMuteLastSymbolInSSBSlot (ID: 11118238)

**3.5.17.0-8.0-2**  (ID: `11118245`)

R&D parameters [Before CNI-92005-23R3]This parameter enables muting of last symbol on SSB slot

Value range:

0 = No muting of DL symbol.

1 = Muting of last DL symbol only in SSB slot 0 (first SSB slot).

2 = Muting of last DL symbol in all SSB slots.

Default value: 0(No muting of DL symbol)

Step size: -

Unit: -

Online modifiable: No

Only valid for FR1 TDD. Shall be set to 0 for FR2 and FDD.

Used in: 5G_UP_ALG_2330_replaced([11114751](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kpnth_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

[End CNI-92005-23R3]

Note: rdMuteLastSymbolInSSBSlot was replaced by NRCELL.muteLastSymbolInSSBSlot.

Value range: 10~20

Default value: 20

Unit: NA

Online modifiable: No

### 3.5.17.0-10 R&amp;D parameters rdFixBISlotTimings (ID: 11118254)

**3.5.17.0-10.0-1**  (ID: `11118258`)

R&D parameters Defines whether to fixed to 0.75ms timing pattern

Value range: 0 (dynamic timing pattern in 0.5ms and 0.75ms), 1 (fixed to 0.75ms timing pattern)

Default value: 0

Unit: NA

Online modifiable: No

Step Size: 1

Specified in 5G_UP_ALG_29578_replaced([11116328](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp2-G_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)).

### 3.5.17.0-11 R&amp;D parameters rdAvgMaxSizeDlCs1BiSlotFilterConst (ID: 11118262)

**3.5.17.0-11.0-1**  (ID: `11118265`)

R&D parameters The R&D parameter defines the exponential filter for size of max CSI1 list for DL in the Bi-directional slots for dynamic switching timing pattern from 0.5ms to 0.75ms. The less the easier to trigger switch.

Value range: 1, 2, ..., 1000

Default value: 174

Step size: 1

Online modifiable: No

Specified in: 5G_UP_ALG_30274_replaced([11116355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp3lI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

---

**3.5.17.0-12.0-1**  (ID: `11118270`)

R&D parameters The R&D parameter defines the threshold of IIR filtered average of size of max CSI1 list for DL in the Bi-directional slots.

Value range: [1.01, ..., 50]

Default value: 1.1

Step size: 0.01

Online modifiable: No

Specified in: 5G_UP_ALG_30274_replaced([11116355](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp3lI_vSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ))

R&D parameters The R&D parameter defines max CS1 list size for DL threshold for switching from 0.75ms to 0.5m. The less the easier to trigger switch.

Value range: [2, ..., 50]

Default value: 49

Step size: 1

Online modifiable: No

### 3.5.17.0-15 R&amp;D parameters rdReallocateCsiRsSlotOffset (ID: 11118286)

**3.5.17.0-15.0-1**  (ID: `11118290`)

R&D parameters Short description: This parameter activates the cell specific CSI-RS slot offset re-allocation function and forces the CSI-RS re-allocated to the TRS slot when BB pooling is enabled for TDD FR1.

Value range: true, false  
Default value: false  
Step size: -  
Unit: -  
Online modifiable: No  
Type: Control  
Performance Impact: None  
Used in: 5G_UP_ALG_11339_replaced([11116731](https://dn-prod.ext.net.nokia.com/rm/resources/BI_kp6odPvSEe-AqvopbP1qhQ)) Korean customer KT.

---

### 3.5.17.0-16 R&amp;D parameters rdCsiRsSlotOffsetForcedValueI (ID: 11118293)

**3.5.17.0-16.0-1**  (ID: `11118295`)

R&D parameters Short description: This parameter defines the cell specific CSI-RS re-allocated slot offset based on the TRS slot offset i if rdReallocateCsiRsSlotOffset is enabled.

Value range: 0 … 1000  
Default value: 20  
Step size: 1  
Unit: slot  
Online modifiable: No  
Type: Control  
Performance Impact: None  
Used in: Korean customer KT.

---

### 3.5.17.0-17 R&amp;D parameters rdCsiRsSlotOffsetForcedValueJ (ID: 11118299)

**3.5.17.0-17.0-1**  (ID: `11118302`)

R&D parameters Short description: This parameter defines the cell specific CSI-RS re-allocated slot offset based on the TRS slot offset j and TRS periodicity if rdReallocateCsiRsSlotOffset is enabled.

Value range: 0 … 1000  
Default value: 180  
Step size: 1  
Unit: slot  
Online modifiable: No  
Type: Control  
Performance Impact: None  
Used in: Korean customer KT
